Indicates whether this SLA contract includes financial penalty clauses for breach events (True) or is informational only (False).

description: Fraud risk classification for this carrier based on historical NRTRDE anomalies, grey route incidents, and GSMA FASG (Fraud and Security Group) advisories. Drives real-time fraud monitoring thresholds and NRTRDE file frequency.
reference: GSMA PRD FS.11 (FASG); GSMA PRD TD.32; ISO/IEC 27001

description: Type of interconnect arrangement with this carrier: direct (direct circuit interconnect), transit (traffic routed via a third-party transit carrier), peering (IP or voice peering arrangement), wholesale (wholesale traffic purchase/sale), none (no active interconnect).
reference: FCC Part 69; ITU-T D.150; TM Forum SID

description: IOT tariff group classification assigned to this carrier, used to look up applicable Inter-Operator Tariff (IOT) rates for roaming settlement. Aligns with GSMA IOT rate tables and bilateral agreement terms. Drives rating of inbound and outbound roaming CDRs.
reference: GSMA PRD AA.13 (IOT); GSMA PRD BA.12; TM Forum SID

business_glossary_term: GSMA IR.21 Profile Last Updated Date
description: Date on which the carrier's GSMA IR.21 roaming information profile was last updated in the GSMA RAEX (Roaming Agreement Exchange) system. Stale IR.21 data can cause roaming activation failures.

business_glossary_term: GSMA IR.21 Roaming Information Profile Version
description: Version identifier of the carrier's GSMA IR.21 roaming information document on file. IR.21 contains network configuration data required for roaming service activation including IMSI ranges, supported services, and signalling parameters.
Timestamp of the most recent update to this carrier record in the interconnect carrier master registry. Used for change tracking, data freshness monitoring, and audit compliance.

Date of the most recently received TAP (Transferred Account Procedure) file from this carrier. Used to monitor TAP file exchange health and detect stale or missing files that may indicate a processing issue or carrier-side problem.
reference: GSMA PRD TD.57; GSMA PRD BA.12

description: Date of the most recently sent TAP file to this carrier. Used to monitor outbound TAP file exchange health and ensure timely delivery within GSMA-mandated submission windows.
reference: GSMA PRD TD.57; GSMA PRD BA.12

description: Indicates whether LTE (4G) data roaming is active with this carrier. Distinct from VoLTE; covers data-only LTE roaming sessions rated and settled via TAP3 or DIAMETER-based online charging.
reference: GSMA PRD IR.88; 3GPP TS 23.401; GSMA PRD AA.13

description: Three-digit ITU-assigned Mobile Country Code (MCC) identifying the country of the carrier's home network. Combined with MNC to form the full PLMN identifier used in roaming and signalling.
reference: ITU-T E.212; 3GPP TS 23.003; GSMA PRD SE.13

description: Two- or three-digit ITU-assigned Mobile Network Code (MNC) identifying the carrier's specific network within its country. Combined with MCC to form the PLMN identifier used in HLR/HSS, roaming agreements, and CDR processing.
reference: ITU-T E.212; 3GPP TS 23.003; GSMA PRD SE.13

description: Primary email address for the carrier's Network Operations Center (NOC) used for fault escalation, outage notifications, and interconnect incident management. Operational contact for network-layer issues.
reference: TM Forum eTOM; ISO/IEC 27001; GSMA PRD IR.21

description: 24/7 phone number for the carrier's Network Operations Center (NOC) in E.164 international format. Used for real-time fault escalation, SLA breach notification, and interconnect outage coordination.
reference: ITU-T E.164; TM Forum eTOM; GSMA PRD IR.21

business_glossary_term: Near Real-Time Roaming Data Exchange (NRTRDE) Enabled Flag
description: Indicates whether this carrier participates in NRTRDE (Near Real-Time Roaming Data Exchange) for near-real-time roaming fraud detection and usage monitoring. When true, NRTRDE files must be exchanged within the GSMA-mandated 4-hour window.
reference: GSMA PRD TD.32 (NRTRDE); GSMA PRD AA.13

description: Date on which the carrier was formally onboarded into Telecommunication's interconnect systems including OSS/BSS provisioning, TAP file pipeline configuration, and clearinghouse registration. Distinct from relationship_start_date which may precede system activation.
reference: TM Forum eTOM; Oracle Communications OSM; Netcracker OSS/BSS

description: Number of days from invoice date within which payment must be received from or made to this carrier. Standard values are 30, 45, or 60 days. Used by Amdocs Revenue Management for collections and dunning scheduling.

Preferred routing method for interconnect traffic to/from this carrier: direct (direct circuit), via_transit (through a transit carrier), via_iplc (International Private Leased Circuit), via_mpls (MPLS backbone), via_sdwan (SD-WAN overlay). Used by NOC for traffic engineering.
reference: TM Forum SID; ITU-T G.805; GSMA PRD IR.21

description: Broad geographic region grouping for the carrier (e.g., APAC, EMEA, AMER, LATAM). Used for regional traffic analysis, IOT tariff banding, and wholesale reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: APAC|EMEA|AMER|LATAM|MEA|CEUR — promote to reference product]
reference: TM Forum SID; GSMA PRD BA.12

description: Date on which the commercial and operational relationship with this carrier was or is scheduled to be terminated. Null for active ongoing relationships. Used for agreement expiry tracking and traffic wind-down planning.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922; GSMA PRD BA.12

description: Date on which the commercial and operational relationship with this carrier was formally established. Marks the beginning of the carrier's lifecycle in the interconnect registry and the effective date of the first agreement.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922; GSMA PRD BA.12

description: Type of roaming agreement in place with this carrier: bilateral (direct two-way roaming), multilateral (via GSMA or regional body), MVNO_host (carrier hosts an MVNO), transit_only (transit traffic only, no retail roaming), none (no roaming, interconnect only).
reference: GSMA PRD AA.13; TM Forum SID; ITU-T D.150

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code used for financial settlement with this carrier (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Determines the currency in which TAP invoices, IOT charges, and interconnect invoices are denominated and paid.
reference: ISO 4217; GSMA PRD BA.12; ITU-T D.150

description: Frequency of financial settlement with this carrier for interconnect and roaming charges. Determines the billing and reconciliation schedule in Amdocs Revenue Management and the clearinghouse.
reference: GSMA PRD BA.12; ITU-T D.150; TM Forum eTOM

description: Abbreviated or commonly used trading name of the carrier, used in operational reports, dashboards, and TAP file processing where space is constrained.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922; GSMA PRD TD.57

description: SLA tier assigned to this carrier relationship, determining priority of fault resolution, MTTR targets, and escalation paths. Platinum and gold tiers receive 24/7 NOC support with sub-4-hour MTTR commitments.
reference: TM Forum SID; TM Forum eTOM; GSMA PRD BA.12

description: Indicates whether SMS (Short Message Service) interconnect is active with this carrier. Drives SMS CDR routing, A2P/P2P SMS settlement, and grey route detection.
reference: TM Forum SID; 3GPP TS 23.040; GSMA PRD IR.21

description: Code identifying the operational system of record from which this carrier record was sourced: NETCRACKER (Netcracker OSS/BSS Suite), AMDOCS (Amdocs Revenue Management), ORACLE_OSM (Oracle Communications OSM), MANUAL (manually entered). Used for data lineage and reconciliation.

Four-digit Service Provider Identifier (SPID) assigned by the North American Numbering Plan Administrator (NANPA) or local number portability administrator. Used for MNP (Mobile Number Portability) routing and LNP database queries.

business_glossary_term: Transferred Account Data Interchange Group (TADIG) Code
description: Five-character GSMA-assigned TADIG code uniquely identifying the carrier for TAP file exchange and inter-operator roaming settlement. Mandatory for all MNO and MVNO roaming partners. Used as the primary key in TAP file headers and NRTRDE records.
reference: GSMA PRD TD.57; GSMA PRD BA.12; TM Forum SID

description: TAP (Transferred Account Procedure) file format version supported by this carrier for roaming CDR exchange. Determines the file structure and encoding used when generating or receiving TAP files. Critical for TAP file processing pipeline configuration.
reference: GSMA PRD TD.57 (TAP3); GSMA PRD BA.12

description: Indicates whether voice traffic (PSTN/VoIP/VoLTE) interconnect is active with this carrier. Drives CDR routing, rating, and settlement processing for voice traffic.
reference: TM Forum SID; ITU-T E.164; 3GPP TS 23.228

business_glossary_term: Voice over LTE (VoLTE) Roaming Enabled Flag
description: Indicates whether VoLTE (Voice over LTE) roaming via IMS (IP Multimedia Subsystem) is active with this carrier. Requires bilateral IMS interconnect configuration and specific IR.92/IR.94 profile exchange.
reference: GSMA PRD IR.92; GSMA PRD IR.94; 3GPP TS 24.229

description: Primary key for carrier_agreement

Foreign key linking to enterprise.corporate_account. Business justification: Wholesale and MVNO agreements are commercial contracts with enterprise customers. Business need: tracking which enterprise account holds the roaming/interconnect agreement for billing, SLA management,
Reference to the domestic or international operator that is the counterparty to this agreement. Links to the carrier/partner master record in the interconnect domain.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922; GSMA PRD IR.21

description: Reference to the Inter-Operator Tariff (IOT) rate table applicable to this agreement. The IOT table defines per-unit wholesale rates for voice (per minute), SMS (per message), and data (per MB/GB) exchanged between operators.
reference: GSMA PRD AA.13; TM Forum SID GB922; ITU-T D.150

description: Foreign key linking to partner.mvno_profile. Business justification: MVNO wholesale access is governed by a carrier agreement between the host MNO and the MVNO. Linking carrier_agreement to mvno_profile enables MVNO wholesale agreement tracking, capacity management, an

Foreign key linking to partner.partner. Business justification: Roaming agreements with MVNO/reseller partners (not traditional carriers) require partner_id link. Business process: MVNO roaming agreement lifecycle management, partner contract tracking, consolidate

Foreign key linking to network.qos_profile. Business justification: Roaming and interconnect agreements specify QoS requirements (latency, jitter, packet loss) that must be enforced by network QoS profiles. Required for SLA compliance verification and service assuranc

Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: International carrier interconnect and roaming agreements must be notified to or filed with national regulators in many jurisdictions. Compliance officers track the specific regulatory filing record a

Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Roaming/interconnect agreements must comply with specific regulatory obligations (FCC Part 64, international settlement policies, GDPR). Tracks which obligation each agreement satisfies for audit trai

Foreign key linking to partner.roaming_agreement. Business justification: A carrier_agreement of type roaming references a specific partner roaming_agreement for operational parameters (TAP exchange, NRTRDE, steering policy). Linking enables cross-domain agreement lifecycle

Foreign key linking to assurance.sla_contract. Business justification: Carrier agreements have formal SLA performance obligations (TAP transmission windows, settlement cycles). Linking to assurance.sla_contract enables SLA breach monitoring and penalty enforcement agains
Reference to the Service Level Agreement (SLA) record governing quality of service commitments under this carrier agreement, including network availability, latency, and fault resolution targets.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922; ETSI TS 102 250; ITU-T G.1000

description: Descriptive commercial name of the carrier agreement (e.g., 'Roaming IOT Agreement – Carrier X 2024'). Used for display in BSS portals and settlement reports.

Externally-known, human-readable reference number assigned to the bilateral or multilateral carrier agreement. Used in correspondence, TAP file headers, and inter-operator settlement documents. Maps to the contract reference in Amdocs Revenue Management and Netcracker OSS/BSS.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922; GSMA PRD IR.21

description: Current lifecycle state of the carrier agreement. DRAFT = under negotiation; ACTIVE = commercially live and binding; SUSPENDED = temporarily halted by mutual or unilateral action; TERMINATED = permanently ended; EXPIRED = reached end date without renewal; PENDING_APPROVAL = awaiting regulatory or internal sign-off.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922; TM Forum eTOM Process Framework

description: Classification of the carrier agreement by commercial relationship type. IOT = Inter-Operator Tariff roaming; CAMEL = Customised Applications for Mobile networks Enhanced Logic; MVNO_HOSTING = Mobile Virtual Network Operator (MVNO) hosting arrangement; NATIONAL_ROAMING = domestic network sharing; WHOLESALE_BROADBAND = wholesale broadband resale; CAPACITY_RESALE = bandwidth/capacity resale; TRANSIT = transit traffic routing; PEERING = direct traffic exchange. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: IOT|CAMEL|MVNO_HOSTING|NATIONAL_ROAMING|WHOLESALE_BROADBAND|CAPACITY_RESALE|TRANSIT|PEERING — promote to reference product]
reference: TM Forum SID GB922; GSMA PRD AA.13; 3GPP TS 22.101

description: Version number of the agreement document (e.g., '1.0', '2.3'). Incremented when amendments are executed. Enables tracking of agreement evolution and ensures the correct rate tables are applied for each version period.
Total number of formal amendments executed against this agreement since its original signing. Provides a quick indicator of agreement complexity and negotiation history.
Indicates whether the agreement automatically renews upon reaching its effective_until_date without requiring a new signed instrument. True = auto-renews; False = requires explicit renewal action.

Comma-separated list of telecommunication services covered under this agreement (e.g., VOICE, SMS, DATA, VOLTE, VONR, MMS, CAMEL_GPRS). Governs which service types are eligible for IOT settlement and TAP file exchange. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: VOICE|SMS|DATA|VOLTE|VONR|MMS|CAMEL_GPRS|USSD — promote to reference product]
reference: 3GPP TS 22.101; GSMA PRD IR.21; TM Forum SID GB922

description: Timestamp when this carrier agreement record was first created in the data platform. Follows ISO 8601 format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Used for data lineage and audit trail.
reference: ISO 8601; ISO/IEC 27001; TM Forum SID GB922

description: Indicates whether a data protection addendum (DPA) is attached to this agreement, governing the processing of subscriber personal data exchanged between operators in compliance with GDPR or equivalent regulations.
reference: GDPR Article 28; ISO/IEC 27001; BEREC Guidelines on Roaming

description: Percentage discount applied to standard IOT rates when discount_type is PERCENTAGE or VOLUME_TIERED. Expressed as a decimal percentage (e.g., 15.00 = 15%). Null when discount_type is FLAT_RATE or NONE.
reference: GSMA PRD AA.13; TM Forum SID GB922

description: Type of discount structure applied to IOT rates under this agreement. FLAT_RATE = fixed per-unit reduction; VOLUME_TIERED = discount scales with traffic volume; PERCENTAGE = percentage reduction off standard IOT; NEGOTIATED_RATE = bespoke bilaterally agreed rate; NONE = standard IOT applies.
reference: GSMA PRD AA.13; TM Forum SID GB922

description: Contractually agreed mechanism for resolving financial or operational disputes arising under this agreement. GSMA_MEDIATION refers to the GSMA Billing and Accounting Dispute Resolution process.
reference: GSMA PRD BA.12; TM Forum SID GB922

description: Calendar date on which the carrier agreement becomes commercially binding and operationally active. Governs the start of IOT tariff applicability, TAP file exchange obligations, and NRTRDE reporting requirements.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922; GSMA PRD AA.13

description: Calendar date on which the carrier agreement ceases to be binding. Nullable for open-ended agreements. When populated, triggers renewal workflow and settlement true-up processing in Amdocs Revenue Management.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922; GSMA PRD AA.13

description: Indicates whether this agreement includes specific fraud prevention obligations such as NRTRDE, SIM box detection cooperation, or GSMA Fraud Information Gathering System (FIGS) participation.
reference: GSMA PRD FS.11; GSMA PRD IR.82; ITU-T E.408

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the jurisdiction whose laws govern this agreement (e.g., GBR for English law, USA for US law). Relevant for regulatory compliance and dispute resolution.
reference: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3; TM Forum SID GB922

description: External reference number or document identifier for the physical signed agreement document, as stored in the document management system or SAP S/4HANA contract repository. Enables traceability to the legal instrument.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922; SAP S/4HANA Contract Management

description: Date on which the agreement was last formally reviewed by the commercial team for rate competitiveness, volume performance, and compliance. Supports the agreement lifecycle management process.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922; GSMA PRD AA.13

description: Minimum traffic volume committed by one or both parties under this agreement, expressed in the unit defined by min_volume_unit (e.g., minutes, MB, messages). Triggers true-up billing if actual volume falls below this threshold.
reference: GSMA PRD AA.13; TM Forum SID GB922

description: Unit of measure for the minimum volume commitment (min_volume_commitment_units). MINUTES = voice traffic; MEGABYTES/GIGABYTES = data traffic; MESSAGES = SMS/MMS; ERLANGS = traffic intensity for capacity agreements.

Radio access network generation(s) covered by this agreement. 2G = GSM/GPRS/EDGE; 3G = UMTS/HSPA; 4G_LTE = Long-Term Evolution (LTE); 5G_NR = 5G New Radio (NR); ALL = all generations. Determines applicable IOT tariff tables and NRTRDE reporting scope.
reference: 3GPP TS 22.101; GSMA PRD IR.21; ETSI TS 122 101

description: Scheduled date for the next formal review of this agreement. Triggers workflow notifications to the commercial owner and finance team in advance of the review.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922; GSMA PRD AA.13

description: Number of calendar days of advance written notice required by either party to terminate the agreement. Governs the termination workflow in contract lifecycle management.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922; GSMA PRD AA.13

business_glossary_term: Near Real-Time Roaming Data Exchange (NRTRDE) Flag
description: Indicates whether this agreement requires Near Real-Time Roaming Data Exchange (NRTRDE) reporting per GSMA PRD FS.11. NRTRDE enables fraud detection by providing near-real-time CDR visibility to the home operator.
reference: GSMA PRD FS.11; GSMA PRD IR.21

business_glossary_term: Near Real-Time Roaming Data Exchange (NRTRDE) Transmission Window (Hours)
description: Maximum number of hours within which NRTRDE files must be transmitted to the home operator after the roaming CDR is generated. Standard GSMA requirement is 4 hours; some bilateral agreements specify tighter windows.
Duration in months of each automatic renewal cycle when auto_renewal_flag is True. Null when auto-renewal is not applicable.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922; GSMA PRD AA.13

description: Percentage of gross revenue shared with the counterparty operator under MVNO hosting, content, or wholesale broadband agreements. Expressed as a decimal percentage (e.g., 20.00 = 20%). Null for pure IOT/TAP agreements.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922; GSMA PRD AA.13

description: Direction of roaming traffic covered by this agreement. INBOUND = foreign subscribers roaming on Telecommunication's network; OUTBOUND = Telecommunication's subscribers roaming on the counterparty's network; BILATERAL = both directions.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code used for financial settlement under this agreement (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Governs currency conversion in Amdocs Revenue Management inter-operator billing.
reference: ISO 4217; GSMA PRD AA.13; ITU-T D.150

description: Frequency at which financial settlement is performed between the parties under this agreement. Governs the billing cycle in Amdocs Revenue Management inter-operator invoicing.
reference: GSMA PRD AA.13; TM Forum SID GB922; ITU-T D.150

description: Calendar date on which all parties executed the agreement. May precede the effective_from_date during a transition or notice period. Used for legal and audit trail purposes.

Code identifying the operational system of record from which this agreement record was sourced. AMDOCS = Amdocs Revenue Management; NETCRACKER = Netcracker OSS/BSS; SALESFORCE = Salesforce CRM; SAP = SAP S/4HANA; MANUAL = manually entered.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922; TM Forum eTOM

business_glossary_term: Transferred Account Procedure (TAP) File Exchange Flag
description: Indicates whether this agreement mandates Transferred Account Procedure (TAP) file exchange for roaming CDR settlement. True = TAP files must be exchanged per GSMA PRD TD.57 specifications.
reference: GSMA PRD TD.57; GSMA PRD BA.12

description: Version of the GSMA Transferred Account Procedure (TAP) standard used for CDR file exchange under this agreement. Governs file format and field mapping in the TAP processing pipeline.
reference: GSMA PRD TD.57; GSMA PRD BA.12

description: Actual calendar date on which the agreement was terminated, if applicable. Distinct from effective_until_date which is the planned end; this captures early or unilateral termination events.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922; GSMA PRD AA.13

description: Frequency at which minimum volume commitment true-up calculations are performed. If actual traffic falls below min_volume_commitment_units within the period, a shortfall charge is raised in Amdocs Revenue Management.
reference: GSMA PRD AA.13; TM Forum SID GB922

description: Timestamp when this carrier agreement record was last modified in the data platform. Follows ISO 8601 format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Used for change tracking and incremental ETL processing.
reference: ISO 8601; ISO/IEC 27001; TM Forum SID GB922

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the peering arrangement record in the interconnect domain. Primary key for this entity. Role: MASTER_AGREEMENT.

Foreign key linking to interconnect.carrier_agreement. Business justification: peering_arrangement currently only references carrier via peer_carrier_id but has no FK to the governing carrier_agreement. IP peering and voice interconnect arrangements are commercially governed by

Foreign key linking to inventory.fiber_cable. Business justification: Peering sessions at IXPs are physically supported by specific fiber cable runs. Network infrastructure teams need to track which fiber_cable assets carry each peering arrangement for maintenance coord

Foreign key linking to inventory.network_equipment. Business justification: BGP peering sessions run on specific physical routers. Network engineers need to know which inventory.network_equipment asset hosts each peering arrangement for firmware upgrade scheduling, maintenanc

Foreign key linking to partner.agreement. Business justification: IP peering arrangements are governed by commercial partner agreements covering liability, dispute resolution, and payment terms. Linking enables peering contract lifecycle management and ensures peeri

Foreign key linking to interconnect.interconnect_carrier. Business justification: Peering arrangement peer is currently denormalized via peer_carrier_name. Normalizing to interconnect_carrier provides authoritative carrier master reference with full carrier attributes (TADIG, MCC/M

Foreign key linking to interconnect.rate. Business justification: peering_arrangement stores settlement_rate and settlement_rate_unit as inline decimal/string fields, duplicating data that belongs in the rate table. Adding rate_id as a FK to the rate schedule normal

Foreign key linking to assurance.sla_contract. Business justification: Peering arrangements have formal SLA commitments (availability, latency, packet loss targets). The existing sla_reference_code is a denormalized text reference; a proper FK to assurance.sla_contract e

Foreign key linking to partner.sla_definition. Business justification: Peering arrangements have formal SLA targets (availability_target_pct, latency_target_ms, packet_loss_target_pct) defined in SLA definitions. Linking formalizes the SLA governance for peering and enab

Foreign key linking to network.topology. Business justification: IP peering sessions map to specific topology entries representing inter-carrier logical links. NOC teams correlate peering arrangement performance (BGP session health, traffic ratios) against topology
Externally-known, human-readable business identifier for the peering arrangement (e.g., PEER-2024-NYC-001). Used in operational communications, NOC dashboards, and inter-carrier correspondence.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922; GSMA PRD IR.34

description: Descriptive human-readable name for the peering arrangement (e.g., 'Equinix NY4 Private Peering with AS64512'). Used in reporting and NOC tooling.
Free-text operational notes capturing special conditions, negotiation history, technical exceptions, or contextual information about this peering arrangement not captured in structured fields.

Current lifecycle state of the peering arrangement. draft = under negotiation; pending_approval = awaiting internal or regulatory sign-off; active = live and carrying traffic; suspended = temporarily halted; terminated = permanently ended; expired = passed effective end date without renewal.
reference: TM Forum eTOM; TM Forum SID GB922

description: Indicates whether this peering arrangement automatically renews upon expiry without requiring explicit renegotiation. True = auto-renews; False = requires manual renewal action before expiry_date.
Agreed minimum uptime percentage for the peering session as specified in the SLA (e.g., 99.9900 = 99.99%). Used for SLA compliance reporting and penalty calculation.
reference: ITU-T Y.1541; TM Forum SID GB922; ETSI

description: Type of BGP session established for this peering arrangement. ebgp = External BGP (standard inter-AS peering); ibgp = Internal BGP (used in route-reflector scenarios); ebgp_multihop = eBGP session across multiple IP hops.
Minimum guaranteed bandwidth in megabits per second (Mbps) committed under this peering arrangement. Used for capacity planning, SLA enforcement, and billing in paid peering scenarios.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922; GSMA PRD IR.34; ITU-T Y.1541

description: Timestamp when this peering arrangement record was first created in the data platform. Audit trail field supporting data governance and lineage requirements.
reference: ISO/IEC 27001; TM Forum SID GB922; GDPR Article 5

description: Calendar date on which the peering arrangement becomes operationally binding and traffic exchange may commence. Aligns with BGP session activation date.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922; GSMA PRD IR.34

description: Calendar date on which the peering arrangement is scheduled to expire. Null for open-ended arrangements. Used for renewal alerting and SLA compliance tracking.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922; GSMA PRD IR.34

description: IP protocol version(s) supported by this peering session. ipv4 = IPv4 only; ipv6 = IPv6 only; dual_stack = both IPv4 and IPv6 sessions active. Drives network engineering and capacity planning.

Name of the Internet Exchange Point (IXP) where public peering occurs (e.g., Equinix IX, DE-CIX, AMS-IX, LINX). Null for private PNI arrangements. Used for peering point inventory and cost allocation.
reference: GSMA PRD IR.34; Euro-IX; TM Forum SID GB922

description: Date on which this peering arrangement was last formally reviewed by network operations or commercial teams for traffic balance, SLA compliance, and commercial terms adequacy.
reference: TM Forum eTOM; TM Forum SID GB922

description: Maximum agreed one-way network latency in milliseconds (ms) for traffic traversing this peering arrangement as defined in the SLA. Used for QoS monitoring and SLA breach detection.
reference: ITU-T Y.1541; ETSI EN 319 401; TM Forum SID GB922

description: Maximum allowable bandwidth in megabits per second (Mbps) for this peering arrangement before traffic shaping or penalties apply. Defines the upper capacity envelope for the interconnect port.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922; ITU-T Y.1541

description: Indicates whether MD5 TCP authentication is enabled on the BGP session for this peering arrangement to prevent session hijacking. True = MD5 authentication active; False = no MD5 authentication.
Scheduled date for the next formal review of this peering arrangement. Used to drive review workflow scheduling in NOC and commercial operations.
reference: TM Forum eTOM; TM Forum SID GB922

description: Email address of the peer carrier's Network Operations Center (NOC) for operational escalations, fault notifications, and maintenance coordination. Classified confidential as organizational contact data.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922; GSMA PRD IR.34

description: Phone number of the peer carrier's Network Operations Center (NOC) for 24/7 operational escalations and incident coordination. Classified confidential as organizational contact data.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922; GSMA PRD IR.34

description: BGP Autonomous System Number (ASN) of this carrier's routing domain used in this peering session. Required when the carrier operates multiple ASNs across different network segments.
reference: IETF RFC 4271; IETF RFC 6793; ARIN; RIPE NCC

business_glossary_term: Our Border Gateway Protocol (BGP) IP Address
description: IP address of this carrier's BGP router used to establish the peering session with the counterparty. May be IPv4 or IPv6. Classified confidential as it reveals internal network topology.
Maximum agreed packet loss percentage for traffic on this peering arrangement as defined in the SLA (e.g., 0.0100 = 0.01%). Used for QoS (Quality of Service) performance monitoring.
reference: ITU-T Y.1541; ETSI EN 319 401; TM Forum SID GB922

description: BGP Autonomous System Number (ASN) of the peering counterparty as assigned by a Regional Internet Registry (ARIN, RIPE, APNIC). Uniquely identifies the peer's routing domain. Range: 1–4294967295 (32-bit ASN per RFC 6793).
reference: IETF RFC 4271; IETF RFC 6793; ARIN; RIPE NCC

business_glossary_term: Peer Border Gateway Protocol (BGP) IP Address
description: IP address of the counterparty's BGP router used to establish the peering session. May be IPv4 or IPv6. Classified confidential as it reveals network topology details.

Classification of the peering arrangement by commercial and technical model. public_ixp = peering via a public Internet Exchange Point; private_pni = Private Network Interconnect (dedicated cross-connect); settlement_free = no financial settlement between parties; paid = one or both parties pay for traffic exchange; partial = selective prefix exchange rather than full table.
reference: GSMA PRD IR.34; IETF RFC 7948; TM Forum SID GB922

description: Unique identifier of the physical or logical interconnect port used for this peering arrangement (e.g., switch/port designation from the IXP or PNI cross-connect). Links to network inventory management.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922; Netcracker OSS/BSS Resource Inventory

description: Physical port speed in gigabits per second (Gbps) of the interconnect port used for this peering arrangement (e.g., 1.000, 10.000, 100.000). Used for capacity planning and utilization reporting.
reference: IEEE 802.3; TM Forum SID GB922; ITU-T G.709

description: Maximum number of BGP route prefixes this carrier will accept from the peer. Exceeding this limit triggers a BGP session reset or route filtering. Critical for route table stability and security.

Allowable percentage deviation from the agreed traffic ratio before the arrangement is considered out of balance and subject to review or financial settlement. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 20.00 = ±20%).
reference: GSMA PRD IR.34; TM Forum SID GB922

description: Number of calendar days before expiry_date that a renewal notice must be issued to the peer carrier. Used to trigger automated renewal workflow alerts in the NOC and commercial teams.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922; GSMA PRD IR.34

business_glossary_term: Resource Public Key Infrastructure (RPKI) Validation Enabled
description: Indicates whether Resource Public Key Infrastructure (RPKI) route origin validation is enforced on BGP announcements received from this peer. True = RPKI validation active; False = not enforced. Supports FCC routing security mandates.
reference: IETF RFC 6811; FCC BGP Security; NIST SP 800-189

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code used for financial settlement in paid peering arrangements (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Null for settlement-free arrangements.
reference: ISO 4217; TM Forum SID GB922; GSMA PRD IR.34

description: Identifier of the originating operational system record for this peering arrangement (e.g., Netcracker OSS/BSS resource inventory record ID or Nokia NetAct configuration management object ID). Supports data lineage and reconciliation.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922; Netcracker OSS/BSS Suite

description: Minimum number of calendar days' written notice required to terminate this peering arrangement. Contractual obligation that governs decommissioning timelines.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922; GSMA PRD IR.34

description: Indicates whether traffic flows in both directions (bilateral), only from this carrier to the peer (unilateral), or whether the peer provides transit routing (transit). Drives settlement and capacity planning.
reference: IETF RFC 7948; TM Forum SID GB922

description: Agreed inbound traffic ratio component used to calculate the traffic balance ratio (e.g., 1.0 in a 1:1 ratio). Settlement-free peering typically requires ratios within a defined tolerance band (e.g., 1:2 or 2:1).
reference: GSMA PRD IR.34; TM Forum SID GB922

description: Agreed outbound traffic ratio component used to calculate the traffic balance ratio (e.g., 2.0 in a 1:2 ratio). Imbalance beyond the agreed threshold may trigger renegotiation or conversion to paid peering.
reference: GSMA PRD IR.34; TM Forum SID GB922

description: Timestamp when this peering arrangement record was last modified in the data platform. Used for change tracking, incremental data loads, and audit compliance.
reference: ISO/IEC 27001; TM Forum SID GB922; GDPR Article 5

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the Point of Interconnect (POI) master record. Primary key for the interconnect_poi entity in the Silver Layer lakehouse.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922; Netcracker OSS/BSS Resource Inventory

description: Reference to the carrier interconnect or roaming agreement governing the commercial and technical terms for this POI. Links to the interconnect.carrier_agreement SSOT.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922; GSMA PRD IR.21

description: Reference to the carrier or operator whose network connects at this POI. Links to the carrier/partner master record for the interconnecting operator.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922; GSMA PRD IR.21

description: Reference to the Inter-Operator Tariff (IOT) table applicable for traffic exchanged at this POI. IOT rates govern the financial settlement between operators for roaming and interconnect traffic.
reference: GSMA PRD TD.32 (IOT); TM Forum SID GB922; Amdocs Revenue Management Rating

description: Foreign key linking to workforce.depot. Business justification: Telecom operations assign each Point of Interconnect to a responsible depot for maintenance dispatch. The depot-to-POI geographic assignment drives which technician crews are dispatched for POI mainte

Identifier of the corresponding network element in the Network Management System (NMS) — Nokia NetAct or Ericsson Network Manager — that monitors this POI. Enables correlation between the business POI record and real-time network telemetry.
reference: Nokia NetAct Configuration Management; Ericsson Network Manager; TM Forum eTOM GB921

description: Foreign key linking to interconnect.peering_arrangement. Business justification: Point of Interconnect (POI) represents physical/logical handoff points that may be associated with specific peering arrangements. Adding FK to peering_arrangement links infrastructure to commercial ag
Self-referencing identifier of the backup or redundant POI that provides failover capacity for this POI. Null if no redundant POI is designated. Used in network resilience and failover planning.
reference: ITU-T G.841; TM Forum SID GB922

description: Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: Points of Interconnect with regulatory designation (e.g., designated reference interconnect offers) require regulatory filings establishing or modifying that designation. The plain attribute `regulato

Foreign key linking to workforce.technician. Business justification: Telecommunications POIs require assigned technicians for maintenance, fault resolution, capacity upgrades, and emergency response. Business process: POI maintenance scheduling, SLA compliance tracking

Foreign key linking to enterprise.site. Business justification: Physical interconnection points located at enterprise customer sites (on-net buildings, colocation facilities). Business need: tracking which enterprise site hosts interconnect infrastructure for capa
Reference to the Service Level Agreement (SLA) defining performance commitments (availability, latency, packet loss) for this POI. Used for SLA monitoring and breach reporting.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922; ITU-T Y.1541; ETSI EN 302 217

description: Foreign key linking to network.topology. Business justification: A Point of Interconnect is served by a specific topology path. Network operations teams must correlate POI operational status against topology link state for fault management and capacity reporting —

Foreign key linking to network.transmission_link. Business justification: Interconnect points of interconnection physically terminate on transmission links. Operations teams need to track which transmission infrastructure serves each POI for capacity planning, fault isolati
Date on which this POI was first activated and began carrying live traffic. Used for asset age tracking, depreciation scheduling, and regulatory reporting of interconnect history.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922; Oracle Communications Order and Service Management

description: Total provisioned bandwidth capacity at this POI in megabits per second (Mbps). Applicable primarily for IP/SIP, MPLS, and Ethernet interconnect types. Used for traffic engineering and QoS planning.
reference: ITU-T G.826; ETSI EN 302 217; TM Forum SID GB922

description: Telcordia/iconectiv Common Language Location Identifier (CLLI) code for the network element or building where this POI is located. Industry-standard 11-character code used in OSS provisioning and regulatory filings.
reference: Telcordia GR-485-CORE (CLLI); FCC 47 CFR Part 51; Oracle Communications Order and Service Management

business_glossary_term: Configuration Management Database (CMDB) Configuration Item (CI) ID
description: ServiceNow CMDB Configuration Item (CI) identifier for this POI. Enables linking the POI business record to ITSM incident, change, and problem management workflows.
reference: ServiceNow ITSM CMDB; ISO/IEC 20000-1; TM Forum eTOM GB921

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code for the country where the POI is physically located (e.g., USA, GBR, DEU). Determines applicable regulatory framework and IOT tariff jurisdiction.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; ITU-T E.164; GSMA PRD IR.21

description: Timestamp when this POI record was first created in the system of record (Netcracker OSS/BSS Resource Inventory). Audit trail field for data lineage and compliance.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922; ISO/IEC 27001; ServiceNow CMDB

description: Name of the data center or colocation facility hosting the POI for IP-based or MPLS interconnects (e.g., 'Equinix NY5', 'CyrusOne Dallas'). Applicable for IP/SIP and MPLS interconnect types.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922; Netcracker OSS/BSS Resource Inventory

description: Date on which this POI was or is scheduled to be permanently decommissioned and removed from service. Null if the POI is still active. Used for lifecycle management and capacity reclamation planning.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922; SAP S/4HANA Asset Management

description: Name of the telephone exchange, central office, or network exchange facility where the POI is physically housed (e.g., 'Manhattan Central Office', 'Chicago Exchange 5'). Used in OSS topology mapping.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922; Nokia NetAct Configuration Management

description: Technology type of the interconnect at this POI. TDM = Time Division Multiplexing (legacy PSTN); IP_SIP = IP-based Session Initiation Protocol; MPLS = Multiprotocol Label Switching; ETHERNET = Layer 2 Ethernet handoff; FIBER = direct fiber interconnect.
reference: ITU-T G.805; ETSI EN 300 175; 3GPP TS 23.002; TM Forum SID GB922

description: Traffic jurisdiction classification for this POI. INTERSTATE = traffic crossing state lines (FCC jurisdiction); INTRASTATE = within-state traffic (state PUC jurisdiction); INTERNATIONAL = cross-border traffic; LOCAL = local exchange traffic. Determines applicable tariff and regulatory regime.
reference: FCC 47 CFR Part 51 and Part 69; ITU-T D.150; BEREC Guidelines

description: Scheduled maintenance window for this POI expressed as a cron-style or free-text schedule (e.g., 'SUN 02:00-04:00 UTC'). Used by NOC for planned outage coordination and change management.
reference: TM Forum eTOM GB921; ServiceNow ITSM Change Management; Nokia NetAct

description: Mobile or fixed network generation supported at this POI. Determines applicable technical standards, QoS parameters, and IOT tariff applicability. 5G NR = 5G New Radio; LTE = Long-Term Evolution.
reference: 3GPP TS 23.002; ITU-T IMT-2020; GSMA PRD IR.21

description: Contact details (name, phone, or email) for the Network Operations Center (NOC) team responsible for monitoring and managing this POI. Used for fault escalation and incident coordination.
reference: TM Forum eTOM GB921; Nokia NetAct Fault Management

description: Free-text operational notes or remarks about this POI, including special configuration details, known limitations, or historical context not captured in structured fields.

Current operational lifecycle state of the POI. ACTIVE = in-service and carrying traffic; PROVISIONING = being set up; SUSPENDED = temporarily halted; DECOMMISSIONED = permanently retired from service.
reference: TM Forum eTOM GB921; Nokia NetAct Configuration Management; ServiceNow CMDB

description: Classification of the peering arrangement at this POI. PUBLIC = via internet exchange point (IXP); PRIVATE = direct bilateral peering; BILATERAL = two-party agreement; MULTILATERAL = multi-party peering fabric. Applicable for IP peering POIs.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922; GSMA PRD IR.34; IETF RFC 7948

description: Business classification of the POI by traffic scope. DOMESTIC = national carrier interconnect; INTERNATIONAL = cross-border carrier handoff; TRANSIT = pass-through traffic routing; PEERING = bilateral traffic exchange; WHOLESALE = MVNO or reseller interconnect.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922; FCC 47 CFR Part 51; ITU-T E.164

description: Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for the POI, used in regulatory filings, carrier agreements, and OSS/BSS systems. Unique across all interconnect points. Sourced from Netcracker OSS/BSS Resource Inventory.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922; FCC Interconnection Rules 47 CFR Part 51

description: Human-readable descriptive name for the POI, typically referencing the exchange location or data center (e.g., 'NYC-Manhattan-Equinix-POI-01'). Used in operational dashboards and carrier communications.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922; Netcracker OSS/BSS Resource Inventory

description: Code identifying the Point of Presence (POP) network node associated with this POI. A POP is the physical network access location; multiple POIs may share a POP. Used in network topology and routing tables.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922; Netcracker OSS/BSS Resource Inventory

description: Signaling or routing protocol standard used at this POI. SIP = Session Initiation Protocol; H.323 = ITU multimedia protocol; SS7 = Signaling System 7 (PSTN); ISUP = ISDN User Part; SIGTRAN = SS7 over IP; BGP = Border Gateway Protocol; LDP = Label Distribution Protocol (MPLS).
reference: ITU-T Q.761; IETF RFC 3261 (SIP); ETSI EN 300 356; 3GPP TS 29.165

description: Indicates whether this POI has a redundant or backup interconnect path configured (True = redundant path exists; False = single path, no redundancy). Critical for SLA risk assessment and network resilience planning.
reference: ITU-T G.841; TM Forum SID GB922; ETSI EN 302 217

description: Regulatory classification of the POI as determined by applicable telecommunications authority (e.g., FCC, Ofcom, BEREC). Determines whether interconnect rates are tariff-regulated or commercially negotiated. Critical for compliance reporting.
reference: FCC 47 CFR Part 51; Ofcom General Conditions; BEREC Guidelines; ITU-T D.150

description: Total provisioned circuit capacity at this POI expressed as number of circuits (DS0 equivalents for TDM, or logical channels for IP). Principal quantitative measure of the POI resource. Used for capacity planning and SLA enforcement.
reference: ITU-T G.703; TM Forum SID GB922; Ericsson Network Manager Transport Management

description: Permitted traffic flow direction at this POI. INBOUND = traffic received from the connected carrier; OUTBOUND = traffic sent to the connected carrier; BIDIRECTIONAL = traffic flows in both directions.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922; ITU-T E.164; GSMA PRD IR.21

description: Reference to the trunk group associated with this POI. A trunk group is a logical bundle of circuits or channels provisioned at this interconnect point for traffic routing.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922; Ericsson Network Manager Transport Management

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to this POI record. Used for change tracking, data freshness monitoring, and Silver Layer incremental load processing.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922; ISO/IEC 27001; ServiceNow CMDB

description: Number of circuits currently in active use at this POI. Used to calculate utilization percentage for capacity management, congestion alerting, and expansion planning.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922; Nokia NetAct Performance Management

description: Unique surrogate primary key for the IOT tariff record in the interconnect domain. Serves as the SSOT identifier for wholesale rate lookup during TAP file rating and settlement invoice generation.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922; GSMA TAP3 Specification

description: Foreign key linking to partner.roaming_agreement. Business justification: IOT (Inter-Operator Tariff) rates are negotiated and published as part of roaming agreements. Linking iot_tariff to roaming_agreement enables tariff publication tracking per agreement and GSMA complia

Name or identifier of the business authority (person, team, or system) that approved this IOT tariff record for production use. Required for internal governance and audit compliance in the interconnect settlement process.
The date and time at which this IOT tariff record was formally approved for production use. Part of the tariff lifecycle audit trail. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.

The billing increment in seconds used for voice service IOT charge calculation. For example, a value of 60 means charges are calculated per full minute; a value of 1 means per-second billing. Applicable only for voice service types.

The date and time at which this IOT tariff record was first created in the data platform. Serves as the audit creation timestamp for data lineage and compliance purposes. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922; ISO/IEC 27001; GDPR Article 5

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the IOT rate is denominated (e.g., EUR, USD, SDR). GSMA IOT rates are typically denominated in EUR or IMF Special Drawing Rights (SDR). Used for settlement invoice currency conversion and financial reconciliation.
reference: ISO 4217; GSMA IOT Specification; GSMA TAP3 Specification

description: The GSMA-defined or operator-defined destination zone code to which this IOT rate applies. Zones group countries or network destinations for wholesale tariff banding. Used in TAP file rating to match usage records to the correct IOT rate.
reference: GSMA IOT Specification; GSMA TAP3 Specification; ITU-T E.164

description: Human-readable name of the destination zone (e.g., 'Zone 1 - EU', 'Zone 2 - Rest of World'). Used in settlement reports, finance dashboards, and operator billing correspondence.
reference: GSMA IOT Specification; TM Forum SID GB922

description: Negotiated discount percentage applied to the standard IOT rate under a bilateral roaming agreement. Expressed as a decimal fraction (e.g., 0.1500 = 15% discount). Null if no discount applies. Used in settlement invoice calculation to derive the effective net rate.
reference: GSMA PRD AA.12; TM Forum SID GB922

description: The calendar date from which this IOT tariff version becomes binding and applicable for wholesale roaming charge rating. Aligns with the GSMA IOT effective date published in the inter-operator tariff notification.
reference: GSMA IOT Specification; GSMA TAP3 Specification

description: The net IOT rate after applying any negotiated discount_percentage to the standard iot_rate. Stored as a pre-computed field to support high-volume TAP file rating performance. Expressed in the tariff currency per rate_unit.
reference: GSMA IOT Specification; GSMA TAP3 Specification; TM Forum SID GB922

description: The calendar date on which this IOT tariff version ceases to be applicable. Null indicates an open-ended tariff with no defined expiry. Used to enforce correct rate selection during TAP file rating for a given usage date.
reference: GSMA IOT Specification; GSMA TAP3 Specification

description: The exchange rate applied to convert the IOT rate from currency_code to settlement_currency_code for settlement invoice generation. Sourced from the agreed exchange rate mechanism defined in the bilateral roaming agreement (e.g., ECB rate, fixed contractual rate).
reference: GSMA PRD AA.12; ISO 4217; ECB Exchange Rate Reference

description: The GSMA-defined rate group classification for this IOT tariff entry. Rate groups are published in the GSMA IOT rate tables and used to categorise wholesale rates for settlement and benchmarking purposes. Examples: RG1, RG2, RG3 for voice; DG1, DG2 for data.
reference: GSMA IOT Specification; GSMA PRD IR.21

business_glossary_term: Home Network Public Land Mobile Network (PLMN) Code
description: The GSMA-assigned PLMN code (MCC+MNC) of the home operator network for which this IOT tariff is applicable. Enables multi-PLMN operators to maintain separate IOT rate tables per home network entity.
reference: GSMA PLMN List; ITU-T E.212; 3GPP TS 23.003

description: The initial billing period in seconds for voice calls, which may be charged at a different rate or as a flat fee before the standard per-interval rate applies. Common in IOT tariffs where the first 30 or 60 seconds are charged as a connection fee.
reference: GSMA TAP3 Specification; GSMA IOT Specification

description: The IOT rate applied during the initial_duration_seconds period of a voice call, if different from the standard iot_rate. Null if no differentiated initial rate applies. Expressed in the tariff currency.
reference: GSMA IOT Specification; GSMA TAP3 Specification

description: Classification of the IOT tariff by commercial arrangement type. 'standard' for GSMA-published rates, 'preferential' for bilaterally negotiated lower rates, 'reciprocal' for symmetric bilateral agreements, 'benchmark' for reference rates used in dispute resolution.
reference: GSMA IOT Specification; GSMA PRD AA.12; TM Forum SID GB922

description: The date on which this IOT tariff was officially published by the GSMA or notified by the visited operator. Used for audit trail and to verify compliance with GSMA notification lead-time requirements prior to the effective_from_date.
reference: GSMA IOT Specification; GSMA PRD IR.21

description: The wholesale IOT rate charged by the visited operator to the home operator for the specified service type and destination zone. Expressed in the currency defined by currency_code. For voice: rate per minute; for SMS: rate per message; for data: rate per kilobyte or megabyte as defined by rate_unit. This is the principal quantitative fact of the IOT tariff record.
reference: GSMA IOT Specification; GSMA TAP3 Specification; TM Forum SID GB922

description: The minimum wholesale charge applicable per usage event under this IOT tariff, expressed in the tariff currency. Ensures a floor charge is applied even for very short calls or minimal data usage. Used in TAP file rating to enforce minimum billing thresholds.
reference: GSMA IOT Specification; GSMA TAP3 Specification

description: The reference number or identifier of the official GSMA or bilateral operator notification document that introduced or amended this IOT tariff. Provides an audit trail linking the tariff record to the formal inter-operator communication.
reference: GSMA IOT Specification; GSMA PRD IR.21; TM Forum SID GB922

business_glossary_term: Near Real-Time Roaming Data Exchange (NRTRDE) Eligible Indicator
description: Indicates whether usage records rated under this IOT tariff are subject to NRTRDE near-real-time roaming data exchange reporting to the home operator. True for operators with NRTRDE obligations under GSMA PRD TD.57. Used for fraud detection and real-time roaming cost monitoring.
reference: GSMA PRD TD.57 NRTRDE; GSMA TAP3 Specification

description: Indicates whether this IOT tariff applies to peak, off-peak, or flat (time-independent) usage periods. Some bilateral roaming agreements differentiate IOT rates by time-of-day. 'flat' indicates no time-of-day differentiation.
reference: GSMA IOT Specification; TM Forum SID GB922

description: The unit of measure for the IOT rate. Defines the denominator for the iot_rate value. 'per_minute' for voice, 'per_message' for SMS/MMS, 'per_kb' or 'per_mb' for data, 'per_event' for supplementary services. Required for correct TAP file charge calculation.
reference: GSMA TAP3 Specification; GSMA IOT Specification

description: Free-text field for operational notes, special conditions, or exceptions associated with this IOT tariff record. Used by the interconnect team to document bilateral negotiation context, GSMA waiver references, or rate dispute notes.

Classification of the inter-operator roaming agreement under which this IOT tariff applies. 'bilateral' for direct operator-to-operator agreements, 'multilateral' for GSMA-governed multi-party agreements, 'hub' for roaming hub-mediated arrangements, 'mvno' for MVNO wholesale agreements, 'transit' for transit traffic arrangements.
reference: GSMA PRD IR.21; GSMA PRD AA.12; TM Forum SID GB922

description: The rounding method applied to calculated IOT charges during TAP file rating. Governs how fractional charge amounts are rounded to the precision defined by tap_decimal_places. Ensures consistent charge calculation between home and visited operators.
reference: GSMA TAP3 Specification; GSMA BA.12 TAP Data Dictionary

description: Granular classification of the roaming service within the service_type. Examples: MOC (Mobile Originated Call), MTC (Mobile Terminated Call), MO-SMS, MT-SMS, GPRS, VoLTE, 5G NR data. Used for detailed IOT rate differentiation and TAP file reconciliation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: moc|mtc|mo_sms|mt_sms|gprs|volte|5g_nr|mms_mo|mms_mt|supplementary — promote to reference product]
reference: GSMA TAP3 Specification; 3GPP TS 32.240; ITU-T E.164

description: The category of roaming service to which this IOT rate applies. Drives the rating engine selection logic in TAP file processing. Values align with GSMA TAP3 service type codes: voice (MOC/MTC), SMS (MO/MT), data (GPRS/LTE/5G), MMS, and supplementary services.
reference: GSMA TAP3 Specification; 3GPP TS 32.240; TM Forum SID GB922

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code used for the actual financial settlement between operators, which may differ from the IOT rate currency_code. For example, IOT rates may be denominated in EUR but settled in USD per bilateral agreement.
reference: ISO 4217; GSMA PRD AA.12; GSMA TAP3 Specification

description: The native primary key or record identifier of this IOT tariff in the originating operational system (e.g., Amdocs Revenue Management tariff ID, Netcracker OSS catalogue ID). Enables traceability from the Silver layer back to the system of record.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922; TM Forum eTOM

description: Code identifying the operational system of record from which this IOT tariff record was sourced or ingested (e.g., 'AMDOCS_RM', 'NETCRACKER_OSS', 'GSMA_PORTAL'). Supports data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver layer.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922; TM Forum eTOM

description: The number of decimal places used to express the IOT rate in TAP file records, as defined by the GSMA TAP3 specification. Governs the precision of charge amounts encoded in TAP files exchanged between operators. Typically 5 for most currencies.
reference: GSMA TAP3 Specification; GSMA BA.12 TAP Data Dictionary

description: The GSMA file exchange format type for which this IOT tariff is applicable. TAP3 for standard roaming settlement files, NRTRDE for Near Real-Time Roaming Data Exchange files used for fraud detection, RAP for Returned Account Procedure files handling billing disputes.
reference: GSMA TAP3 Specification; GSMA PRD TD.57 NRTRDE; GSMA PRD RAP Specification

description: Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this IOT tariff entry within the GSMA/operator tariff catalogue. Used as the business key in TAP file references and inter-operator correspondence.
reference: GSMA IOT Rate Group Specification; TM Forum SID GB922

description: Human-readable descriptive name for the IOT tariff entry, used in settlement reports, operator portals, and finance reconciliation dashboards.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922; GSMA IOT Specification

description: Current lifecycle state of the IOT tariff record. Controls whether the tariff is eligible for use in TAP file rating and settlement invoice generation. 'superseded' indicates a newer version has replaced this record.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922; GSMA IOT Specification

description: Version identifier for the IOT tariff record, enabling tracking of rate changes over time. Incremented each time the tariff is revised. Used to ensure TAP file rating applies the correct rate version.
reference: GSMA TAP3 Specification; TM Forum SID GB922

description: Indicates whether the IOT rate is inclusive of applicable taxes (True) or exclusive of taxes (False). Relevant for operators in jurisdictions where VAT or GST applies to wholesale roaming charges. Impacts settlement invoice tax treatment.
reference: GSMA IOT Specification; ETSI EN 301 908; EU VAT Directive 2006/112/EC

description: The date and time at which this IOT tariff record was most recently modified in the data platform. Used for change tracking, incremental data loads, and audit compliance. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922; ISO/IEC 27001; GDPR Article 5

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the visited network where the roaming subscriber consumed the service. Used to map usage to the correct IOT tariff zone and for regulatory reporting of international roaming charges.
reference: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3; GSMA TAP3 Specification; ITU-T E.164

business_glossary_term: Visited Network Public Land Mobile Network (PLMN) Code
description: The GSMA-assigned Public Land Mobile Network (PLMN) code (MCC+MNC) of the visited operator network. Used to uniquely identify the roaming partner network for IOT rate lookup and TAP file routing.
reference: GSMA PLMN List; ITU-T E.212; 3GPP TS 23.003

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each interconnect rate record in the wholesale rate schedule. Primary key for the interconnect_rate data product.
Reference to the bilateral carrier agreement or roaming agreement under which this rate applies. Links the rate to its governing commercial contract.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922; GSMA PRD IR.21

description: Foreign key linking to interconnect.iot_tariff. Business justification: rate is the internal wholesale interconnect rate schedule while iot_tariff is the published inter-operator tariff. Internal rates are typically derived from or aligned with IOT tariffs. Adding iot_tar

Foreign key linking to network.qos_profile. Business justification: Interconnect rates often vary by QoS class (e.g., premium voice vs. best-effort data). Tariff design requires linking rate structures to network QoS profiles for accurate billing and service different

Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: Regulated interconnect termination rates (e.g., MTR, FTR under regulatory cap) must be filed with or approved by national regulators. The plain attribute `regulatory_reference` on rate denormalizes th

Foreign key linking to partner.roaming_agreement. Business justification: Interconnect rates for roaming traffic are negotiated and published under specific roaming agreements. Linking enables rate validation against roaming agreement terms and ensures rate changes are trac

Self-referencing identifier pointing to the newer rate record that replaced this one when rate_status is 'superseded'. Enables forward navigation of the rate version chain for audit and dispute resolution purposes.
Name or employee identifier of the commercial or regulatory manager who approved this rate for activation. Required for audit trail and segregation of duties compliance in wholesale rate management.
reference: ISO/IEC 27001; TM Forum SID GB922; SOX Internal Controls

description: Date and time (ISO 8601 with timezone) when this rate was formally approved for activation. Part of the rate lifecycle audit trail required for regulatory compliance and dispute resolution.

Structural basis on which the rate is calculated: flat (single rate regardless of volume), tiered (rate changes at volume thresholds), volume_discount (discounted rate applied retroactively above threshold), or distance_based (rate varies by geographic distance between interconnect points). Determines the rating algorithm applied.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922; Amdocs Revenue Management Rating Engine

description: Date and time (ISO 8601 with timezone) when this rate record was first created in the system. Audit trail field for data lineage and compliance purposes.

ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the interconnect rates are denominated and settlement is conducted (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Must align with the settlement currency defined in the governing carrier agreement.
reference: ISO 4217; GSMA TAP3 Specification; ITU-T D.150

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code identifying the destination country for international termination or transit rates (e.g., USA, GBR, DEU). Used for international settlement and regulatory compliance reporting.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; ITU-T E.164; FCC International Settlement Policy

description: E.164 dialled number prefix or number range (e.g., '+1202', '+44207') to which this rate applies. Enables prefix-based rate lookup during CDR rating and settlement. May represent a country code, area code, or specific number block.
Calendar date from which this interconnect rate becomes effective and applicable for CDR rating and settlement calculations. Inclusive start boundary of the rate validity period.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922; FCC Part 51; Ofcom FAMR 2021

description: Calendar date on which this interconnect rate ceases to be effective (exclusive end boundary). Null indicates an open-ended rate with no scheduled expiry. Used to enforce rate versioning and prevent application of expired rates.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922; FCC Part 51; Ofcom FAMR 2021

description: Code identifying the specific Point of Presence (POP) or interconnect gateway at which traffic is exchanged under this rate. Rates may vary by interconnect point due to transport cost differences. Aligns with network inventory records.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922; Netcracker OSS/BSS Resource Inventory; ITU-T Q.700

description: IOT tariff classification code as defined by GSMA inter-operator tariff tables, applicable when this rate is used for roaming wholesale settlement. Maps to the GSMA IOT rate category (e.g., IOT-A, IOT-B, IOT-C) for NRTRDE and TAP file processing.
reference: GSMA PRD IR.21; GSMA TAP3 Specification; GSMA NRTRDE Specification

description: Date and time (ISO 8601 with timezone) when this rate record was most recently updated. Used for change tracking, incremental data pipeline processing, and audit compliance.

ITU-assigned three-digit Mobile Country Code (MCC) identifying the country of the counterparty mobile network. Used in roaming rate lookup and NRTRDE/TAP file processing to match rates to visited network operator records.
reference: ITU-T E.212; 3GPP TS 23.003; GSMA PRD IR.21

description: Minimum billable call duration in seconds applied during rating. Calls shorter than this threshold are billed at this minimum (e.g., 30-second minimum billing). Common in wholesale voice interconnect agreements to prevent micro-call gaming.
reference: GSMA PRD IR.85; TM Forum SID GB922; Amdocs Revenue Management

description: Two or three-digit Mobile Network Code (MNC) identifying the specific mobile network operator within the MCC country. Combined with MCC forms the PLMN identifier used in roaming rate resolution and TAP file matching.
reference: ITU-T E.212; 3GPP TS 23.003; GSMA PRD IR.21

description: Mobile or fixed network generation standard to which this rate applies (e.g., 2G, 3G, 4G LTE, 5G NR, VoLTE, VoIP). Rates may differ by network generation due to technology-specific interconnect costs and regulatory frameworks.

Free-text field for commercial or operational annotations related to this rate (e.g., special negotiation conditions, dispute history notes, regulatory waiver references). Not used in automated rating; for human reference only.

business_glossary_term: Near Real-Time Roaming Data Exchange (NRTRDE) Applicable Flag
description: Indicates whether this rate is subject to NRTRDE (Near Real-Time Roaming Data Exchange) reporting obligations. When true, CDRs rated under this rate must be transmitted to the counterparty within the NRTRDE transmission window for fraud prevention purposes.
reference: GSMA NRTRDE Specification (PRD IR.21); GSMA PRD FS.11

description: Per-unit wholesale charge applied during off-peak hours (typically evenings and nights on weekdays). Lower than peak rate to reflect reduced network demand. Expressed in settlement currency per billing unit.
reference: FCC Part 51; Ofcom FAMR 2021; TM Forum SID GB922

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code of the country where the traffic originates. Required for international transit and termination rates to distinguish bilateral from multilateral routing scenarios and apply correct regulatory frameworks.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; ITU-T E.164; FCC International Settlement Policy

description: Local time (HH:MM, 24-hour format) at which the peak rate period ends each weekday. Defines the upper boundary of the peak window for CDR rating tier selection.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922; Amdocs Revenue Management Rating Engine Configuration

description: Per-unit wholesale charge applied during peak hours (typically business hours on weekdays). Expressed in the settlement currency per billing unit (e.g., per minute for voice, per event for SMS, per MB for data). This is the principal quantitative rate value for the resource.
reference: FCC Part 51; Ofcom FAMR 2021; EU Regulation 531/2012 (Roam-Like-At-Home); TM Forum SID GB922

description: Local time (HH:MM, 24-hour format) at which the peak rate period begins each weekday. Used by the rating engine to determine which rate tier applies to a given CDR based on call start time.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922; Amdocs Revenue Management Rating Engine Configuration

description: Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this rate entry within the wholesale rate schedule. Used in TAP file references and inter-operator billing reconciliation.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922; GSMA TAP3 Specification

description: Human-readable descriptive name for the rate entry (e.g., 'US Domestic Voice Termination Peak', 'EU Roam-Like-At-Home SMS'). Used in rate schedule documentation and commercial negotiations.

Current lifecycle state of the rate record: active (currently in force for rating), pending (approved but not yet effective), superseded (replaced by a newer rate version), expired (past effective period), or withdrawn (cancelled before activation). Controls whether the rate is used in CDR rating and settlement.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922; Amdocs Revenue Management Rate Table Lifecycle

description: Classification of the rate by traffic direction and role: termination (delivering traffic to destination network), origination (collecting traffic from originating network), transit (passing traffic through an intermediate network), transit_international (cross-border transit), or access (local loop access charge). Governs regulatory framework applicability.
reference: FCC Part 51 Access Charge Rules; Ofcom FAMR; BEREC Guidelines on Interconnection

description: Maximum rate permitted by the applicable regulatory framework for this service type and destination (e.g., EU RLAH wholesale voice cap of €0.032/min, Ofcom FAMR charge control ceiling). The actual rate must not exceed this cap. Used for compliance validation and regulatory reporting.
reference: EU Regulation 531/2012 (RLAH); Ofcom FAMR 2021; FCC Part 51; BEREC BoR (22) 137

description: Duration increment in seconds to which call durations are rounded up for billing purposes (e.g., 6-second or 60-second rounding). Directly impacts settlement amounts and must align with the counterparty carrier agreement terms.
reference: GSMA PRD IR.85; TM Forum SID GB922; FCC Part 51

description: Type of telecommunications service to which this rate applies: voice (per-minute voice call termination/origination), sms (short message service per-event), data (per-kilobyte or per-megabyte data transit), mms (multimedia messaging), or premium_sms (premium-rate short message service). Determines the billing unit and regulatory regime.
reference: 3GPP TS 32.240; TM Forum SID GB922; GSMA PRD IR.85

description: Name of the operational system of record from which this rate was ingested (e.g., 'Amdocs Revenue Management', 'Netcracker OSS/BSS', 'Manual Upload'). Supports data lineage tracking and reconciliation between source systems and the lakehouse.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922; Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer Lineage Standards

description: TAP file rate category code as defined in the GSMA Transferred Account Procedure (TAP3) specification. Maps this rate to the correct TAP record type for inter-operator roaming settlement file generation and validation.
reference: GSMA TAP3 Specification (TD.57); GSMA PRD IR.21

description: IANA time zone identifier (e.g., 'America/New_York', 'Europe/London') used to interpret peak/off-peak/weekend time boundaries for this rate. Ensures correct tier selection across geographic regions and daylight saving transitions.
reference: IANA Time Zone Database; ITU-T E.164

description: Indicates whether this rate applies to inbound traffic (received from the counterparty carrier), outbound traffic (sent to the counterparty carrier), or bilateral (symmetric rate applicable in both directions). Critical for settlement calculation and IOT tariff alignment.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922; GSMA TAP3 Specification

description: Unit of measure against which the rate is applied for billing purposes: per_minute or per_second for voice, per_event for SMS/MMS, per_mb/per_kb/per_gb for data. Determines how CDR volumes are multiplied by the rate to produce settlement charges.
reference: 3GPP TS 32.240; GSMA TAP3 Specification; TM Forum SID GB922

description: Sequential version number of this rate record, incremented each time the rate is amended. Enables rate history tracking and ensures the correct version is applied to CDRs within a given effective period. Version 1 is the initial rate.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922; Amdocs Revenue Management Rate Versioning

description: Minimum traffic volume (in the rate_unit) at which a tiered or volume-discount rate becomes applicable. Null for flat-rate structures. Used by the rating engine to select the correct rate tier based on cumulative monthly traffic volumes.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922; Amdocs Revenue Management

description: Per-unit wholesale charge applied during weekend periods (Saturday and Sunday). May differ from both peak and off-peak weekday rates. Expressed in settlement currency per billing unit.
reference: FCC Part 51; Ofcom FAMR 2021; TM Forum SID GB922

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each Mobile Number Portability (MNP) transaction record in the interconnect domain. Primary key for this entity.
Reference to the inter-operator carrier agreement governing the MNP transaction between Telecommunication and the counterparty operator.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922; GSMA PRD IR.85

description: Foreign key linking to sales.channel. Business justification: The sales channel (retail store, online, telesales) that originated a port-in must be tracked on the MNP transaction for channel performance reporting, commission attribution, and port-in volume analy

Foreign key linking to enterprise.corporate_account. Business justification: Enterprise customers porting number blocks during office relocations or provider changes. Business need: tracking which enterprise account initiated bulk porting requests for service continuity, billi

Foreign key linking to interconnect.interconnect_carrier. Business justification: MNP transaction donor carrier is heavily denormalized with donor_tadig_code, donor_carrier_name, donor_mcc, donor_mnc. Normalizing to interconnect_carrier eliminates all redundancy and provides author

Foreign key linking to enterprise.enterprise_contract. Business justification: Enterprise customers porting large corporate number blocks (bulk MNP) do so under enterprise contracts that specify porting SLAs, settlement amounts, and regulatory deadlines. Enterprise contract mana

Foreign key linking to order.fulfillment_order. Business justification: MNP order fulfillment tracking: every MNP port-in/port-out is triggered by a fulfillment_order. Linking mnp_transaction to fulfillment_order enables end-to-end MNP order lifecycle reporting and regula

business_glossary_term: Hlr Inventory Network Equipment Id (Foreign Key)
description: Foreign key linking to inventory.network_equipment. Business justification: MNP transactions require HLR/HSS updates on specific physical network equipment. Troubleshooting failed ports and SLA breach analysis require knowing which inventory.network_equipment (HLR/HSS node) p

Foreign key linking to network.element. Business justification: Mobile number portability requires HLR/HSS updates to redirect routing. Operations must track which HLR/HSS network element processed each port for audit, troubleshooting, and regulatory compliance. R

Foreign key linking to order.line. Business justification: MNP porting is per-MSISDN/line. Linking mnp_transaction to the specific order line enables per-line MNP status tracking, settlement reconciliation, and regulatory per-number porting audit — a standard

Foreign key linking to compliance.mnp_compliance. Business justification: Each porting transaction generates a compliance record tracking SLA adherence, authorization verification, regulatory notification. Essential for porting compliance monitoring, regulatory reporting, a

Foreign key linking to inventory.msisdn_range. Business justification: MNP porting transactions must identify which MSISDN range the ported number belongs to for routing updates, regulatory compliance reporting, and range utilization tracking. Number portability operatio

Foreign key linking to sales.opportunity. Business justification: Port-in MNP transactions are the operational fulfillment of a won sales opportunity (competitor win). Telecom sales operations track port-in completions against opportunities for win-rate reporting, s

Foreign key linking to partner.agreement. Business justification: MNP inter-operator charges and settlement terms are governed by partner agreements. Linking enables MNP settlement reconciliation against partner agreement payment terms and regulatory deadline tracki

Foreign key linking to interconnect.poi. Business justification: MNP transactions involve physical or logical interconnect handoff points for number porting. The POI (Point of Interconnect) is the physical/logical boundary where the porting transaction is executed

Foreign key linking to interconnect.rate. Business justification: mnp_transaction has inter_operator_charge_applicable (boolean) and settlement_amount (decimal) indicating that inter-operator charges apply for MNP events. Adding rate_id links the MNP transaction to

Foreign key linking to partner.partner. Business justification: Number portability transactions often involve partner networks (MVNOs) as recipient operators. Business process: MNP coordination with partner operators, regulatory compliance reporting, partner opera

Foreign key linking to customer.subscriber. Business justification: Mobile Number Portability transactions are directly tied to a specific subscriber's MSISDN. Linking mnp_transaction to subscriber is essential for porting lifecycle management, regulatory SLA complian

The Port Authorisation Code (PAC) or equivalent subscriber-provided authorisation token used to validate the subscriber's consent for the port-out. Mandatory in many regulatory frameworks (e.g., Ofcom PAC regime, FCC subscriber verification). Null for port-in transactions where PAC is consumed by the gaining operator.
reference: Ofcom General Conditions C8; FCC 47 CFR Part 52; BEREC MNP Guidelines

description: Reference to the batch file or TAP/NRTRDE file submission in which this MNP transaction was included for inter-operator exchange or regulatory reporting. Supports reconciliation with interconnect file processing workflows.
reference: GSMA PRD TD.57 (TAP3); GSMA PRD IR.85; TM Forum SID GB922
