description: Free-text description of the reason the MNP request was cancelled. Populated only when port_status = 'cancelled'. Common reasons include subscriber withdrawal, duplicate request cancellation, or operator-initiated cancellation.
reference: 3GPP TS 23.116; FCC 47 CFR Part 52; TM Forum SID GB922

description: The actual date and time when the number portability transfer was successfully completed and the MSISDN became active on the recipient network. Null if the port has not yet completed.
reference: 3GPP TS 23.116; FCC 47 CFR Part 52; TM Forum SID GB922

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the jurisdiction in which the MNP transaction is executed. Determines the applicable regulatory framework (FCC, Ofcom, BEREC NRA, etc.) and porting timeline obligations.
reference: ISO 3166-1; ITU-T E.164; FCC 47 CFR Part 52; BEREC MNP Guidelines

description: The date and time when this MNP transaction record was first created in the system of record (Oracle Communications Order and Service Management / Netcracker OSS). Audit trail field for data lineage.

Result of the fraud prevention check performed on the MNP request. 'not_checked' — fraud screening not yet performed; 'passed' — no fraud indicators detected; 'flagged' — suspicious activity detected, under review; 'blocked' — request blocked due to confirmed fraud. Supports GSMA fraud prevention guidelines.
reference: GSMA PRD FS.11; GSMA PRD IR.85; ISO/IEC 27001; FCC 47 CFR Part 64

business_glossary_term: Home Location Register / Home Subscriber Server (HLR/HSS) Update Status
description: Status of the HLR (2G/3G) or HSS/UDM (4G/5G) subscriber profile update triggered by the MNP transaction. 'pending' — update not yet sent; 'completed' — subscriber profile successfully updated; 'failed' — update failed, requiring manual intervention.
reference: 3GPP TS 23.116; 3GPP TS 29.272; Ericsson Network Manager; Nokia NetAct

description: The Integrated Circuit Card Identifier of the SIM card associated with the MSISDN at the time of the porting request. Captured to support SIM swap validation and fraud prevention during the MNP process.
reference: ITU-T E.118; 3GPP TS 22.042; GSMA SGP.02; ISO/IEC 7812

description: The International Mobile Subscriber Identity associated with the subscriber's SIM at the time of the porting request. Used for HLR/HSS deprovisioning on port-out and provisioning on port-in. Constitutes PII as it uniquely identifies a subscriber.
reference: ITU-T E.212; 3GPP TS 23.003; GSMA PRD IR.21; GDPR Article 4

description: Indicates whether an inter-operator charge (IOT) is applicable for this MNP transaction under the governing carrier agreement. True = charge applies and will be raised in settlement; False = no charge applicable (e.g., regulatory zero-rating or waiver).
reference: GSMA PRD IR.85; TM Forum SID GB922; ITU-T D-Series Recommendations

business_glossary_term: Mobile Station International Subscriber Directory Number (MSISDN)
description: The full E.164-formatted mobile phone number being ported. This is the subscriber's publicly dialable number and constitutes PII as it directly identifies an individual subscriber.
reference: ITU-T E.164; 3GPP TS 23.003; GDPR Article 4; FCC 47 CFR Part 52

description: The radio access network generation (2G/3G/4G/5G) on which the subscriber's service is provisioned at the time of the MNP transaction. Relevant for HLR vs HSS/UDM routing update determination and network-specific porting procedures.
reference: 3GPP TS 22.011; 3GPP TS 23.116; ETSI; TM Forum SID GB922

business_glossary_term: Number Portability Administration Centre (NPAC) Order ID
description: The order identifier assigned by the Number Portability Administration Centre (NPAC) or equivalent national clearinghouse system (e.g., Syniverse, CODA) for this porting transaction. Used for cross-system reconciliation and regulatory audit.
reference: FCC 47 CFR Part 52; GSMA PRD IR.85; NTIA

description: The operator or entity that holds the ITU/national regulatory authority allocation of the number range (NXX or equivalent) containing the ported MSISDN. May differ from the donor operator in MVNO or wholesale scenarios.
reference: ITU-T E.164; FCC 47 CFR Part 52; Ofcom National Telephone Numbering Plan; NTIA

description: Externally-known unique reference number assigned to this MNP porting request by the Number Portability Administration Centre (NPAC) or equivalent national clearinghouse. Used for cross-operator tracking and regulatory audit.
reference: FCC 47 CFR Part 52; GSMA PRD IR.85; ITU-T E.164

description: Current lifecycle status of the MNP transaction. 'pending' — request received and awaiting validation; 'validated' — subscriber eligibility confirmed; 'completed' — number successfully ported; 'rejected' — request denied (see rejection_reason); 'cancelled' — request withdrawn before completion.
reference: 3GPP TS 23.116; TM Forum SID GB922; FCC 47 CFR Part 52; BEREC MNP Guidelines

description: Indicates the direction of the number portability event from Telecommunication's perspective. 'port_in' means the subscriber is moving their number to Telecommunication from another operator (donor). 'port_out' means the subscriber is leaving Telecommunication to another operator (recipient).
reference: 3GPP TS 23.116; TM Forum SID GB922; GSMA PRD IR.85

description: The elapsed time in hours between request_timestamp and completion_timestamp. Used for SLA compliance monitoring against regulatory maximum porting time mandates (e.g., FCC 1-business-day rule, Ofcom 1-working-day rule). Null if port not yet completed.
reference: FCC 47 CFR Part 52; Ofcom General Conditions; BEREC MNP Guidelines

description: The scheduled end date and time of the porting window. If the port is not completed within this window, the transaction may expire or require rescheduling per regulatory requirements.
reference: FCC 47 CFR Part 52; Ofcom General Conditions; BEREC MNP Guidelines; 3GPP TS 23.116

description: The scheduled start date and time of the porting window during which the number transfer is authorised to execute. Regulatory frameworks (e.g., FCC, Ofcom) mandate specific porting windows.
reference: FCC 47 CFR Part 52; Ofcom General Conditions; BEREC MNP Guidelines; 3GPP TS 23.116

description: Human-readable commercial name of the recipient (gaining) operator. Complements recipient_tadig_code for reporting and business user readability.
reference: GSMA PRD IR.85; TM Forum SID GB922

business_glossary_term: Recipient Network Transferred Account Data Interchange Group (TADIG) Code
description: The GSMA TADIG code identifying the recipient (gaining) operator network to which the MSISDN is being ported. Complements donor_tadig_code to fully describe the inter-operator porting pair.
reference: GSMA PRD IR.21; GSMA TADIG Code Registry; TM Forum SID GB922

description: The latest permissible timestamp by which the MNP transaction must be completed under applicable regulatory rules (e.g., FCC 1-business-day, Ofcom 1-working-day). Used for SLA breach detection and regulatory compliance monitoring.
reference: FCC 47 CFR Part 52; Ofcom General Conditions; BEREC MNP Guidelines; NTIA

description: Standardised code indicating why the MNP request was rejected. Common codes include: MSISDN_NOT_FOUND, OUTSTANDING_BALANCE, ACTIVE_CONTRACT, INVALID_ACCOUNT_HOLDER, DUPLICATE_REQUEST, REGULATORY_HOLD. Populated only when port_status = 'rejected'. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: MSISDN_NOT_FOUND|OUTSTANDING_BALANCE|ACTIVE_CONTRACT|INVALID_ACCOUNT_HOLDER|DUPLICATE_REQUEST|REGULATORY_HOLD|FRAUD_SUSPECTED|TECHNICAL_ERROR — promote to reference product]
reference: 3GPP TS 23.116; FCC 47 CFR Part 52; GSMA PRD IR.85; BEREC MNP Guidelines

description: Free-text narrative description of the rejection reason, providing additional context beyond the rejection_reason_code. Populated only when port_status = 'rejected'. Used for dispute resolution and regulatory reporting.
reference: 3GPP TS 23.116; FCC 47 CFR Part 52; TM Forum SID GB922

description: The date and time when the MNP porting request was formally submitted by the subscriber or the gaining operator. This is the principal business event timestamp marking the start of the porting lifecycle.
reference: 3GPP TS 23.116; FCC 47 CFR Part 52; GSMA PRD IR.85

description: The number of times this MNP transaction has been retried following a failure or rejection. Used for operational monitoring, SLA tracking, and identifying systemic porting issues with specific operators.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922; TM Forum eTOM

description: The routing number (RN) assigned to the ported MSISDN in the Number Portability database, used by all operators to correctly route calls and SMS to the ported number on the recipient network. Aligned with ITU-T E.164 routing principles.
reference: ITU-T E.164; 3GPP TS 23.116; GSMA PRD IR.85; FCC 47 CFR Part 52

description: Indicates whether the routing number in the Number Portability database and HLR/HSS/UDM has been successfully updated following port completion. 'pending' — update queued; 'updated' — successfully propagated; 'failed' — update error requiring intervention; 'not_required' — no routing change needed.
reference: 3GPP TS 23.116; 3GPP TS 23.003; TM Forum SID GB922; GSMA PRD IR.85

description: Classification of the subscriber service type associated with the porting MSISDN. Determines applicable porting rules, timelines, and regulatory obligations. 'mvno' indicates the subscriber is on a Mobile Virtual Network Operator (MVNO) arrangement.
reference: 3GPP TS 22.011; TM Forum SID GB922; GSMA PRD IR.85; FCC 47 CFR Part 52

description: The monetary amount charged or credited between Telecommunication and the counterparty operator for this MNP transaction under the applicable inter-operator tariff (IOT). Expressed in the settlement currency defined in the carrier agreement. Null if inter_operator_charge_applicable = False.
reference: GSMA PRD IR.85; TM Forum SID GB922; ITU-T D-Series Recommendations

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the currency in which the inter-operator settlement amount for this MNP transaction is denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Must align with the settlement currency in the governing carrier agreement.
reference: ISO 4217; GSMA PRD IR.85; TM Forum SID GB922

description: Indicates whether this MNP transaction exceeded the regulatory or contractual maximum porting time SLA. True = SLA breached; False = within SLA. Used for regulatory compliance reporting and inter-operator dispute management.
reference: FCC 47 CFR Part 52; Ofcom General Conditions; BEREC MNP Guidelines; TM Forum SID GB922

description: Identifies the operational system of record that originated this MNP transaction record. Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and audit traceability across BSS/OSS platforms.

Indicates whether the subscriber's explicit consent for the number port has been verified and recorded, as required by regulatory frameworks. True = consent confirmed; False = consent not yet verified or verification failed. Critical for regulatory compliance.
reference: FCC 47 CFR Part 52; Ofcom General Conditions C8; GDPR Article 7; BEREC MNP Guidelines

description: The date and time when this MNP transaction record was most recently modified in the system of record. Used for change tracking, SLA monitoring, and incremental data pipeline processing.

business_glossary_term: Near Real-Time Roaming Data Exchange (NRTRDE) Record ID
description: Unique surrogate identifier for each NRTRDE event record in the Silver layer lakehouse. Primary key for this entity.
Reference to the bilateral roaming agreement governing this traffic exchange between the visited and home operators. Determines applicable Inter-Operator Tariff (IOT) rates and settlement terms.
reference: GSMA PRD AA.12 Roaming Agreement; TM Forum SID

description: Foreign key linking to interconnect.iot_tariff. Business justification: NRTRDE record currently references IOT tariff via string iot_tariff_code. Normalizing to iot_tariff enables proper rate validation and settlement tracking. The FK will be populated during NRTRDE proce

Foreign key linking to partner.mvno_profile. Business justification: NRTRDE records for MVNO subscribers require MVNO attribution for near-real-time fraud monitoring per MVNO profile (subscriber_capacity_limit, fraud thresholds). Linking enables MVNO-specific fraud ale

Foreign key linking to compliance.privacy_consent. Business justification: NRTRDE records contain near-real-time subscriber data requiring consent for processing and sharing with roaming partners. Essential for consent validation in real-time data exchange and GDPR complianc

Identifier of the radio cell in the visited network where the roaming subscriber was served. Used for network-level fraud detection (e.g., impossible travel analysis) and geographic usage pattern analysis.

Foreign key linking to interconnect.rate. Business justification: nrtrde_record captures estimated_charge_amount for early fraud and usage monitoring but has no FK to the rate schedule used for estimation. Adding rate_id links each NRTRDE event to the applicable who

Foreign key linking to partner.roaming_agreement. Business justification: NRTRDE records are near-real-time roaming data exchange events governed by roaming agreements (nrtrde_enabled, nrtrde_threshold_amount). Linking enables per-agreement fraud threshold breach detection

Foreign key linking to customer.subscriber. Business justification: Near-real-time roaming data exchange (NRTRDE) records must be linked to the home-network subscriber for real-time roaming fraud detection and high-usage alerting. Regulatory GSMA requirements mandate
Reference to the TAP file that this NRTRDE record will ultimately reconcile against during post-event settlement. Links pre-TAP roaming visibility to the formal TAP billing cycle.
reference: GSMA TD.57 TAP3 Standard; GSMA BA.40

description: Foreign key linking to interconnect.tap_record. Business justification: NRTRDE records are near-real-time precursors to TAP records — they provide early visibility into roaming usage before the formal TAP file is produced. nrtrde_record has a tap_reconciliation_status fie

Foreign key linking to interconnect.interconnect_carrier. Business justification: NRTRDE record visited carrier is denormalized via visited_tadig, visited_plmn_id, visited_country_code. Normalizing to interconnect_carrier eliminates redundancy and provides authoritative carrier mas

Access Point Name used by the roaming subscriber for the data session, identifying the packet data network gateway and service type (e.g., internet, IMS, enterprise VPN). Applicable for DATA record types. Used for data service classification and IOT tariff selection.

Sequential number of the NRTRDE transmission batch (file) from the visited operator, used to detect missing batches and ensure complete data receipt. Increments with each new file transmission per GSMA BA.40 batch numbering rules.

Duration of the voice call or supplementary service event in seconds, as reported by the visited network. Applicable for VOICE and SUPPLEMENTARY record types. Used for IOT charge calculation and revenue assurance validation.
reference: GSMA BA.40; GSMA TD.57 TAP3; 3GPP TS 32.250

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the estimated IOT charge is denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency settlement reconciliation between international operators.
reference: ISO 4217; GSMA BA.40; GSMA PRD AA.14

description: Monetary difference between the estimated IOT charge in this NRTRDE record and the final charge confirmed in the reconciled TAP file. Populated after TAP reconciliation. Positive value indicates NRTRDE overestimated; negative indicates underestimation. Used for revenue assurance leakage analysis.
reference: GSMA BA.40; GSMA TD.57 TAP3; TM Forum Revenue Assurance Guidelines

description: Date and time (UTC) when this NRTRDE record was first ingested and created in the Silver layer lakehouse. Audit trail field for data lineage and processing SLA measurement.
reference: TM Forum Data Management Guidelines; ISO/IEC 27001

description: Volume of data transmitted from the network to the roaming subscriber's device (downlink) in kilobytes during a GPRS/data session. Applicable for DATA record types. Used for IOT data charge calculation.
reference: GSMA BA.40; GSMA TD.57 TAP3; 3GPP TS 32.251

description: Volume of data transmitted from the roaming subscriber's device to the network (uplink) in kilobytes during a GPRS/data session. Applicable for DATA record types. Used for IOT data charge calculation.
reference: GSMA BA.40; GSMA TD.57 TAP3; 3GPP TS 32.251

description: The destination number dialed by the roaming subscriber for voice calls (B-party number in E.164 format). Applicable for MOC record types. Used for fraud detection (e.g., premium rate number abuse, international revenue share fraud) and traffic analysis.
reference: ITU-T E.164; 3GPP TS 32.250; GSMA PRD FS.11

description: Estimated wholesale charge for this roaming event calculated by the visited network using the applicable IOT tariff at time of NRTRDE transmission. This is a pre-settlement estimate; the final charge is confirmed in the TAP file. Used for early revenue assurance and cash flow forecasting.
reference: GSMA BA.40; GSMA PRD AA.14 IOT; TM Forum SID

description: Calendar date (UTC) on which the roaming service event occurred, derived from service_start_timestamp. Used as a partition key in the Silver layer lakehouse for efficient time-range queries and daily revenue assurance reporting.
reference: GSMA BA.40; TM Forum Lakehouse Architecture

description: Flag indicating that this NRTRDE record has triggered a fraud alert to the Revenue Assurance domain based on anomalous usage patterns (e.g., impossible travel, velocity breach, SIM cloning signatures). True = fraud alert raised; False = no alert.
reference: GSMA PRD FS.11 Fraud Information Gathering System (FIGS); GSMA BA.40

description: Flag set by the visited network indicating that this subscriber's cumulative roaming usage has exceeded a predefined high-usage threshold, triggering potential fraud alert or credit limit notification to the home operator per GSMA BA.40 high-usage detection rules.
reference: GSMA BA.40 NRTRDE Standard; GSMA PRD FS.11 Fraud

business_glossary_term: Home Network Transferred Account Data Interchange Group (TADIG) Code
description: 5-character GSMA TADIG code identifying the subscriber's home network operator. Used to route NRTRDE records to the correct home PLMN for revenue assurance and fraud detection.
reference: GSMA TADIG Code Registry; GSMA BA.40; ITU-T E.212

description: 15-digit unique identifier of the mobile device used during the roaming event. Used for device-level fraud detection (e.g., blacklisted IMEI, IMEI/IMSI mismatch indicating SIM cloning) and equipment analytics.
reference: GSMA IMEI Database; 3GPP TS 23.003; GSMA PRD FS.11

description: 15-digit globally unique identifier assigned to the subscriber's SIM card, used to identify the roaming subscriber across visited and home networks. Classified as restricted PII as it directly identifies an individual subscriber.

Location Area Code identifying the geographic area within the visited network where the roaming event occurred. Used alongside Cell ID for subscriber location tracking and fraud detection in the interconnect domain.

business_glossary_term: Mobile Station International Subscriber Directory Number (MSISDN)
description: The subscriber's mobile phone number in E.164 format, representing the dialable number associated with the roaming event. Classified as restricted PII as it directly identifies an individual.

Radio access technology generation used to deliver the roaming service. Values: 2G (GSM/GPRS/EDGE), 3G (UMTS/HSPA), 4G_LTE (Long-Term Evolution), 5G_NR (5G New Radio), WIFI (Wi-Fi calling/VoWiFi). Impacts IOT tariff applicability and fraud pattern analysis.

Version of the GSMA BA.40 NRTRDE standard used to generate this record (e.g., 2.0, 3.0). Required for backward-compatible processing as operators may transmit records under different standard versions during migration periods.

Current lifecycle state of the NRTRDE record within the interconnect operations processing pipeline. RECEIVED=ingested from visited network; VALIDATED=passed format/content checks; ENRICHED=IOT tariff and agreement data applied; RECONCILED=matched to TAP file; REJECTED=failed validation; DISPUTED=charge under dispute.
reference: GSMA BA.40; TM Forum eTOM Process Framework

description: Specific CDR record type classifying the direction and nature of the roaming event. MOC=Mobile Originated Call, MTC=Mobile Terminated Call, GPRS=data session, SMS_MO=outbound SMS, SMS_MT=inbound SMS. Aligns with TAP3 record type taxonomy.
reference: GSMA TD.57 TAP3; 3GPP TS 32.250; GSMA BA.40

description: Standardized code indicating the reason a NRTRDE record was rejected during validation processing (e.g., invalid IMSI format, unknown TADIG, missing mandatory field, duplicate sequence number). Populated only when processing_status = REJECTED. Aligns with GSMA TAP error code taxonomy.
reference: GSMA TD.57 TAP3 Error Codes; GSMA BA.40

description: Indicates whether this roaming event is inbound (foreign subscriber roaming on our network — we are the visited operator) or outbound (our subscriber roaming on a foreign network — we are the home operator). Determines revenue assurance treatment and settlement flow.
reference: GSMA BA.40; GSMA PRD AA.12; TM Forum SID

description: Sequential number assigned to the NRTRDE record within a transmission batch, used to detect gaps, duplicates, and ordering issues during inter-operator exchange per GSMA BA.40.

The exact date and time (UTC) when the roaming service event concluded. For voice calls this is call release time; for data sessions this is PDP context deactivation. Used to derive call/session duration and validate against usage volume.

The exact date and time (UTC) when the roaming service event began — the principal real-world event time. For voice calls this is call answer time; for data sessions this is PDP context activation; for SMS this is submission time. Core field for NRTRDE 4-hour transmission SLA compliance measurement per GSMA BA.40.

Category of telecommunications service consumed during the roaming event. Drives IOT tariff selection, usage volume interpretation, and revenue assurance thresholds. Values: VOICE (voice call), SMS (short message), DATA (packet data), MMS (multimedia message), SUPPLEMENTARY (supplementary services).
reference: GSMA BA.40; 3GPP TS 32.250; TM Forum SID

description: Flag indicating whether this NRTRDE record was transmitted outside the GSMA BA.40 4-hour SLA window (transmission_latency_minutes > 240). True = SLA breached; False = within SLA. Used for inter-operator SLA performance reporting and penalty calculation.

Name of the NRTRDE transmission file from which this record was extracted, following GSMA BA.40 file naming conventions. Used for batch traceability, reprocessing, and audit trail in the interconnect operations pipeline.
reference: GSMA BA.40 NRTRDE Standard; TM Forum Data Lineage

description: Status of reconciliation between this NRTRDE pre-TAP record and the corresponding TAP3 billing record. PENDING=TAP file not yet received; MATCHED=successfully reconciled; UNMATCHED=no corresponding TAP record found; PARTIAL=partial match with charge discrepancy. Critical for revenue assurance.
reference: GSMA TD.57 TAP3; GSMA BA.40; TM Forum Revenue Assurance

description: Elapsed time in minutes between service_start_timestamp and transmission_timestamp. Measures compliance with the GSMA BA.40 4-hour (240-minute) near-real-time transmission SLA. Records exceeding 240 minutes are flagged for SLA breach reporting.

Date and time (UTC) when the NRTRDE record was transmitted by the visited network to the home network. Used to calculate transmission latency against the GSMA BA.40 4-hour SLA requirement from service delivery.

Date and time (UTC) when this NRTRDE record was last modified in the Silver layer lakehouse, such as after TAP reconciliation, status update, or fraud flag enrichment. Audit trail field for data lineage.
reference: TM Forum Data Management Guidelines; ISO/IEC 27001

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the TAP file record in the interconnect settlement system. Primary key for the tap_file data product.
reference: TM Forum SID; GSMA TAP3 Specification

description: Foreign key linking to interconnect.carrier_agreement. Business justification: TAP file currently references roaming agreement via string roaming_agreement_reference. Normalizing to carrier_agreement enables proper settlement tracking, tariff validation, and dispute resolution.

Foreign key linking to enterprise.corporate_account. Business justification: MVNO and wholesale scenarios where TAP files contain usage by enterprise customer's subscribers. Business need: attributing roaming usage to enterprise accounts for billing reconciliation, revenue ass

Foreign key linking to interconnect.iot_tariff. Business justification: tap_file currently stores iot_tariff_version as a free-text string but has no FK to the iot_tariff table. TAP files are processed against a specific IOT tariff version; linking directly to iot_tariff.

Foreign key linking to partner.roaming_agreement. Business justification: TAP files are generated and exchanged under specific roaming agreements (governing tap_file_frequency, tap_version, nrtrde_enabled). Linking enables roaming settlement reconciliation reports and SLA m

Foreign key linking to interconnect.interconnect_carrier. Business justification: TAP file sender is currently denormalized via sender_tadig_code. Normalizing to interconnect_carrier provides authoritative carrier master reference and eliminates redundant country_code. The FK will

The date and time at which the recipient operator or clearinghouse issued a formal acknowledgement (RAP or positive ACK) for this TAP file. Marks the completion of the file exchange lifecycle for settlement purposes.
reference: GSMA TAP3 TD.57; GSMA RAP Specification TD.32

description: The name of the inter-operator clearinghouse or data exchange hub through which this TAP file was routed (e.g., Syniverse, BICS, GSMA Clearing House). Identifies the intermediary responsible for file routing, validation, and settlement statement generation.
reference: GSMA TAP3 TD.57; GSMA Clearing House Procedures

description: The unique reference number assigned by the inter-operator clearinghouse (e.g., GSMA Clearing House, Syniverse, BICS) upon receipt of this TAP file. Used for end-to-end traceability, dispute resolution, and reconciliation against clearinghouse settlement statements.
reference: GSMA TAP3 TD.57; GSMA Clearing House Procedures

description: The date and time at which this TAP file record was first captured in the interconnect settlement data platform (Silver layer). Audit trail field for data lineage and reconciliation.

The timestamp of the latest CDR event contained within this TAP file, representing the end of the usage data period covered by the batch. Used alongside data_period_start_timestamp for gap detection and completeness validation.
reference: GSMA TAP3 TD.57; GSMA PRD TD.32

description: The timestamp of the earliest CDR event contained within this TAP file, representing the start of the usage data period covered by the batch. Used for period-based reconciliation, billing cycle alignment, and regulatory reporting.
reference: GSMA TAP3 TD.57; GSMA PRD TD.32

description: The reference number of any commercial dispute raised against this TAP file by the recipient operator or clearinghouse. Populated when file_status is 'disputed'. Links to the dispute management workflow for IOT charge challenges and resolution tracking.
reference: GSMA PRD BA.12; TM Forum eTOM; GSMA TAP3 TD.57

description: The foreign exchange rate applied to convert the TAP file settlement currency to the home operator's local accounting currency at the time of settlement. Used for financial reporting, revenue assurance, and EBITDA calculations in multi-currency environments.
reference: GSMA PRD BA.12; ISO 4217; SAP S/4HANA Finance Module

description: The date and time at which the TAP file was generated by the sending operator's mediation or billing system (Amdocs Revenue Management). Represents the principal business event time for the file batch. Used for timeliness SLA measurement per GSMA NRTRDE and TAP submission deadlines.
reference: GSMA TAP3 TD.57; GSMA NRTRDE Specification

description: The physical size of the TAP file in bytes as received from the sender or clearinghouse. Used for transmission integrity checks, storage capacity planning, and anomaly detection (unusually large or small files may indicate encoding errors).

Current lifecycle state of the TAP file within the inter-operator settlement workflow. Transitions: created → sent → acknowledged (clean acceptance) or rejected (validation failure) → disputed (commercial challenge) or resubmitted (corrected re-send). Drives settlement reconciliation and SLA monitoring.
reference: GSMA TAP3 TD.57; TM Forum eTOM Process Framework

description: Classifies the type of inter-operator data exchange file: TAP (standard Transferred Account Procedure batch), NRTRDE (Near Real-Time Roaming Data Exchange for fraud detection), RAP (Returned Account Procedure for rejected records), or STOP (Stop file halting processing). Determines processing pipeline and settlement treatment.
reference: GSMA TAP3 TD.57; GSMA NRTRDE Specification PRD TD.32

description: Indicates whether this TAP file is a test/simulation file (True) or a live production file (False) carrying real roaming charges. Test files must be excluded from financial settlement calculations and revenue reporting.
reference: GSMA TAP3 TD.57; GSMA PRD TD.32

description: The predominant radio access network technology type of the CDRs contained in this TAP file (e.g., LTE, 5G NR, VoLTE). Used for technology-segmented roaming analytics, IOT tariff differentiation, and network capacity planning.
reference: 3GPP TS 32.297; GSMA TAP3 TD.57; ETSI TS 132 297

business_glossary_term: Near Real-Time Roaming Data Exchange (NRTRDE) Reference
description: The reference identifier linking this TAP file to its corresponding NRTRDE near-real-time roaming data exchange record. NRTRDE files are transmitted within 4 hours of call completion for fraud detection; this reference enables correlation between the NRTRDE early notification and the final TAP settlement file.
reference: GSMA NRTRDE Specification PRD TD.32; GSMA TAP3 TD.57

description: For resubmitted TAP files, the sequence number of the original file that this resubmission replaces. Enables lineage tracing from corrected files back to the original rejected batch. Null for first-time submissions.
reference: GSMA TAP3 TD.57; GSMA RAP Specification TD.32

description: The outcome of the TAP file processing pipeline within the operator's BSS/OSS systems (Amdocs Revenue Management, Netcracker OSS/BSS). Indicates whether all CDRs were successfully rated and posted to settlement accounts, partially processed, or failed. Drives exception management workflows.
reference: TM Forum eTOM; GSMA TAP3 TD.57; Amdocs Revenue Management

description: The reference number of the RAP (Returned Account Procedure) file generated in response to validation failures in this TAP file. Links the TAP file to its corresponding rejection notification for dispute tracking and resubmission management.
reference: GSMA RAP Specification TD.32; GSMA TAP3 TD.57

description: The date and time at which the TAP file was received and acknowledged at the clearinghouse or by the recipient operator. Used to calculate transit latency and confirm delivery within bilateral SLA windows.
reference: GSMA TAP3 TD.57; GSMA PRD TD.32

description: The ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the recipient operator's home country. Used for bilateral traffic analysis, regulatory reporting, and determining applicable IOT tariff zones and settlement currency conventions.
reference: ISO 3166-1; GSMA TAP3 TD.57; ITU-T E.212

description: The five-character TADIG code identifying the receiving (home) network operator to whom this TAP file is addressed. Determines routing through the clearinghouse and the settlement counterparty for IOT charges.
reference: GSMA TADIG Code Registry; GSMA TAP3 TD.57

description: The reconciliation state of this TAP file against the clearinghouse settlement statement. Tracks whether the file's declared charges match the clearinghouse-confirmed amounts. Discrepancies trigger revenue assurance investigations. Used for MRR and ARPU reporting accuracy.
reference: TM Forum eTOM; GSMA Clearing House Procedures; GSMA PRD BA.12

description: The number of times this TAP file has been resubmitted after rejection or dispute. A value greater than zero indicates the file has been corrected and resent. Used for operator performance monitoring and SLA compliance tracking.
reference: GSMA TAP3 TD.57; GSMA RAP Specification TD.32

description: Indicates whether this TAP file represents inbound roaming (foreign subscribers using our network — we are the visited operator and sender) or outbound roaming (our subscribers using a foreign network — we are the home operator and recipient). Critical for revenue assurance and IOT settlement accounting.
reference: GSMA TAP3 TD.57; TM Forum eTOM; GSMA PRD BA.12

description: The sequential file number assigned by the sending operator to uniquely identify this TAP file within the bilateral exchange. Follows GSMA TAP3 five-digit sequence numbering convention (00001–99999, wrapping). Used for duplicate detection and gap analysis in file receipt tracking.
reference: GSMA TAP3 Specification TD.57; ITU-T E.212

description: The ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the TAP file charges are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Governs the currency for clearinghouse settlement and financial reconciliation between operators.
reference: ISO 4217; GSMA TAP3 TD.57; GSMA PRD BA.12

description: The reference number of the clearinghouse settlement statement that includes charges from this TAP file. Links the TAP file batch to the financial settlement document used for inter-operator payment and accounting entries in SAP S/4HANA.
reference: GSMA Clearing House Procedures; SAP S/4HANA Finance Module; GSMA PRD BA.12

description: The name of the operational system of record that generated or ingested this TAP file record (e.g., Amdocs Revenue Management, Comverse ONE, Netcracker OSS/BSS). Used for data lineage tracking and issue triage in the Silver layer.

The date and time at which the TAP file was submitted to the clearinghouse or directly to the recipient operator. Used to measure compliance with GSMA TAP submission timeliness requirements (typically within 36 hours of the last CDR in the file).
reference: GSMA TAP3 TD.57; GSMA PRD TD.32

description: The TAP3 protocol release version used to encode this file (e.g., TAP3R10, TAP3R12). Governs the ASN.1 schema and field set applicable to the file. Critical for parser selection during file ingestion and validation.
reference: GSMA TAP3 Specification TD.57; ETSI TS 132 297

description: The total tax component included in the TAP file charge amount, as declared in the file header. Applicable in jurisdictions where VAT or withholding tax applies to inter-operator roaming settlements. Used for financial reporting and tax compliance.
reference: GSMA TAP3 TD.57; GSMA PRD BA.12; FCC Regulatory Reporting

description: The total number of Call Detail Records (CDRs) contained within this TAP file as declared in the file header. Used for completeness validation against the actual parsed record count and for settlement volume reporting.
reference: GSMA TAP3 TD.57; TM Forum SID

description: The gross total Inter-Operator Tariff (IOT) charge amount declared in the TAP file header, representing the sum of all roaming charges for CDRs in this batch. Expressed in the settlement currency. Used as the primary financial control total for clearinghouse reconciliation and operator settlement.
reference: GSMA TAP3 TD.57; GSMA IOT Specification PRD BA.12

description: The date and time at which this TAP file record was most recently modified in the interconnect settlement data platform. Tracks status transitions, validation updates, and reconciliation changes.

The total number of validation errors identified during TAP file parsing and structural/business rule validation. A non-zero value triggers RAP (Returned Account Procedure) generation. Used for quality monitoring and operator performance tracking.
reference: GSMA TAP3 TD.57; GSMA RAP Specification

description: A structured summary of the validation errors encountered during TAP file processing, including error codes and descriptions as defined in the GSMA TAP3 specification. Supports root cause analysis, operator dispute resolution, and resubmission guidance.
reference: GSMA TAP3 TD.57; GSMA RAP Specification TD.32

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each individual TAP roaming usage record (CDR) within the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for the tap_record data product.
reference: GSMA TAP3 Specification (TD.57); TM Forum SID

description: Reference to the bilateral roaming agreement governing this inter-operator traffic exchange, linking to the applicable IOT tariff and settlement terms.
reference: GSMA PRD IR.21; TM Forum SID

description: Foreign key linking to interconnect.iot_tariff. Business justification: TAP record has iot_rate_applied and iot_rate_unit but no FK to the tariff definition. Normalizing to iot_tariff enables proper rate validation and audit trail. Note: iot_rate_applied and iot_rate_unit

Foreign key linking to partner.mvno_profile. Business justification: TAP records for MVNO subscribers must be attributed to the MVNO profile for wholesale roaming billing and capacity reporting. Linking enables MVNO-level roaming usage analytics and wholesale invoice g

Identifier of the radio cell or base station where the roaming subscriber was served during this event, as reported by the visited network. Used for geographic traffic analysis and network capacity planning.

Foreign key linking to interconnect.rate. Business justification: tap_record stores iot_rate_applied and iot_rate_unit as inline values but has no FK to the rate table. Each TAP CDR is rated against a specific wholesale rate schedule. Adding rate_id links the CDR to

Foreign key linking to partner.roaming_agreement. Business justification: Each TAP record is a roaming usage event governed by a specific roaming agreement. Linking enables per-agreement usage analytics, fraud threshold monitoring (roaming_agreement.fraud_threshold_amount),

Foreign key linking to customer.subscriber. Business justification: Roaming revenue assurance and fraud detection require linking each TAP record to the home-network subscriber who generated the roaming usage. Telecom domain experts universally expect this link for su
Reference to the parent TAP3 file batch from which this individual roaming CDR was extracted. Supports file-level reconciliation and settlement tracing.

Foreign key linking to interconnect.interconnect_carrier. Business justification: TAP record visited carrier is denormalized via visited_network_tadig and visited_country_code. Normalizing to interconnect_carrier eliminates redundancy and provides authoritative carrier master refer

Classification of the billable roaming event captured in this TAP record. MOC=Mobile Originated Call, MTC=Mobile Terminated Call, GPRS=data session, SMS_MO/MT=Short Message Service originated/terminated, MMS_MO/MT=Multimedia Message Service. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: MOC|MTC|GPRS|SMS_MO|SMS_MT|MMS_MO|MMS_MT — promote to reference product]

E.164 telephone number of the called party (B-number) for MOC voice events. Used for traffic analysis, fraud detection (e.g., IRSF detection), and regulatory reporting. Null for MTC, GPRS, and messaging events.
reference: ITU-T E.164; GSMA TAP3 Specification (TD.57) — CalledNumber; FCC 47 CFR Part 64

description: Classification of the charge category applied to this roaming event (e.g., AIRTIME for standard voice/data, ROAMING_SURCHARGE for additional roaming fees, INTERCONNECT for transit charges). Used for revenue assurance and settlement reconciliation.
reference: GSMA PRD BA.12 (IOT); TM Forum SID; Amdocs Revenue Management

description: Gross monetary amount charged to the home network operator for this individual roaming event, expressed in the settlement currency. Calculated by the visited network by applying the applicable IOT rate to the consumed duration or volume.
reference: GSMA TAP3 Specification (TD.57) — ChargeInformation.chargeAmount; GSMA PRD BA.12 (IOT)

description: Volume of data transmitted from the network to the subscriber's device (downlink) during a GPRS/data session, measured in kilobytes. Null for non-data call event types.
Volume of data transmitted from the subscriber's device to the network (uplink) during a GPRS/data session, measured in kilobytes. Null for non-data call event types.
ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the destination country for the called party in MOC voice events. Used for international traffic analysis and IOT tariff zone classification.
Discount applied to the gross charged amount for this roaming event as per the bilateral roaming agreement or special IOT discount arrangement. Reduces the net settlement obligation.
reference: GSMA TAP3 Specification (TD.57) — DiscountInformation; GSMA PRD BA.12

description: Duration of the voice call or data session in seconds as recorded by the visited network. Zero for instantaneous events (SMS/MMS). Used for IOT charge calculation and traffic analytics.

Exchange rate applied to convert the settlement currency amount to the home network's functional accounting currency at the time of TAP file processing. Used for financial reporting and revenue assurance reconciliation.
reference: GSMA PRD BA.12; ISO 4217; SAP S/4HANA Finance module

description: Flag set to True when this TAP record has been identified as potentially fraudulent by the fraud management system (e.g., IRSF, SIM cloning, abnormal roaming patterns). Triggers investigation workflow in the Revenue Assurance domain.
reference: GSMA PRD FS.11 (Fraud Information Gathering System); GSMA PRD IR.82; FCC Anti-Fraud Regulations

description: Charged amount converted to the home network operator's functional accounting currency using the exchange_rate. Used for financial reporting, EBITDA calculation, and MRR/revenue recognition in SAP S/4HANA.
reference: GSMA PRD BA.12; SAP S/4HANA Finance; TM Forum SID

business_glossary_term: Home Network Transferred Account Data Interchange Group (TADIG) Code
description: 5-character GSMA TADIG code identifying the home network operator (HPMN) to which the roaming subscriber belongs and to whom the TAP file is addressed for settlement.
reference: GSMA TADIG Code Registry; GSMA PRD TD.57; TM Forum SID

description: 15-digit unique identifier of the mobile device used by the roaming subscriber during this call event. Used for fraud detection, device analytics, and GSMA equipment identity register checks.

15-digit globally unique identifier assigned to the visiting subscriber's SIM card, comprising MCC+MNC+MSIN. Used to identify the roaming subscriber for settlement and fraud detection. Classified as restricted PII as it uniquely identifies an individual subscriber.
reference: 3GPP TS 23.003; GSMA TAP3 Specification (TD.57); GDPR Article 4

description: The specific IOT unit rate applied by the visited network to calculate the charged amount for this roaming event (e.g., rate per minute for voice, rate per KB for data). Sourced from the applicable IOT tariff table in effect at the time of service.
reference: GSMA PRD BA.12 (Inter-Operator Tariff); GSMA TAP3 Specification (TD.57)

description: Unit of measure for the IOT rate applied to this roaming event (e.g., PER_MINUTE for voice calls, PER_KB or PER_MB for data sessions, PER_MESSAGE for SMS/MMS).
reference: GSMA PRD BA.12 (Inter-Operator Tariff); GSMA TAP3 Specification (TD.57)

description: UTC timestamp of the most recent modification to this TAP record in the Silver Layer (e.g., status change from PENDING to ACCEPTED, fraud flag update, RAP reference population). Supports audit trail and change tracking.
Location Area Code (LAC) or Tracking Area Code (TAC) identifying the geographic area within the visited network where the roaming subscriber was registered during this event.

business_glossary_term: Mobile Station International Subscriber Directory Number (MSISDN)
description: The E.164 telephone number of the visiting roaming subscriber. Used for call routing, subscriber identification, and customer-facing settlement records. Classified as restricted PII.
reference: ITU-T E.164; 3GPP TS 23.003; GSMA TAP3 Specification (TD.57); GDPR Article 4

description: Radio access technology used by the visiting subscriber during this roaming event (e.g., 2G/GSM, 3G/UMTS, 4G LTE, 5G NR). Relevant for IOT tariff differentiation and network analytics.
reference: 3GPP TS 29.274; GSMA TAP3 Specification (TD.57); ETSI TS 132 250

business_glossary_term: Near Real-Time Roaming Data Exchange (NRTRDE) Flag
description: Indicates whether this roaming CDR was also transmitted via the NRTRDE near-real-time roaming data exchange mechanism (within 4 hours of the call event) in addition to the standard TAP3 file. Used for fraud detection and real-time roaming cost monitoring.
reference: GSMA PRD IR.82 (NRTRDE); GSMA PRD FS.11

business_glossary_term: Near Real-Time Roaming Data Exchange (NRTRDE) Transmission Timestamp
description: UTC timestamp when this CDR was transmitted via the NRTRDE mechanism to the home network. Null if nrtrde_flag is False. Used to verify compliance with the GSMA 4-hour NRTRDE transmission SLA.
reference: GSMA PRD IR.82 (NRTRDE); GSMA PRD FS.11

description: Indicates whether this TAP record represents a partial CDR (e.g., a long-duration data session split across multiple records by the visited network). True=partial record requiring reassembly; False=complete single record.

Current processing and settlement lifecycle status of this TAP roaming record. ACCEPTED=validated and accepted for settlement; REJECTED=failed TAP validation; DISPUTED=under inter-operator dispute; PENDING=awaiting validation; SETTLED=financially settled.
reference: GSMA TAP3 Specification (TD.57); GSMA PRD BA.12; TM Forum eTOM

description: GSMA-standard error/rejection code assigned when a TAP record fails validation (record_status = REJECTED). Codes are defined in GSMA TD.57 Annex and indicate the specific validation rule that was violated (e.g., missing mandatory field, invalid IMSI format, timestamp out of range).
reference: GSMA TAP3 Specification (TD.57) — Annex; GSMA PRD TD.60 (RAP)

description: Indicates the direction of roaming from the perspective of the home network operator. INBOUND=a subscriber from another network is roaming on our network (we are the VPMN); OUTBOUND=our subscriber is roaming on another network (we are the HPMN).
reference: GSMA PRD IR.21; TM Forum SID; GSMA TAP3 Specification (TD.57)

description: Sequential position of this CDR within its parent TAP3 file, as assigned by the visited network operator. Used for ordering, gap detection, and duplicate identification during TAP file processing.
3GPP teleservice or bearer service code identifying the specific service used during this roaming event (e.g., 11=telephony, 22=SMS, 41=data circuit async). Maps to the TAP3 BasicServiceUsed element.
UTC timestamp marking the exact moment the billable roaming event ended (call release for voice, session close for GPRS). Null for instantaneous events such as SMS/MMS.

UTC timestamp marking the exact moment the billable roaming event began (call answer time for voice, session start for GPRS, message submission for SMS/MMS). Principal business event time per TAP3 specification.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the inter-operator settlement charge for this TAP record is denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Determined by the bilateral roaming agreement.
reference: ISO 4217; GSMA TAP3 Specification (TD.57) — TapCurrency; GSMA PRD BA.12

description: Calendar month (YYYY-MM format) to which this TAP record is assigned for inter-operator financial settlement. Determines the billing cycle for IOT invoice generation between the visited and home network operators.
reference: GSMA PRD BA.12 (IOT Settlement); GSMA TAP3 Specification (TD.57)

description: Operational system of record from which this TAP record was ingested into the Databricks Silver Layer (e.g., Amdocs Revenue Management TAP processing module, Comverse ONE, NRTRDE hub, or industry clearinghouse). Used for data lineage and reconciliation.
reference: TM Forum eTOM; Amdocs Revenue Management; Comverse ONE

description: UTC timestamp when this TAP record was first created and written to the Databricks Silver Layer during TAP file ingestion and parsing. Audit trail for data lineage and SLA monitoring.
reference: TM Forum SID; GSMA TAP3 Specification (TD.57)

description: Sequential file number assigned by the visited network operator to the TAP3 file containing this record. Together with visited_network_tadig and home_network_tadig, uniquely identifies the source TAP file for audit and reconciliation.
Version of the GSMA TAP3 specification used to encode this roaming record (e.g., TAP3R12). Required for correct parsing, validation, and backward-compatibility handling during TAP file processing.

Tax or levy amount applicable to this roaming event as specified in the TAP record, if any. Included per TAP3 TaxInformation element where the visited network applies local taxation to wholesale charges.
reference: GSMA TAP3 Specification (TD.57) — TaxInformation; ITU-T D.98

description: Unique identifier for the asset lifecycle event record. Primary key.

Foreign key linking to compliance.audit. Business justification: Asset lifecycle events (decommissioning, write-offs, major state changes) are frequently triggered by compliance audit findings. The existing audit_date and auditor_name plain attributes are denormali

Foreign key linking to inventory.cpe_asset. Business justification: asset_lifecycle_event tracks all lifecycle state transitions for every asset type in the inventory domain. The existing asset_reference: BIGINT is a polymorphic column with asset_category as discrimin

Foreign key linking to workforce.depot. Business justification: Asset lifecycle events (goods receipt, transfer, audit, decommission) occur at specific depots. Telecom warehouse operations require depot attribution on lifecycle events for audit trails, inter-depot

Foreign key linking to inventory.fiber_cable. Business justification: Typed FK from asset_lifecycle_event to fiber_cable. Fiber cable lifecycle events (installation, inspection, splice repair, conduit damage, decommission) must link directly to the fiber cable record. P
Reference to the customer order or internal order that triggered this lifecycle event (e.g., service activation order, equipment procurement order). Nullable for non-order-driven events.

Foreign key linking to inventory.ip_address_pool. Business justification: Typed FK from asset_lifecycle_event to ip_address_pool. IP address pools are logical inventory assets that undergo lifecycle transitions (creation, exhaustion alerts, CGNAT enablement, decommission, p

Foreign key linking to inventory.msisdn_range. Business justification: Typed FK from asset_lifecycle_event to msisdn_range. MSISDN/DID number ranges are logical inventory assets that undergo lifecycle transitions (allocation from regulator, activation, quarantine, expira

Foreign key linking to inventory.network_equipment. Business justification: Typed FK from asset_lifecycle_event to network_equipment. Network equipment lifecycle events (installation, hardware swap, end-of-life, audit) must link directly to the network equipment record. This

Foreign key linking to assurance.noc_incident. Business justification: Bulk asset replacements and emergency deployments during major NOC incidents must be linked to the incident record for cost tracking, post-incident review, and regulatory reporting of infrastructure c

Foreign key linking to inventory.olt_asset. Business justification: Typed FK from asset_lifecycle_event to olt_asset. OLT lifecycle events (commissioning, maintenance, firmware upgrade, decommission) must link directly to the OLT asset record. Part of the normalizatio

Foreign key linking to inventory.ont_asset. Business justification: Typed FK from asset_lifecycle_event to ont_asset. ONT lifecycle events (installation, firmware update, decommission, optical power audit) must link directly to the ONT asset record. Part of the normal

Foreign key linking to assurance.outage_record. Business justification: Asset replacements performed during outage restoration (e.g., emergency OLT swap, fiber splice) must link to the outage record for restoration cost tracking, post-outage review, and regulatory reporti

Foreign key linking to partner.partner. Business justification: Asset lifecycle events (installation, maintenance, decommission) are frequently executed by or attributed to specific partners. Partner SLA performance reporting and partner-specific asset lifecycle a

Foreign key linking to assurance.problem_record. Business justification: ITIL problem management permanent fixes often involve asset changes (firmware upgrades, hardware replacement). Linking asset_lifecycle_event to problem_record supports tracking which asset changes wer

Foreign key linking to network.ran_cell. Business justification: RAN cell commissioning, decommissioning, hardware swap, and software upgrade events are tracked as asset lifecycle events. Telecom field operations teams record RAN cell state transitions (installed,

Foreign key linking to inventory.sim_stock. Business justification: Typed FK from asset_lifecycle_event to sim_stock, replacing the polymorphic asset_reference pattern. SIM stock lifecycle events (activation, deactivation, profile download, quarantine) must be traceab
Reference to the field technician or workforce member who performed the lifecycle event (deployment, swap, repair). Nullable for automated or non-field events.

Foreign key linking to assurance.alarm_event. Business justification: Asset state changes (decommission, replacement, emergency swap) are often triggered by specific alarm events. Linking asset_lifecycle_event to alarm_event supports tracing asset decisions back to the

Foreign key linking to assurance.trouble_ticket. Business justification: When event_type='FAILURE' or 'RETURN', links asset failure events to originating trouble tickets for root cause analysis, warranty claim substantiation, and vendor quality scorecards.

Aggregate throughput capacity across all ports in gigabits per second (Gbps). Used for capacity planning and traffic engineering.
