description: Indicates whether this rate plan applies day-of-week pricing modifiers (e.g., weekend discount rates). When TRUE, the rating engine evaluates the CDR date against the configured weekend/weekday windows.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 — DayOfWeekPricingCondition; Amdocs Revenue Management Rating Configuration

description: Fixed monetary penalty charged to a subscriber who cancels the service before the contract_term_months expires. Denominated in currency_code. A value of 0 indicates no early termination fee. Used in churn management and collections workflows.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 — PenaltyCharge; FCC Consumer Protection Rules 47 CFR Part 64; Ofcom General Conditions

description: The calendar date after which this rate plan is no longer valid for rating new events. Exclusive boundary. NULL indicates an open-ended plan with no scheduled termination. Used for plan deprecation and migration workflows.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 — TimePeriod; IFRS 15 — Revenue from Contracts with Customers

description: The calendar date from which this rate plan becomes active and eligible for rating new subscriptions and usage events. Inclusive boundary. Used for temporal validity checks in the rating engine and revenue recognition.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 — TimePeriod; IFRS 15 — Revenue from Contracts with Customers

description: Maximum data volume in megabytes beyond which the fair-use policy is triggered, resulting in speed throttling or additional charges. Distinct from the included_data_volume_mb allowance — this is the absolute ceiling before network management actions are applied. NULL indicates no fair-use cap.
reference: BEREC Net Neutrality Guidelines BoR(20)112; FCC Open Internet Order; TM Forum SID GB922 — PolicyCondition

description: The General Ledger account code in SAP S/4HANA to which revenue from this rate plan is posted. Enables financial reporting, EBITDA analysis, and MRR/ARPU reconciliation at the plan level.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Chart of Accounts; IFRS 15; TM Forum SID GB922 — FinancialAccount

description: Data volume allowance included in the base recurring charge, expressed in megabytes (MB). A value of -1 or NULL indicates unlimited data. The rating engine deducts consumed data against this allowance before applying overage rates or fair-use policy throttling.
reference: 3GPP TS 32.251 — Packet Switched Charging; TM Forum SID GB922 — UsageVolumeProduct

description: Number of SMS messages included in the base recurring charge before overage rates apply. A value of -1 or NULL indicates unlimited SMS. Used by the rating engine for SMS CDR allowance tracking.
reference: 3GPP TS 32.240 — Charging Architecture; TM Forum SID GB922 — UsageVolumeProduct

description: Number of voice call minutes included in the rate plan's base recurring charge before overage rates apply. A value of -1 or NULL indicates unlimited voice. Used by the rating engine to track allowance consumption against CDRs.
reference: 3GPP TS 32.240 — Charging Architecture; TM Forum SID GB922 — UsageVolumeProduct

description: Indicates whether this rate plan is a promotional or limited-time offer. When TRUE, the plan may have an associated promotion expiry date and is tracked separately in ARPU and MRR reporting to distinguish promotional revenue from base revenue.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 — PromotionSpecification; Amdocs Revenue Management Promotion Configuration

description: The timestamp when this rate plan record was last updated in the source system or Silver layer. Used for incremental ETL processing, change detection, and audit compliance.
reference: ISO/IEC 27001 — Information Security Management; TM Forum SID GB922 — AuditTrail

description: Multiplier applied to the base rate during off-peak hours when time_of_day_modifier_enabled is TRUE. A value of 0.5 means 50% discount during off-peak. NULL when time-of-day pricing is not enabled.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 — TimeOfDayPricingCondition; Amdocs Revenue Management Rating Configuration

description: The per-unit charge applied to usage that exceeds the included allowance. The unit of measure is defined by overage_unit_of_measure. For data, this is typically per MB or per GB; for voice, per minute; for SMS, per message. Denominated in currency_code.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 — UsageChargeSpecification; Amdocs Revenue Management Rating Configuration

description: The unit of measure against which the overage_rate_per_unit is applied. Defines the granularity of overage billing (e.g., per MB for data, per minute for voice, per message for SMS). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: MB|GB|minute|second|message|event|kB — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 — UnitOfMeasure; 3GPP TS 32.240 — Charging Architecture

description: The daily end time (HH:MM, 24-hour format) of the peak pricing window. Used by the rating engine to determine whether a CDR falls within peak or off-peak hours. NULL when time-of-day pricing is not enabled.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 — TimeOfDayPricingCondition; Amdocs Revenue Management Rating Configuration

description: Multiplier applied to the base rate during peak hours when time_of_day_modifier_enabled is TRUE. A value of 1.0 means no adjustment; 1.5 means 50% premium during peak. NULL when time-of-day pricing is not enabled.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 — TimeOfDayPricingCondition; Amdocs Revenue Management Rating Configuration

description: The daily start time (HH:MM, 24-hour format) of the peak pricing window. Used by the rating engine to determine whether a CDR falls within peak or off-peak hours. NULL when time-of-day pricing is not enabled.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 — TimeOfDayPricingCondition; Amdocs Revenue Management Rating Configuration

description: Current lifecycle state of the rate plan within the Amdocs Rating engine. Controls whether the plan is available for new subscriptions and active rating. 'draft' = under configuration; 'active' = live for rating; 'suspended' = temporarily halted; 'deprecated' = replaced by a newer plan; 'inactive' = decommissioned.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 — LifecycleStatus; Amdocs Revenue Management Rating Configuration

description: Billing modality of the rate plan. 'postpaid' = charges billed after consumption cycle; 'prepaid' = charges deducted from pre-loaded balance via Comverse ONE real-time rating; 'hybrid' = mixed prepaid/postpaid; 'wholesale' = inter-operator or MVNO wholesale pricing; 'mvno' = Mobile Virtual Network Operator resale pricing.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 — BillingArrangement; GSMA PRD IR.21

description: The date on which the promotional pricing expires and the standard rate plan pricing takes effect. Applicable only when is_promotional is TRUE. NULL for non-promotional plans.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 — PromotionSpecification; Amdocs Revenue Management Promotion Configuration

description: Method used to calculate partial-period charges when a subscription starts or ends mid-billing-cycle. 'daily' = charge proportional to days active; 'full_period' = charge full cycle regardless; 'none' = no proration applied.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 — BillingCycleSpecification; Amdocs Revenue Management Billing Configuration; IFRS 15

description: Externally-known alphanumeric code that uniquely identifies the rate plan within the Amdocs Rating engine and is referenced by downstream billing, CDR processing, and invoice generation systems. Used as the business key for cross-system reconciliation.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 — PricingLogicAlgorithm; Amdocs Revenue Management Rating Configuration

description: Detailed narrative description of the rate plan's pricing logic, applicable services, and commercial intent. Used by product managers, billing analysts, and regulatory reporting teams to understand the plan's scope.

Human-readable name of the rate plan as displayed in the Amdocs Rating engine configuration and referenced in billing statements and internal reporting (e.g., 'Unlimited 5G Consumer Postpaid', 'SMB Data Tier 3').

Number of pricing tiers (step thresholds) defined for this rate plan. A value of 1 indicates flat-rate pricing; values greater than 1 indicate tiered/stepped pricing where different rates apply at different usage volumes. Drives the rating engine's tier evaluation logic.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 — PricingLogicAlgorithm; Amdocs Revenue Management Rating Configuration

description: Indicates whether usage events (CDRs) under this rate plan are rated in real-time (online charging via Comverse ONE for prepaid) or in batch mode (offline charging via Amdocs for postpaid), or a hybrid of both.
reference: 3GPP TS 32.240 — Charging Architecture; TM Forum SID GB922 — RatingFunction; Comverse ONE Real-Time Rating

description: The fixed periodic charge applied to a subscription on this rate plan per billing cycle (e.g., monthly base fee). Represents the Monthly Recurring Revenue (MRR) component at the plan level. Denominated in the currency specified by currency_code.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 — RecurringCharge; Amdocs Revenue Management Billing Configuration

description: Code that maps this rate plan to the appropriate revenue recognition treatment in SAP S/4HANA Finance. Determines how recurring and one-time charges are recognized over the contract term per IFRS 15 / ASC 606 performance obligation rules.
reference: IFRS 15 — Revenue from Contracts with Customers; ASC 606; SAP S/4HANA Revenue Accounting and Reporting (RAR)

description: Per-megabyte charge applied to data usage while roaming internationally, denominated in currency_code. Applies when roaming_enabled is TRUE and the subscriber is on a visited network. Used for IOT (Inter-Operator Tariff) reconciliation.
reference: GSMA PRD IR.21; GSMA TAP3 — IOT; EU Roaming Regulation 2022/612; TM Forum SID GB922 — RoamingCharge

description: Indicates whether international roaming usage is permitted and rated under this rate plan. When FALSE, roaming CDRs are blocked or rated at a default barring rate. Relevant for NRTRDE/TAP roaming data exchange compliance.
reference: GSMA PRD IR.21 — Roaming Database; GSMA TAP3 — Transferred Account Procedure; 3GPP TS 22.011

description: Per-minute charge applied to voice calls while roaming internationally, denominated in currency_code. Applies when roaming_enabled is TRUE. Used for IOT reconciliation and NRTRDE reporting.
reference: GSMA PRD IR.21; GSMA TAP3 — IOT; EU Roaming Regulation 2022/612; TM Forum SID GB922 — RoamingCharge

description: The telecommunications service category to which this rate plan applies. Determines which usage event types (CDRs) are rated against this plan. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: voice|data|sms|roaming|iot|iptv|voip|mms — promote to reference product]
reference: 3GPP TS 32.240 — Charging Architecture; TM Forum SID GB922 — ServiceSpecification

description: Identifier of the operational source system from which this rate plan record was extracted (e.g., 'AMDOCS_RATING', 'COMVERSE_ONE'). Supports data lineage, multi-source reconciliation, and Silver layer provenance tracking.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 — DataSource; ISO/IEC 27001 — Data Provenance

description: Tax classification code assigned to this rate plan, used by the Amdocs tax engine to determine applicable tax rates (e.g., USF, VAT, GST, E911 surcharges). Maps to the tax jurisdiction configuration in the billing system.
reference: FCC Universal Service Fund (USF) Rules 47 CFR Part 54; ITU-T D.150 — Taxation of Telecommunications; ISO 20022 — Tax Information

description: The radio access or fixed-line network technology to which this rate plan applies. Used by the rating engine to apply technology-specific pricing tiers (e.g., 5G NR premium data rates vs LTE standard rates). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 2G|3G|4G_LTE|5G_NR|FTTH|GPON|ADSL|VDSL|WIFI — promote to reference product]
reference: 3GPP TS 22.261 — Service Requirements for 5G; ITU-T G.984 — GPON; ETSI TS 136 series — LTE

description: Per-unit charge applied to usage within the first pricing tier (from 0 to tier_threshold_1_mb). Denominated in currency_code per overage_unit_of_measure.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 — PricingLogicAlgorithm; Amdocs Revenue Management Rating Configuration

description: Per-unit charge applied to usage within the second pricing tier (from tier_threshold_1_mb to tier_threshold_2_mb). Denominated in currency_code per overage_unit_of_measure. NULL if fewer than 2 tiers.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 — PricingLogicAlgorithm; Amdocs Revenue Management Rating Configuration

description: Per-unit charge applied to usage beyond tier_threshold_2_mb (third tier and above). Denominated in currency_code per overage_unit_of_measure. NULL if fewer than 3 tiers.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 — PricingLogicAlgorithm; Amdocs Revenue Management Rating Configuration

description: Usage volume threshold in MB at which the first pricing tier ends and the second tier begins. NULL if the plan has fewer than 2 tiers. Used by the Amdocs rating engine for stepped-rate calculation.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 — PricingLogicAlgorithm; Amdocs Revenue Management Rating Configuration

description: Usage volume threshold in MB at which the second pricing tier ends and the third tier begins. NULL if the plan has fewer than 3 tiers.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 — PricingLogicAlgorithm; Amdocs Revenue Management Rating Configuration

description: Indicates whether this rate plan applies time-of-day pricing modifiers (e.g., peak/off-peak rates). When TRUE, the rating engine evaluates the CDR timestamp against the defined peak/off-peak windows to apply the appropriate rate multiplier.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 — TimeOfDayPricingCondition; Amdocs Revenue Management Rating Configuration

description: Monotonically incrementing version counter for this rate plan record. Incremented each time the plan configuration is modified. Supports audit trail, change management, and point-in-time rating reconstruction for dispute resolution.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 — VersionNumber; Amdocs Revenue Management Configuration Audit

description: Multiplier applied to the base rate on weekend days (Saturday and Sunday) when day_of_week_modifier_enabled is TRUE. A value of 0.0 means free weekend calls/data. NULL when day-of-week pricing is not enabled.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 — DayOfWeekPricingCondition; Amdocs Revenue Management Rating Configuration

description: Unique surrogate primary key for each rated usage event record in the billing silver layer. Generated by the Amdocs Rating engine or Comverse ONE real-time rating pipeline upon successful rating of a raw CDR, IPDR, or partner usage feed.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 - Billing Domain

description: Foreign key linking to product.addon. Business justification: Usage events may consume addon allowances (data packs, international calling, roaming). Required for accurate addon usage tracking, quota enforcement, overage billing, and addon product performance an

Reference to the billing account under which this rated event is charged. Links the usage event to the customer financial account for invoice aggregation and ARPU/MRR reporting in Amdocs Revenue Management.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 - Customer Account; Amdocs Revenue Management

description: Foreign key linking to interconnect.carrier_agreement. Business justification: Roaming revenue assurance and inter-operator cost attribution require directly linking rated roaming events to the governing carrier agreement. Analysts reconcile retail charges against wholesale agre

Foreign key linking to product.catalog_item. Business justification: Rated usage events must link to the catalog item whose allowances and rating rules apply. Essential for usage analytics by product, fair use policy enforcement, and product design optimization.

Reference to the originating raw CDR, IPDR, or partner usage feed record from which this rated event was produced. Bridges the CDR domain to the billing/financial domain and enables traceability back to the network event.
reference: 3GPP TS 32.297 - CDR File Format; TM Forum SID GB922

description: The billing period (formatted as YYYY-MM) to which this rated event is allocated for invoicing. Determines which invoice cycle aggregates this charge. Used for MRR reporting, revenue recognition, and billing cycle reconciliation in Amdocs.
reference: Amdocs Revenue Management Billing Cycle; TM Forum SID GB922; IFRS 15

description: Foreign key linking to inventory.cpe_asset. Business justification: Broadband usage rated events originate from a specific CPE device. Linking rated_event to cpe_asset enables per-device usage analytics, CPE-level fraud detection, and billing validation reports that c
Reference to the invoice on which this rated event charge appears. Populated after invoice generation; null for events not yet invoiced or for prepaid events settled via balance deduction.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 - Invoice; Amdocs Revenue Management Invoicing

description: Foreign key linking to compliance.lawful_intercept_order. Business justification: Rated events (CDRs) are delivered to law enforcement under lawful intercept orders. Real business process: LEA requests require CDR extraction with chain of custody tracking. Compliance teams link del

Foreign key linking to enterprise.managed_service. Business justification: Usage events (data consumption, API calls, voice minutes) must link to managed service instances for capacity planning, SLA performance measurement, usage-based billing validation, and service-specifi

Foreign key linking to inventory.msisdn_range. Business justification: Each rated_event carries an MSISDN that belongs to a specific inventory MSISDN range. This link enables range-level usage aggregation, regulatory number utilization reporting, and roaming settlement r

Foreign key linking to product.offering. Business justification: Offer-level usage analytics and revenue assurance require rated events to reference the commercial offering under which usage was rated. Telecom revenue assurance teams correlate rated events to offer

Foreign key linking to inventory.ont_asset. Business justification: Fiber/GPON usage rated events are generated at the ONT device level. Linking rated_event to ont_asset enables per-ONT usage billing validation, capacity planning reports, and SLA breach detection for

Reference to the original rated event record that this record supersedes or corrects. Populated for re-rated or reversed events to maintain the audit chain. Enables before/after comparison for revenue assurance and dispute resolution.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 - Billing Event Correction; Amdocs Revenue Management; ISO/IEC 27001

description: Foreign key linking to partner.partner. Business justification: Roaming events and wholesale usage must track originating/terminating partner network. Required for roaming event settlement, wholesale usage billing, inter-operator charging, and TAP/RAP file reconci

Foreign key linking to billing.prepaid_balance. Business justification: A rated_event for prepaid subscribers directly consumes from a prepaid_balance. Linking rated_event to the prepaid_balance it debited enables real-time balance deduction traceability and prepaid usage

The network cell or base station identifier (e.g., eNodeB/gNB cell ID) where the subscriber was served during this event. Used for network-level usage analytics, RAN capacity planning, and geographic revenue reporting. Sourced from CDR network element data.
reference: 3GPP TS 32.297; 3GPP TS 38.401 (5G NR gNB); Ericsson Network Manager RAN

description: Foreign key linking to interconnect.rate. Business justification: The rating engine applies a specific interconnect rate to produce each roaming rated_event charge. Linking rated_event to the exact interconnect.rate record used enables roaming cost reconciliation, r
Reference to the rate plan applied during rating of this event. Determines the pricing tiers, bundle allowances, and charging rules used to compute the charge amount.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 - Product Offering Price; Amdocs Revenue Management Rating

description: Foreign key linking to inventory.sim_stock. Business justification: Usage events (calls, SMS, data sessions) are generated by specific SIM cards. Rating engines need SIM-to-event linkage for fraud detection, roaming reconciliation, and SIM swap validation. Critical fo

Foreign key linking to network.slice. Business justification: 5G slice-differentiated charging: rated events for services delivered over a specific network slice must reference that slice for slice-level usage analytics, per-slice revenue reporting, and charging

Foreign key linking to customer.subscriber. Business justification: Rated events must be traceable to a subscriber for usage dispute resolution, regulatory CDR audits, and roaming reconciliation. rated_event carries msisdn/imsi as plain strings but lacks a proper FK;

Reference to the active subscriber service subscription (rate plan contract) under which this usage event is rated. Determines applicable rate plan, bundles, and allowances in Amdocs and Comverse ONE.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 - Service Subscription; 3GPP TS 32.240

description: Foreign key linking to interconnect.tap_record. Business justification: Roaming CDRs rated in billing must reference source TAP records for wholesale settlement reconciliation, dispute resolution, and revenue assurance audits—critical for matching retail charges to wholes

The APN used by the subscriber for data sessions, identifying the packet data network gateway and service context (e.g., internet, enterprise VPN, IMS). Used for data service classification, QoS policy application, and DPI-based rating.
reference: 3GPP TS 23.003 APN; 3GPP TS 32.251 PS Charging; Amdocs Revenue Management

description: Indicates whether this rated event was charged against an included bundle allowance (True) or rated as an out-of-bundle/overage charge (False). Used to track bundle utilization, identify overage revenue, and support customer usage analytics.
reference: Amdocs Revenue Management Bundle Management; TM Forum SID GB922; Comverse ONE Balance Management

description: The destination number (B-party) dialed or messaged by the subscriber for voice calls and SMS events. Used for inter-operator settlement, fraud analysis, and regulatory lawful intercept compliance. Classified as confidential PII.
reference: 3GPP TS 32.250; ITU-T E.164; CALEA; ETSI LI Standards

description: The gross charge amount applied to this rated event before discounts and taxes, in the billing currency. Computed by the rating engine based on the applicable rate plan tier and rated volume/duration. Core financial field for revenue recognition and invoice line generation.
reference: Amdocs Revenue Management Rating; TM Forum SID GB922; IFRS 15; ITU-T D.150

description: Classifies the nature of the charge associated with this rated event. 'usage' = consumption-based charge; 'recurring' = periodic subscription charge; 'one_time' = activation/setup fee; 'adjustment' = billing correction; 'reversal' = credit/reversal of a prior charge. Used for revenue categorization and GL posting.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 - Applied Customer Billing Rate; Amdocs Revenue Management; IFRS 15

description: The date and time when this rated event record was first persisted in the Databricks silver layer. Used for data lineage, audit trail, and pipeline monitoring. Distinct from rating_timestamp (when rated) and event_start_timestamp (when the event occurred).
reference: TM Forum SID GB922; ISO/IEC 27001 Audit Trail

description: The ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the charge, discount, and tax amounts are expressed (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Essential for multi-currency billing environments and inter-operator settlement.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; ITU-T D.150; GSMA TAP3 IOT

description: The total discount amount applied to this rated event (promotional, loyalty, bundle, or contractual discounts) in the billing currency. Reduces the gross charge to arrive at the net charge. Used for discount analytics and revenue assurance.
reference: Amdocs Revenue Management Rating; TM Forum SID GB922; IFRS 15

description: The reference number of the billing dispute or inter-operator dispute case associated with this rated event, if applicable. Populated when rating_status = 'disputed'. Links to the dispute management workflow in Amdocs or ServiceNow ITSM for resolution tracking.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 - Billing Dispute; Amdocs Revenue Management; GSMA TAP3 Dispute

description: Indicates the origination direction of the usage event. MO (Mobile Originated) = subscriber initiated the call/session; MT (Mobile Terminated) = subscriber received the call/session. Relevant for voice and SMS rating and inter-operator settlement.
reference: 3GPP TS 32.250 CS Domain Charging; GSMA TAP3

description: The date and time when the usage event concluded (e.g., call release time, data session termination). Used to compute event duration and for billing cycle boundary determination.

Externally-known business identifier assigned by the rating engine or source OSS/BSS system to uniquely identify this rated event within a billing cycle or batch. Used for reconciliation, dispute resolution, and inter-operator settlement references.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922; GSMA NRTRDE; TAP3 IOT

description: The date and time when the usage event began (e.g., call answer time, data session start, SMS submission time). This is the principal real-world event time used for billing cycle allocation, time-of-day rating, and CDR correlation.
reference: 3GPP TS 32.297; TM Forum SID GB922; ITU-T Q.825

description: Classification of the usage event by service type. Drives rating logic selection and reporting segmentation. Examples: voice (MO/MT calls), SMS, data (packet sessions), roaming (visited network), content (OTT/IPTV), IoT (machine-to-machine). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: voice|sms|data|roaming|content|iot|mms|video — promote to reference product]
reference: 3GPP TS 32.240 Charging Architecture; TM Forum SID GB922

business_glossary_term: Home Public Land Mobile Network (Home PLMN)
description: The MCC+MNC code identifying the subscriber's home network operator (HPLMN). Used to determine domestic vs. roaming status and to apply correct inter-operator tariffs (IOT) for roaming settlement via GSMA TAP/NRTRDE.
reference: 3GPP TS 23.003; ITU-T E.212; GSMA TAP3; GSMA NRTRDE

description: The IMEI of the device used during this usage event. Used for device-level analytics, fraud detection (stolen device usage), and regulatory reporting. Classified as confidential device identifier.
reference: 3GPP TS 23.003; GSMA IMEI Database; FCC Regulations

description: The IMSI uniquely identifying the SIM/subscriber on the network. Used for roaming settlement, fraud detection, and subscriber authentication correlation. Classified as restricted PII as it uniquely identifies a subscriber.

The inter-operator tariff (IOT) charge amount payable to or receivable from the visited network operator for roaming events, in the settlement currency. Populated only for roaming events (is_roaming = true). Used for GSMA TAP/NRTRDE wholesale settlement and interconnect accounting.
reference: GSMA TAP3; GSMA NRTRDE; ITU-T D.150 IOT; TM Forum SID GB922 Interconnect

description: Flag indicating whether this usage event occurred while the subscriber was roaming on a visited network (VPLMN). True = roaming event subject to inter-operator settlement; False = domestic event. Drives roaming-specific rating rules and GSMA TAP/NRTRDE processing.

business_glossary_term: Mobile Station International Subscriber Directory Number (MSISDN)
description: The subscriber's MSISDN (phone number in E.164 format) associated with this usage event. Primary subscriber identifier for rating, billing, and inter-operator settlement. Classified as restricted PII as it directly identifies an individual subscriber.
reference: ITU-T E.164; 3GPP TS 23.003; GDPR Article 4; CCPA

description: The final net charge amount for this rated event after applying discounts and taxes (charge_amount - discount_amount + tax_amount) in the billing currency. This is the amount posted to the customer invoice and used for revenue recognition and ARPU calculation.
reference: Amdocs Revenue Management; TM Forum SID GB922; IFRS 15; ITU-T D.150

description: The radio access or network technology used to deliver the service for this event (e.g., 4G LTE, 5G NR, VoLTE, FTTH/GPON). Used for technology-based revenue analytics, network capacity planning, and QoS reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 2G|3G|4G_LTE|5G_NR|VoLTE|VoIP|WiFi|FTTH|unknown — promote to reference product]
reference: 3GPP TS 23.501 (5G NR); 3GPP TS 36.300 (LTE); ITU-T G.984 (GPON); ETSI

description: The billable duration of the usage event in seconds as determined by the rating engine after applying rounding rules (e.g., per-second, per-minute rounding). Applicable for voice and video call events. May differ from raw CDR duration due to rating rounding rules.
reference: 3GPP TS 32.250; Amdocs Revenue Management Rating; TM Forum SID GB922

description: The number of billable units consumed in this event as determined by the rating engine. For SMS: number of message segments; for content: number of items; for IoT: number of transactions. Complements rated_duration_seconds and rated_volume_bytes for non-time/non-data event types.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922; Amdocs Revenue Management Rating; Comverse ONE

description: The billable data volume in bytes as determined by the rating engine after applying any volume rounding or threshold rules. Applicable for data, roaming data, and IoT events. Used for bundle consumption tracking and overage charge calculation.
reference: 3GPP TS 32.251 PS Domain Charging; Amdocs Revenue Management; TM Forum SID GB922

description: Identifies the rating system that processed and priced this event. 'amdocs' = Amdocs Revenue Management batch/real-time rating; 'comverse_one' = Comverse ONE prepaid real-time rating; 'partner_feed' = rated by a partner/MVNO; 'manual' = manually rated adjustment. Used for data lineage and revenue assurance.
reference: Amdocs Revenue Management; Comverse ONE; TM Forum SID GB922

description: Indicates whether this event was rated in real-time (online charging, e.g., Comverse ONE prepaid), batch mode (Amdocs post-processing), or offline (delayed/manual). Affects revenue recognition timing and prepaid balance deduction sequencing.
reference: 3GPP TS 32.240 Online/Offline Charging; Amdocs Revenue Management; Comverse ONE

description: Current lifecycle state of the rated event within the billing workflow. 'rated' = successfully priced; 're_rated' = repriced after correction; 'disputed' = under customer or inter-operator dispute; 'written_off' = revenue written off; 'pending' = awaiting rating completion; 'rejected' = failed rating validation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: rated|re_rated|disputed|written_off|pending|rejected — promote to reference product]
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 - Billing Event State; Amdocs Revenue Management

description: The specific pricing tier or band within the rate plan applied to this event (e.g., TIER_1_BUNDLE, TIER_2_OVERAGE, PEAK, OFF_PEAK, ROAM_ZONE_A). Indicates which step of a tiered or time-of-day pricing structure was used. Critical for revenue assurance and rate plan analytics.
reference: Amdocs Revenue Management Rating Tiers; TM Forum SID GB922 - Product Offering Price

description: The date and time when the rating engine (Amdocs or Comverse ONE) processed and priced this usage event. Distinct from the event start/end timestamps. Used for rating pipeline SLA monitoring, re-rating audit trails, and revenue recognition timing.
reference: Amdocs Revenue Management Rating Engine; TM Forum SID GB922; IFRS 15 Revenue Recognition

description: Free-text or coded reason explaining why this event was re-rated (e.g., rate plan correction, tariff update, CDR amendment, dispute resolution). Populated only when rating_status = 're_rated'. Essential for revenue assurance audit trails.
reference: Amdocs Revenue Management Re-Rating; TM Forum SID GB922; Revenue Assurance Best Practices

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the country where the roaming event occurred. Populated only when is_roaming = true. Used for roaming revenue reporting, IOT application, and regulatory compliance with visited country regulations.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; GSMA TAP3; ITU-T E.212

description: The specific service or product code from the Netcracker Service Catalog that this usage event is associated with (e.g., VOICE_DOMESTIC, DATA_4G, SMS_INTL, ROAM_DATA_EU). Used to apply service-specific rating rules and bundle consumption tracking.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 - Service Specification; Netcracker OSS/BSS Service Catalog

business_glossary_term: Serving Public Land Mobile Network (Serving PLMN / Visited PLMN)
description: The MCC+MNC code of the network that served the subscriber during this event. For domestic events equals the home PLMN; for roaming events identifies the visited network (VPLMN). Critical for inter-operator settlement and roaming IOT application.
reference: 3GPP TS 23.003; ITU-T E.212; GSMA TAP3; GSMA NRTRDE

description: The GSMA TAP3 file identifier referencing the inter-operator roaming data exchange file from which this roaming rated event was sourced. Used for roaming settlement reconciliation, dispute management, and NRTRDE compliance tracking.
reference: GSMA TAP3 Specification; GSMA NRTRDE; ITU-T D.150

description: The tax amount levied on this rated event in the billing currency (e.g., VAT, GST, USF, E911 surcharges). Computed based on applicable tax jurisdiction rules. Required for tax reporting, regulatory compliance, and invoice accuracy.

Maximum acceptable round-trip latency in milliseconds as defined in the SLA. Typically measured between hub and spoke sites or between critical site pairs.

business_glossary_term: Service Level Agreement (SLA) Packet Loss Target Percentage
description: Maximum acceptable packet loss percentage as defined in the SLA. Typical enterprise SLAs guarantee less than 0.1% to 1% packet loss.
Number of spoke sites (branch offices, remote locations) in hub-and-spoke or hybrid topologies. Spoke sites typically connect to hub sites for centralized services and internet breakout.

Logical design pattern of the SD-WAN topology. Hub-and-spoke centralizes traffic through hub sites; full-mesh provides direct connectivity between all sites; partial-mesh selectively connects high-traffic site pairs; point-to-point connects two sites; hybrid combines multiple patterns.
External business identifier or code for this topology, used in customer-facing documentation, service orders, and billing. May be customer-provided or system-generated.
Business-friendly name for this SD-WAN topology configuration, typically reflecting the customer organization or deployment scope (e.g., 'Global HQ Mesh', 'Regional Hub-Spoke').

Current operational status of this SD-WAN topology. Active indicates fully operational; provisioning indicates deployment in progress; suspended indicates temporary service hold; decommissioning indicates teardown in progress.

Policy governing how the SD-WAN controller steers traffic across available underlay paths. Application-aware uses deep packet inspection to route by application; latency/bandwidth/cost-based optimize for specific metrics; manual uses static rules.
Comma-separated list of physical transport technologies used as underlay for this SD-WAN overlay (e.g., 'MPLS,Broadband,LTE'). Underlay diversity enables path redundancy and failover.
Indicates whether URL filtering (web content filtering) is enabled to block access to malicious, inappropriate, or non-business websites based on category and reputation.

business_glossary_term: Service Level Agreement (SLA) Breach End Timestamp

business_glossary_term: Service Level Agreement (SLA) Breach Start Timestamp

Boolean flag indicating whether the enterprise customer has disputed the SLA breach determination, credit calculation, or root cause analysis. Triggers dispute resolution workflow.

Boolean flag indicating whether the SLA breach requires escalation to senior management or executive account team based on severity, customer tier, or repeat occurrence.

Timestamp when the SLA breach event record was last modified. Tracks updates to status, credit issuance, customer acknowledgment, and resolution details.
Description of preventive actions implemented to avoid recurrence of similar SLA breaches. Used for continuous service improvement and customer communication.

description: Count of previous breaches of the same SLA metric for this service instance within the current measurement period. Used for trend analysis and contract review triggers.

business_glossary_term: Repeat Service Level Agreement (SLA) Breach Flag
description: Boolean flag indicating whether this is a repeat breach of the same SLA metric for the same service instance within a defined time window. Triggers enhanced escalation and remediation.
Reference number for the resolution action or change request that addressed the root cause of the SLA breach. Links to change management or problem management records.
Timestamp when the underlying service issue was resolved and normal SLA performance was restored. Used to calculate total impact duration.

description: Root cause category for the SLA breach as determined by network operations and engineering analysis. Used for trend analysis and preventive action planning. description: Detailed narrative description of the root cause analysis findings. Provides context for the breach and remediation actions taken.

description: Unique identifier for the SLA measurement record. Primary key for the enterprise SLA measurement entity.

Reference to the specific enterprise managed service being measured (SD-WAN circuit, MPLS connection, unified communications service, IoT solution, etc.).
The total duration in minutes that the service was in breach of the SLA target during the measurement period. Null if no breach occurred. Used to calculate service credits and assess severity.
Boolean indicator of whether the measured value breached (failed to meet) the target value for this measurement period. True indicates a breach occurred; False indicates target was met or exceeded.
Classification of the breach severity based on the magnitude of deviation from target and business impact. Severity levels typically determine credit entitlement amounts and escalation procedures.

The monetary credit amount (in contract currency) that the enterprise customer is entitled to receive as compensation for this SLA breach, calculated according to the credit schedule defined in the SLA contract.
Indicates whether the enterprise customer was notified of this SLA measurement result, particularly for breach events. True if notification was sent; False otherwise.
The date and time when the customer was notified of this SLA measurement result. Null if no notification was sent. Used for compliance with notification SLAs.

A quality score (0-100) indicating the reliability and completeness of the measurement data. Lower scores may indicate gaps in monitoring coverage, probe failures, or data collection issues that affect measurement accuracy.

Indicates whether the enterprise customer has disputed this SLA measurement result. True if disputed; False otherwise. Disputed measurements may require investigation and resolution before credit processing.
Current status of the dispute resolution process for this measurement. Null if no dispute was raised. Tracks the lifecycle of customer challenges to SLA measurement accuracy.

Indicates whether any contractual exclusions were applied to this measurement (e.g., planned maintenance windows, force majeure events, customer-caused outages). True if exclusions were applied; False otherwise.
Detailed explanation of why an exclusion was applied to this measurement, referencing the specific SLA contract clause. Null if no exclusion was applied.

The method used to capture the SLA metric. Options include automated network probes, synthetic transactions, passive traffic monitoring, customer-reported incidents, or manual calculation from operational data.
The end date and time of the measurement period for this SLA metric observation. Defines the close of the window during which performance was measured.
The start date and time of the measurement period for this SLA metric observation. Defines the beginning of the window during which performance was measured.

The operational system or network monitoring platform that captured this SLA measurement (e.g., Nokia NetAct, Ericsson Network Manager, ServiceNow, third-party monitoring tool). Provides data lineage and audit trail.

Current lifecycle status of this SLA measurement record. Draft measurements are preliminary; validated measurements have passed quality checks; approved measurements are ready for reporting; disputed measurements are under review; finalized measurements are locked for billing and reporting.
Free-text notes providing additional context about this measurement, such as unusual circumstances, data quality issues, or operational events that affected the measurement.
The business reporting period to which this measurement belongs (e.g., 2024-Q1, 2024-03, Week-12-2024). Used for aggregating measurements into periodic SLA reports and dashboards.
The contractually agreed target value for this SLA metric as defined in the SLA contract. Performance is evaluated by comparing measured_value against this target.

The unit of measure for both measured_value and target_value. The user ID or name of the operations staff member who validated this SLA measurement. Null for automated validations. Provides accountability and audit trail.
The date and time when this SLA measurement was validated and approved for reporting. Null if not yet validated.

description: Primary key for the topology_site_membership association

description: Foreign key linking to the SD-WAN topology that this site is enrolled in.

business_glossary_term: Topology Site Membership - Enterprise Site Id
description: Foreign key linking to the enterprise site that is a member of this topology.

Date when this specific site was activated and enrolled into this specific topology. Distinct from enterprise_site.activation_date (when the site became operational) and sdwan_topology.activation_date (when the topology went live). Tracks when the site-topology membership became operational.

The bandwidth slice (in Mbps) allocated to this site within this topology. This is the provisioned capacity for this site's participation in the topology — distinct from enterprise_site.bandwidth_requirement_mbps (the site's total requirement) and is governed by the topology's overall capacity plan.

Total number of CPE devices (physical or virtual) deployed across all sites in this topology. Each site typically has one or more CPE devices for SD-WAN edge functions. [Moved from sdwan_topology: This is a derived aggregate count of CPE devices across all enrolled sites. It should be computed from topology_site_membership joined to enterprise_site CPE inventory, not stored as a denormalized count on the topology. Removing it eliminates a maintenance burden when sites are added or removed.]

Date when this site's membership in this topology was or will be terminated. Enables historical tracking of which sites were enrolled in which topologies over time, supporting audit, billing reconciliation, and migration history.

Number of hub sites in hub-and-spoke or hybrid topologies. Hub sites typically host centralized services (data centers, cloud on-ramps, security stacks) and aggregate traffic from spoke sites. [Moved from sdwan_topology: This is a derived aggregate count of hub-role memberships in this topology. It should be computed from topology_site_membership records where site_role = HUB, not stored as a denormalized count on the topology. Storing it on sdwan_topology creates a data integrity risk when memberships change.]

Current lifecycle status of this site's enrollment in the topology. Tracks whether the site is actively participating, pending provisioning, suspended, or being migrated to a new topology. Enables phased migration tracking where a site transitions between topologies.

Site-specific QoS profile that overrides the topology-level QoS configuration for this site. Allows individual sites to have differentiated traffic prioritization within the same topology. Belongs to the membership record because it is specific to this site's participation in this topology, not to the site or topology independently.

The functional role this site plays within the SD-WAN topology. Hub sites aggregate traffic from spoke sites; spoke sites are branch endpoints; edge sites have direct internet breakout. This role belongs to the membership, not the site or topology alone — the same site can be a hub in one topology and a spoke in another.
Unique identifier for the Unified Communications subscription record. Primary key.

description: The specific regulatory rule, mandate, or case reference code applicable to this fulfillment order when is_regulatory_order is True. Examples: FCC MNP case number, Ofcom compliance reference. Used for regulatory audit trails and compliance reporting.

description: The type of telecommunications service being provisioned or modified by this fulfillment order. Drives workflow template selection, resource inventory allocation, and regulatory reporting categorization. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: MOBILE|BROADBAND|FIBER|IPTV|VOIP|FIXED_LINE|IOT|ENTERPRISE_WAN — promote to reference product]

business_glossary_term: Service Level Agreement (SLA) Breach Threshold Hours
description: The number of hours elapsed from SLA measurement start at which the SLA is formally classified as BREACHED. Typically equals sla_target_hours but may differ for grace-period configurations. Triggers regulatory reporting obligations and penalty calculations.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 SLA Management; FCC Telecommunications Regulations; Ofcom Service Quality Guidelines

description: The precise date and time when the fulfillment order SLA was formally breached. Null for orders that have not breached. Used for regulatory reporting, penalty calculation, and SLA performance analytics. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 SLA Management; FCC Telecommunications Regulations; Ofcom Service Quality Guidelines

business_glossary_term: Service Level Agreement (SLA) Measurement End Event
description: The business event that stops the SLA measurement clock for this fulfillment order. Defines what constitutes successful completion for SLA purposes — e.g., SERVICE_ACTIVATED for mobile activations, CPE_INSTALLED for FTTH orders.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 SLA Management; FCC Service Provisioning Rules; BEREC Quality of Service Guidelines

business_glossary_term: Service Level Agreement (SLA) Measurement Start Event
description: The business event that triggers the start of SLA measurement clock for this fulfillment order. Varies by order type and SLA type — regulatory MNP orders typically start at ORDER_ACCEPTED, while commercial orders may start at PAYMENT_CONFIRMED.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 SLA Management; FCC MNP Porting Rules; GSMA MNP Guidelines

business_glossary_term: Service Level Agreement (SLA) Measurement Start Timestamp
description: The precise date and time when the SLA measurement clock started for this fulfillment order, corresponding to the sla_measurement_start_event. Used as the reference point for all SLA elapsed-time calculations and regulatory compliance measurement. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 SLA Management; FCC MNP Porting Rules

description: Current SLA compliance status of the fulfillment order based on elapsed time versus SLA thresholds. ON_TRACK: within warning threshold. AT_RISK: between warning and breach thresholds. BREACHED: exceeded breach threshold. Primary field for jeopardy detection and regulatory compliance dashboards.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 SLA Management; TM Forum eTOM Jeopardy Management; FCC Telecommunications Regulations

business_glossary_term: Service Level Agreement (SLA) Target Completion Hours
description: The maximum number of hours within which the fulfillment order must be completed to meet the SLA commitment. Measured from the SLA measurement start event. Used for jeopardy detection and regulatory compliance reporting.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 SLA Management; FCC Service Provisioning Rules; BEREC Quality of Service Guidelines

description: Classification of the SLA commitment governing this fulfillment order. REGULATORY: mandated by FCC/Ofcom/BEREC regulations (e.g., MNP porting windows). COMMERCIAL: standard commercial SLA per product tier. ENTERPRISE: premium SLA for enterprise/B2B customers.
reference: FCC Telecommunications Regulations; Ofcom Service Quality Guidelines; BEREC Quality of Service; TM Forum SID GB922 SLA Management

business_glossary_term: Service Level Agreement (SLA) Warning Threshold Hours
description: The number of hours elapsed from SLA measurement start at which an AT_RISK warning is triggered for proactive jeopardy management. Must be less than sla_target_hours. Drives automated alerting in NOC and order management dashboards.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 SLA Management; TM Forum eTOM Jeopardy Management

description: The precise date and time when the fulfillment order was submitted by the customer or agent into the order management system. This is the principal business event timestamp and the anchor point for SLA measurement start calculations. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 Order Management; Oracle Communications OSM

description: The name of the OSM workflow template (order specification) used to orchestrate this fulfillment order. Determines the decomposition strategy, task sequence, and system interactions. Examples: 'MOBILE_NEW_ACTIVATION_V3', 'FTTH_PROVISIONING_V2'.
reference: Oracle Communications Order and Service Management (OSM); TM Forum SID GB922 Order Management

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each order line item within the fulfillment order management system. Primary key for the order_line entity. Role: TRANSACTION_LINE.

description: The sales or service channel through which this order line was initiated (e.g., ONLINE web portal, RETAIL_STORE, CALL_CENTER, DEALER partner, SELF_SERVICE app, B2B_PORTAL). Used for channel performance analytics, commission calculation, and order routing.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 Sales; eTOM 2.3.1

description: The minimum contract commitment period in months associated with the product on this order line (e.g., 0 for month-to-month, 12, 24, 36). Drives early termination fee (ETF) calculation and churn risk assessment in Amdocs Revenue Management.

description: Serial number of the CPE (Customer Premises Equipment) device — such as a router, ONT (Optical Network Terminal), or set-top box — assigned or dispatched as part of this order line. Used for inventory tracking in Netcracker OSS/BSS and field installation management.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 Resource Inventory; Netcracker OSS/BSS Suite

description: The date and time when this order line record was first created in Oracle Communications Order and Service Management. Used for order aging analysis, SLA measurement baseline, and audit trail compliance.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 Order Management; ISO/IEC 27001

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code applicable to the monetary values on this order line (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency enterprise and wholesale order scenarios.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; TM Forum SID GB922

description: Indicates the order decomposition processing state for this line within Oracle Communications Order and Service Management. Order decomposition translates a commercial product order line into technical service and resource fulfillment tasks across OSS systems. Critical for orchestration monitoring and fault management.
reference: Oracle Communications Order and Service Management; TM Forum SID GB922 Order Decomposition; eTOM 1.4.3.1

description: Monetary discount applied to this order line at the time of order capture, reflecting promotional offers, loyalty discounts, or negotiated enterprise pricing. Used by Amdocs Revenue Management for billing and revenue assurance reconciliation.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 Billing; Amdocs Revenue Management

description: The early termination fee amount applicable if the customer cancels this order line before the end of the contract_term_months. Calculated at order capture based on the rate plan and contract term. Used by Amdocs Revenue Management for billing upon disconnection.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 Billing; Amdocs Revenue Management; FCC Consumer Protection Rules

description: Free-text description providing additional context for the order line fallout or failure, supplementing the fallout_reason_code. Captured by Oracle Communications Order and Service Management workflow engine for manual intervention and customer communication.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 Order Management; eTOM 1.4.3.4

description: Standardized code indicating the reason for order line fallout or failure during fulfillment orchestration (e.g., 'CREDIT_FAIL', 'INVENTORY_UNAVAILABLE', 'ADDRESS_NOT_SERVICEABLE', 'PORTING_REJECTED'). Used for fallout management, root cause analysis, and process improvement in Oracle Communications Order and Service Management.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 Order Management; eTOM 1.4.3.4

description: The unique SIM card identifier (ICCID) assigned to this order line for mobile service activations. Used for SIM provisioning in HLR/HSS/UDM systems and inventory management in Netcracker OSS/BSS. Classified as confidential as it identifies a specific subscriber SIM.

The International Mobile Subscriber Identity assigned to the SIM for this order line. Used for subscriber provisioning in HSS (Home Subscriber Server) and UDM (Unified Data Management) systems. Restricted PII as it uniquely identifies a mobile subscriber globally.

Indicates whether this order line involves an eSIM (Embedded SIM) provisioning rather than a physical SIM card. When TRUE, triggers eSIM profile download orchestration via GSMA SGP.22 RSP (Remote SIM Provisioning) instead of physical SIM dispatch.
reference: GSMA SGP.22 RSP Architecture; ETSI TS 103 383; 3GPP TS 22.022

business_glossary_term: Is Mobile Number Portability (MNP) Line Flag
description: Indicates whether this order line involves a Mobile Number Portability (MNP) port-in or port-out transaction. When TRUE, triggers MNP-specific workflow orchestration including donor operator notification and number portability clearinghouse coordination.
reference: GSMA PRD IR.26 MNP; 3GPP TS 23.116; FCC 47 CFR Part 52

description: The date and time when this order line record was last updated in Oracle Communications Order and Service Management. Used for change detection in ETL pipelines, audit trail, and concurrency control.

description: Sequential position of this line item within the parent order. Used for ordering, display, and referencing individual lines in multi-line orders (e.g., line 1 = mobile plan, line 2 = broadband, line 3 = MNP port-in). Satisfies TRANSACTION_LINE LINE_SEQUENCE category.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 Order Management; eTOM Process Framework

description: Current fulfillment lifecycle state of this individual order line. Tracks granular progress independently from the parent order header status, enabling partial fulfillment scenarios where some lines complete while others are held. Sourced from Oracle Communications Order and Service Management workflow engine.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 Order Management; eTOM Process Framework 1.4.3

description: Unique reference number assigned by the number portability clearinghouse or regulatory body for a PORT_IN or PORT_OUT order line. Used for inter-operator tracking, regulatory compliance reporting to FCC/Ofcom/BEREC, and dispute resolution.
reference: GSMA PRD IR.26 MNP; FCC 47 CFR Part 52; Ofcom General Conditions

description: Net commercial price for this order line after applying discounts (unit_price × quantity − discount_amount). Represents the agreed charge basis passed to Amdocs Revenue Management for billing. Distinct from tax-inclusive total.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 Billing; Amdocs Revenue Management

description: Processing priority assigned to this order line for fulfillment queue management. HIGH and CRITICAL priorities are used for enterprise SLA orders, regulatory-mandated activations, and emergency service restorations. Drives scheduling in Oracle Communications Order and Service Management.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 Order Management; eTOM 1.4.3

description: Human-readable name of the product or service being ordered on this line (e.g., '5G Unlimited Plan', 'FTTH 1Gbps Broadband', 'VoLTE Add-On'). Denormalized from the product catalog for operational reporting and customer-facing order confirmations.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 Product Catalog

description: High-level classification of the product or service being ordered on this line. Drives workflow routing, provisioning system selection, and reporting segmentation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: MOBILE|BROADBAND|FIXED_LINE|IPTV|VOIP|IOT|ENTERPRISE|ADD_ON — promote to reference product]
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 Product Catalog; Netcracker OSS/BSS Suite

description: Number of units of the product or service being ordered on this line. Typically 1 for most telecom service lines but may be greater for bulk SIM orders, enterprise seat licenses, or CPE equipment orders. Satisfies TRANSACTION_LINE LINE_QUANTITY category.

description: The customer-requested date on which the product or service on this line should become active. Drives service provisioning scheduling in Oracle Communications Order and Service Management and SLA commitment tracking.

description: Indicates whether fulfillment of this order line requires a field technician visit (e.g., FTTH installation, CPE installation, ONT splicing). When TRUE, triggers work order creation and field dispatch scheduling in Granite Telecommunications Workforce Management.

description: Billing and operational service type for this order line, distinguishing postpaid subscription, prepaid (Comverse ONE), hybrid, MVNO (Mobile Virtual Network Operator) resale, or wholesale service arrangements. Determines downstream billing system routing.

description: The native identifier of this order line record in the originating operational system (Oracle Communications Order and Service Management). Enables lineage tracing, reconciliation, and idempotent upsert processing in the Databricks Silver Layer.

description: Network access technology associated with the product on this order line (e.g., LTE, 5G NR, FTTH/GPON, DSL). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 2G|3G|4G_LTE|5G_NR|FTTH_GPON|FTTB|DSL|CABLE|FIXED_WIRELESS — promote to reference product]
reference: 3GPP TS 22.261; ITU-T G.984 GPON; ETSI EN 302 099

description: Reference to the parent fulfillment order for which this status history record was created. Links every history entry back to its originating order in Oracle Communications Order and Service Management.

description: Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: status_history.is_regulatory_reportable flags transitions that must be reported under a specific regulatory obligation (e.g., MNP porting status changes within mandated windows). Linking to the govern

Reference to a ServiceNow ITSM trouble ticket or incident raised in association with this state transition or amendment (e.g., a FALLOUT event that triggered an incident). Null when no ticket is associated. Supports cross-system traceability for fault management.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 Trouble Ticket Management; ServiceNow ITSM; eTOM Process Framework

description: Identifier of the specific workflow task or orchestration step within Oracle Communications Order and Service Management that generated this history record. Enables traceability from the history record back to the originating workflow execution for technical root-cause analysis.
reference: Oracle Communications Order and Service Management; TM Forum SID GB922 Order Management

description: Name of the OSS/BSS system that originated the transition or amendment event (e.g., Oracle Communications Order and Service Management, Netcracker OSS/BSS Suite, Salesforce CRM, ServiceNow ITSM). Enables source-system traceability for fallout root-cause analysis.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 Resource Management; Oracle Communications Order and Service Management; ServiceNow ITSM

description: Categorizes the type of actor that triggered the state transition or amendment. SYSTEM indicates an automated OSS/BSS process; AGENT indicates a human CSR or field technician; CUSTOMER indicates a self-service action; SUPERVISOR indicates a management override; AUTOMATED_RULE indicates a policy engine or workflow rule.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 Party Interaction; eTOM Process Framework; ISO/IEC 27001

description: For amendment records, the logical name of the order attribute that was changed (e.g., installation_address, appointment_date, selected_product_id, service_configuration_value). Provides context for interpreting original_value and new_value. Null for state transition records.

description: Date and time when the amendment was formally requested by the customer or agent. Distinct from transition_timestamp (which records when the event was processed). Used to measure amendment processing lead time and SLA compliance. Null for state transition records. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 Order Management; FCC Regulatory Reporting; BEREC Guidelines

description: Current processing status of the amendment request. PENDING: awaiting review; APPROVED: authorized but not yet applied to the order; REJECTED: denied by agent or system rule; APPLIED: successfully incorporated into the active order. Null for state transition records.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 Order Management; Oracle Communications Order and Service Management

description: For amendment records, classifies the type of post-submission change requested. Null for state transition records. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: APPOINTMENT_CHANGE|PRODUCT_SELECTION_CHANGE|ADDRESS_CHANGE|SERVICE_CONFIGURATION_CHANGE|CONTACT_CHANGE|PAYMENT_METHOD_CHANGE — promote to reference product]
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 Order Management; Oracle Communications Order and Service Management

description: For amendment records, the incremental cost impact (positive or negative, in the account currency) resulting from the amendment — e.g., a product selection change that increases or decreases the order value. Null for state transition records and zero-cost amendments. Used for revenue assurance and billing reconciliation.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 Order Management; Amdocs Revenue Management; ISO 4217

description: Date and time when this history record was first written to the Silver Layer lakehouse. Distinct from transition_timestamp (the business event time). Used for data pipeline monitoring, late-arrival detection, and audit trail completeness verification. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 Common Business Entities; ISO/IEC 27001

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code applicable to the cost_delta amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Null when cost_delta is null. Supports multi-currency operations across international markets.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; TM Forum SID GB922 Common Business Entities

description: Number of seconds the order spent in the previous_state before transitioning to new_state. Calculated at ingestion time as the difference between this record's transition_timestamp and the prior record's transition_timestamp for the same order. Used for SLA bottleneck analysis and process mining.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 SLA Management; eTOM Process Framework; FCC Regulatory Reporting

description: When new_state is FALLOUT, classifies the root-cause category of the fallout event (e.g., PROVISIONING_FAILURE, CREDIT_REJECTION, ADDRESS_VALIDATION_FAILURE, MNP_REJECTION, NETWORK_CAPACITY, SYSTEM_ERROR). Null for non-FALLOUT transitions. Essential for fallout root-cause analysis and NOC/OSS remediation workflows.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 Order Management; eTOM Process Framework; Oracle Communications Order and Service Management

description: Standardized code indicating how a FALLOUT condition was resolved (e.g., AUTO_RETRY_SUCCESS, MANUAL_INTERVENTION, ORDER_CANCELLED, REROUTED_TO_ALTERNATE_RESOURCE). Populated when transitioning out of FALLOUT state. Null for non-FALLOUT records.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 Order Management; eTOM Process Framework; Oracle Communications Order and Service Management

description: Indicates whether this history record is subject to regulatory reporting obligations (e.g., FCC order completion reporting, BEREC quality-of-service metrics, OFCOM service provisioning KPIs). True flags records that must be included in regulatory submissions.
reference: FCC Regulatory Reporting Requirements; BEREC Guidelines; Ofcom Reporting Requirements; NTIA

description: For MNP port-in or port-out order types, the inter-operator MNP reference number assigned by the number portability clearinghouse. Null for non-MNP orders. Required for GSMA MNP compliance reporting and inter-carrier dispute resolution.
reference: GSMA MNP Guidelines; 3GPP TS 22.011; FCC Number Portability Rules; BEREC MNP Guidelines

description: The order lifecycle state entered as a result of this transition. For amendment records, reflects the order state at the time the amendment was recorded. Valid states include SUBMITTED, VALIDATED, IN_PROGRESS, PENDING_PROVISIONING, COMPLETED, CANCELLED, FALLOUT. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: SUBMITTED|VALIDATED|IN_PROGRESS|PENDING_PROVISIONING|COMPLETED|CANCELLED|FALLOUT — promote to reference product]
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 Order Management; Oracle Communications Order and Service Management

description: For amendment records, the proposed or applied new value for the amended field (serialized as string for flexibility across amendment types). Null for state transition records. Paired with original_value to provide a complete before/after audit record.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 Order Management; FCC Regulatory Reporting; BEREC Guidelines

description: For amendment records, the value of the amended field before the change was applied (serialized as string for flexibility across amendment types). Null for state transition records. Supports dispute resolution and regulatory audit trails.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 Order Management; FCC Regulatory Reporting; BEREC Guidelines

description: Indicates whether a standard business rule, credit policy, or SLA policy was overridden to process this transition or amendment. When True, override_justification must be populated. Supports compliance monitoring and exception reporting.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 Order Management; ISO/IEC 27001; FCC Regulatory Reporting

description: Free-text justification provided by a supervisor or authorized agent when a standard business rule or SLA policy is overridden to approve an amendment or force a state transition. Required for compliance audit trails when override_flag is True.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 Order Management; ISO/IEC 27001; FCC Regulatory Reporting; BEREC Guidelines

description: The order lifecycle state immediately before this transition occurred. Null for the initial SUBMITTED state entry. Valid states include SUBMITTED, VALIDATED, IN_PROGRESS, PENDING_PROVISIONING, COMPLETED, CANCELLED, FALLOUT. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: SUBMITTED|VALIDATED|IN_PROGRESS|PENDING_PROVISIONING|COMPLETED|CANCELLED|FALLOUT — promote to reference product]
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 Order Management; Oracle Communications Order and Service Management

description: Standardized code identifying the business reason for the state transition or amendment (e.g., CREDIT_CHECK_FAILED, PROVISIONING_TIMEOUT, CUSTOMER_REQUESTED_CANCEL, MNP_PORT_DELAY, NETWORK_CAPACITY_UNAVAILABLE). Used for fallout root-cause analysis and SLA reporting.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 Order Management; eTOM Process Framework; Oracle Communications Order and Service Management

description: Free-text narrative providing additional context for the state transition or amendment reason, supplementing the structured reason_code. May include agent notes, system error messages, or customer-provided explanations captured at the time of the event.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 Order Management; Oracle Communications Order and Service Management

description: Discriminator indicating whether this history record captures a state transition (order lifecycle state change) or a post-submission amendment (customer or agent-initiated change to order attributes). Drives downstream processing logic and reporting segmentation.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 Order Management; eTOM Process Framework

description: Monotonically increasing integer representing the chronological order of history records for a given order_id. Sequence 1 is the initial SUBMITTED state entry. Enables ordered reconstruction of the complete order lifecycle and detection of out-of-sequence processing anomalies.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 Order Management; Oracle Communications Order and Service Management

description: Indicates whether this state transition or amendment triggered a reset of the order fulfillment SLA clock. True when the SLA measurement window is restarted (e.g., due to a customer-requested amendment or a FALLOUT recovery). Critical for accurate SLA compliance reporting to FCC and BEREC.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 SLA Management; FCC Regulatory Reporting; BEREC Guidelines; eTOM Process Framework

description: The committed SLA completion deadline applicable at the time of this history record. If sla_reset_flag is True, this reflects the recalculated SLA target post-reset. Enables point-in-time SLA compliance measurement and regulatory reporting. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 SLA Management; FCC Regulatory Reporting; BEREC Guidelines

description: The native primary key or event identifier of this record in the originating source system (e.g., the Oracle Communications Order and Service Management order history event ID). Enables reverse traceability from the Silver Layer back to the operational system for reconciliation and reprocessing.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 Common Business Entities; Oracle Communications Order and Service Management

description: Name of the operational system of record from which this history record was sourced (e.g., Oracle Communications Order and Service Management, Netcracker OSS/BSS Suite, Salesforce CRM). Supports data lineage tracking and multi-system reconciliation in the Silver Layer lakehouse.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 Common Business Entities; Oracle Communications Order and Service Management

description: The precise date and time (with timezone offset) at which the state transition or amendment event occurred. This is the principal business event timestamp used for SLA measurement, fallout root-cause analysis, and regulatory audit trails. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 Order Management; FCC Regulatory Reporting; BEREC Guidelines

description: Date and time when this history record was last modified in the Silver Layer (e.g., due to a late-arriving amendment status update or correction). For a fully immutable audit trail, updates should be rare and logged. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 Common Business Entities; ISO/IEC 27001

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each decomposed order task record generated by the Oracle Communications Order and Service Management (OSM) orchestration engine. Primary key for the order_task data product.
reference: Oracle Communications Order and Service Management (OSM); TM Forum SID GB922

Foreign key linking to workforce.depot. Business justification: Field-work tasks are dispatched from specific depots managing territory coverage, parts inventory, and technician availability. Critical for logistics planning, resource allocation, and technician dis

Foreign key linking to inventory.fiber_cable. Business justification: Fiber splicing, repair, and new-connection tasks reference the specific fiber cable segment being worked on. Field operations for fiber infrastructure require direct cable asset reference for dispatch

Reference to the parent commercial order header that contains the order line from which this task was decomposed. Enables direct navigation from task to top-level order for end-to-end fulfillment traceability.
reference: Oracle Communications Order and Service Management (OSM); TM Forum SID GB922

description: Reference to the parent commercial order line from which this task was decomposed. Establishes the traceability chain from commercial order line to atomic fulfillment task within the OSM orchestration engine.
