Foreign key linking to network.ran_cell. Business justification: Mobile provisioning requests (IMSI/MSISDN activation, SIM provisioning) target a specific RAN cell. Mobile OSS provisioning workflows require cell-level targeting for capacity validation and radio par

Foreign key linking to inventory.sim_stock. Business justification: Provisioning requests configure specific SIM cards in network elements (HLR/HSS/UDM). Real business need: network activation audit, provisioning failure resolution, SIM lifecycle state synchronization

Foreign key linking to enterprise.enterprise_site. Business justification: Provisioning requests for enterprise services are executed at specific physical sites. Direct link enables site-level provisioning status dashboards, installation scheduling, and infrastructure readin

Foreign key linking to network.slice. Business justification: 5G slice provisioning requests target a specific network slice for instantiation, modification, or termination. The provisioning system must reference the slice to send correct parameters to the NSSF

Foreign key linking to customer.subscriber. Business justification: Provisioning requests execute subscriber-level service changes (SIM activation, MSISDN porting, VoLTE enablement). Telecom provisioning systems are subscriber-centric; linking provisioning_request to

Reference to the service instance being provisioned, modified, or deactivated. Links to the service domain to identify the specific service (e.g., mobile line, broadband subscription, IPTV package) subject to this provisioning action.
reference: Netcracker OSS/BSS Suite — Service Catalog; TM Forum SID GB922 — Service

description: Foreign key linking to order.task. Business justification: A provisioning_request is dispatched by OSM as a result of a granular work task. The task orchestrates the provisioning action and the provisioning_request is the downstream instruction sent to OSS sy

Foreign key linking to network.transmission_link. Business justification: Fixed broadband and leased-line provisioning requests target a specific transmission link for activation or modification. Provisioning teams and OSS systems must know which transmission link is being

The date and time when the target OSS system acknowledged receipt of the provisioning instruction. Used to calculate dispatch-to-acknowledgement latency and detect timeout conditions against SLA thresholds.
reference: Oracle Communications Order and Service Management (OSM); TM Forum SID GB922 — Fulfillment

description: The Access Point Name (APN) configured for the mobile data service being provisioned. Determines the packet data network gateway and internet access policy for the subscriber. Applicable for mobile broadband and IoT service provisioning.
reference: 3GPP TS 23.003 — APN Definition; 3GPP TS 23.401 — EPS Bearer Management

description: Code identifying the bandwidth profile (upstream/downstream speed tier) to be applied to the service. Maps to a predefined bandwidth template in the OLT, DSLAM, or BNG configuration. Examples: 100M_FTTH, 1G_FTTH, 50M_ADSL.
reference: ITU-T G.984 — GPON; TM Forum SID GB922 — Service Quality; ETSI TS 101 270 — ADSL

description: The originating channel through which the customer order that triggered this provisioning request was placed. ONLINE: self-service web/app; STORE: retail point of sale; CALL_CENTER: agent-assisted; PARTNER: MVNO or wholesale partner; FIELD_TECH: field technician dispatch; AUTOMATED: system-triggered (e.g., auto-renewal, policy enforcement).
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 — Channel; Salesforce CRM — Lead Management

description: The date and time when the target OSS system reported final completion (success or failure) of the provisioning action. Used to measure end-to-end provisioning cycle time and SLA compliance.
reference: Oracle Communications Order and Service Management (OSM); TM Forum SID GB922 — Fulfillment Order

description: The date and time when this provisioning request record was first created in the OSM system. Serves as the audit creation timestamp for data lineage and compliance purposes. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category.
reference: ISO/IEC 27001 — Information Security Management; TM Forum SID GB922 — Entity Lifecycle

description: Data governance classification level for this provisioning request record, indicating sensitivity and access control requirements. Aligned with the enterprise data classification policy: restricted (highest sensitivity), confidential, internal, public.
reference: ISO/IEC 27001 — Information Security Management; GDPR Article 5 — Data Minimisation; FCC CPNI Rules 47 CFR Part 64

description: The date and time (ISO 8601 with timezone offset) when the provisioning instruction was dispatched from Oracle Communications OSM to the target OSS system. Represents the principal real-world business event time. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP category.
reference: Oracle Communications Order and Service Management (OSM); TM Forum SID GB922 — Fulfillment Order

description: Standardized error or failure reason code returned by the target OSS system when provisioning_outcome is FAILURE or TIMEOUT. Used for fault management, root cause analysis, and automated retry logic. Examples: RESOURCE_UNAVAILABLE, INVALID_PARAMETER, SYSTEM_UNREACHABLE, DUPLICATE_REQUEST.
reference: Nokia NetAct — Fault Management; Ericsson Network Manager — Fault Management; TM Forum SID GB922 — Trouble Ticket

description: Human-readable description of the failure reason as returned by the target OSS system or populated by the OSM orchestration layer. Provides operational context for NOC engineers and support teams investigating provisioning failures.
reference: Nokia NetAct — Fault Management; ServiceNow ITSM — Incident Management

description: The Integrated Circuit Card Identifier (ICCID) — the unique serial number of the SIM or eSIM card — associated with this provisioning request. Used to link the provisioning action to the physical or embedded SIM being activated.
reference: ITU-T E.118 — ICCID; GSMA SGP.02 — eSIM Specification; 3GPP TS 22.022

description: The International Mobile Subscriber Identity (IMSI) — a unique 15-digit identifier stored on the SIM/eSIM — being provisioned in the HLR/HSS/UDM. Applicable for mobile service activations and MNP port-in operations. Classified as restricted PII as it uniquely identifies a subscriber.
reference: 3GPP TS 23.003 — Numbering, Addressing and Identification; GSMA SGP.02 — eSIM; GDPR Article 4

description: Indicates whether a rollback of previously applied provisioning changes is required due to a downstream failure in a multi-step orchestration workflow. When TRUE, the OSM compensation workflow is triggered to reverse partial provisioning actions.
reference: Oracle Communications Order and Service Management (OSM) — Order Orchestration; TM Forum SID GB922 — Fulfillment

description: The date and time of the most recent retry attempt for this provisioning request. Used in conjunction with retry_count to calculate retry intervals and enforce exponential backoff policies.
reference: Oracle Communications Order and Service Management (OSM); TM Forum SID GB922 — Fulfillment

description: The maximum number of retry attempts configured for this provisioning request before it is escalated or marked as permanently failed. Derived from the service type SLA policy at the time of request creation.
reference: Oracle Communications Order and Service Management (OSM); TM Forum SID GB922 — SLA Management

business_glossary_term: Mobile Number Portability (MNP) Donor Operator Code
description: For Mobile Number Portability (MNP) PORT_IN provisioning actions, the network operator code of the donor (losing) operator from whom the number is being ported. Populated per GSMA PRD IR.26 and national MNP regulatory requirements.
reference: GSMA PRD IR.26 — MNP; 3GPP TS 22.042 — MNP; FCC 47 CFR Part 52 — Number Portability; Ofcom — General Conditions

description: The scheduled or actual date on which the Mobile Number Portability (MNP) port-in or port-out is to be executed. Applicable only when provisioning_action is PORT_IN or PORT_OUT. Governed by national MNP regulatory timelines.
reference: GSMA PRD IR.26 — MNP; FCC 47 CFR Part 52 — Number Portability; Ofcom — General Conditions of Entitlement

description: Code identifying the geographic network region or Point of Presence (POP) within which the provisioning action is being executed. Used for regional capacity planning, NOC routing, and geographic SLA analysis.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 — Resource; ITU-T M.3010 — TMN Principles; Nokia NetAct — Configuration Management

description: Serial number of the Optical Network Terminal (ONT) / Customer Premises Equipment (CPE) device being provisioned on the GPON OLT for FTTH fiber services. Used to bind the physical device to the subscriber's service profile in the OLT configuration.
reference: ITU-T G.984 — GPON; ITU-T G.988 — ONU Management; TM Forum SID GB922 — Resource Inventory

description: Business priority assigned to this provisioning request, determining queue position and resource allocation in the OSM orchestration engine. CRITICAL: emergency/regulatory-mandated; HIGH: premium customer or SLA-sensitive; MEDIUM: standard commercial; LOW: batch/bulk provisioning.
reference: Oracle Communications Order and Service Management (OSM); TM Forum SID GB922 — Order Priority

description: The type of provisioning operation to be performed on the target OSS system. ACTIVATE: new service activation; MODIFY: change of service parameters; DEACTIVATE: service disconnection; SUSPEND: temporary service suspension; RESUME: reinstatement after suspension; PORT_IN: Mobile Number Portability (MNP) inbound port; PORT_OUT: MNP outbound port. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ACTIVATE|MODIFY|DEACTIVATE|SUSPEND|RESUME|PORT_IN|PORT_OUT — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 — Fulfillment; 3GPP TS 22.042 — MNP; GSMA PRD IR.26 — MNP

description: Final result of the provisioning execution as reported by the target OSS system. SUCCESS: service fully activated/modified/deactivated as requested; FAILURE: provisioning could not be completed; TIMEOUT: target system did not respond within the configured timeout window; PARTIAL: some but not all service components were provisioned (e.g., multi-component service). Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER QUANTITATIVE_RESULT category for non-monetary transactions.
reference: Oracle Communications Order and Service Management (OSM); TM Forum SID GB922 — Fulfillment Order

description: Externally-known, human-readable business identifier for the provisioning request as generated by Oracle Communications OSM (e.g., PROV-2024-0001234). Used for cross-system traceability across OSS/BSS platforms. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER category.
reference: Oracle Communications Order and Service Management (OSM); TM Forum SID GB922 — Fulfillment Order

description: Current lifecycle state of the provisioning request. PENDING: awaiting dispatch; DISPATCHED: sent to target OSS system; IN_PROGRESS: being processed by target system; SUCCESS: provisioning completed successfully; FAILURE: provisioning failed; TIMEOUT: no acknowledgement received within SLA window; CANCELLED: request withdrawn before execution. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER LIFECYCLE_STATUS category. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: PENDING|DISPATCHED|IN_PROGRESS|SUCCESS|FAILURE|TIMEOUT|CANCELLED — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: Oracle Communications Order and Service Management (OSM); TM Forum SID GB922 — Fulfillment Order State Machine

description: Number of times this provisioning request has been retried after an initial failure or timeout. Used to enforce maximum retry policies, trigger escalation workflows, and analyze systemic provisioning issues.
reference: Oracle Communications Order and Service Management (OSM); TM Forum SID GB922 — Fulfillment

description: The date and time when the rollback compensation action was executed for this provisioning request. Populated only when is_rollback_required is TRUE and the rollback has been completed.
reference: Oracle Communications Order and Service Management (OSM) — Order Orchestration; TM Forum SID GB922 — Fulfillment

description: The date on which the service activation or modification is scheduled to take effect, as agreed with the customer or mandated by the order. May differ from dispatch_timestamp for future-dated provisioning requests.
reference: Oracle Communications Order and Service Management (OSM); TM Forum SID GB922 — Fulfillment Order

description: Serialized (JSON or XML) payload containing the service-specific configuration parameters dispatched to the target OSS system. For mobile services: MSISDN, IMSI, APN, QoS profile; for FTTH/GPON: ONT serial number, VLAN ID, bandwidth profile; for IMS: SIP URI, codec list. Stored as STRING to accommodate heterogeneous service types.
reference: 3GPP TS 23.401 — EPS Architecture; ITU-T G.984 — GPON; ETSI TS 124 229 — IMS SIP

description: Classification of the telecommunications service being provisioned. Determines the provisioning workflow, target OSS system, and service parameters payload structure. Examples: MOBILE (LTE/5G NR), BROADBAND (DSL/cable), FIBER_FTTH (Gigabit Passive Optical Network), VoIP, IPTV, FIXED_LINE, SD-WAN, MPLS. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: MOBILE|BROADBAND|FIBER_FTTH|VOIP|IPTV|FIXED_LINE|SD_WAN|MPLS — promote to reference product]
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 — Service Specification; ITU-T Y.2001 — NGN Framework

description: Indicates whether this provisioning request breached its SLA target duration. Set to TRUE when the actual provisioning duration exceeded sla_target_duration_seconds. Used for SLA reporting, penalty calculations, and operational performance management.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 — SLA Management; FCC Broadband Performance Standards; Ofcom — Quality of Service Regulations

business_glossary_term: Service Level Agreement (SLA) Target Provisioning Duration (Seconds)
description: The contracted Service Level Agreement (SLA) target duration in seconds within which this provisioning request must be completed end-to-end. Used for SLA compliance monitoring and breach detection in NOC dashboards.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 — SLA Management; FCC Broadband Performance Standards; Ofcom — Quality of Service Regulations

description: Code identifying the upstream BSS/OSS system that originated this provisioning request. OSM: Oracle Communications Order and Service Management; NETCRACKER: Netcracker OSS/BSS Suite; AMDOCS: Amdocs Revenue Management; COMVERSE: Comverse ONE prepaid charging; MANUAL: manually created by NOC operator.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 — Entity Lifecycle; Oracle Communications Order and Service Management (OSM)

description: Code identifying the downstream OSS system to which this provisioning instruction is dispatched. Valid values include: HLR (Home Location Register), HSS (Home Subscriber Server), UDM (Unified Data Management), IMS (IP Multimedia Subsystem), GPON_OLT (Gigabit Passive Optical Network Optical Line Terminal), IPAM (IP Address Management), DSLAM (Digital Subscriber Line Access Multiplexer), RAN (Radio Access Network), PCRF (Policy and Charging Rules Function), PCF (Policy Control Function). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: HLR|HSS|UDM|IMS|GPON_OLT|IPAM|DSLAM|RAN|PCRF|PCF — promote to reference product]
reference: 3GPP TS 23.002 — Network Architecture; ETSI TS 123 002; TM Forum SID GB922 — Resource

description: Identifier of the specific system instance or node within the target OSS system (e.g., HSS cluster node ID, OLT shelf/port identifier) to which the provisioning command is routed. Supports multi-node environments and geo-redundant deployments.
reference: Ericsson Network Manager — Core Network Management; Nokia NetAct — Configuration Management

description: The date and time when this provisioning request record was last modified. Tracks state transitions, retry events, and status updates for audit trail and data lineage. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED category.
reference: ISO/IEC 27001 — Information Security Management; TM Forum SID GB922 — Entity Lifecycle

description: The Virtual LAN (VLAN) ID assigned to the subscriber's service on the GPON OLT or DSLAM for traffic segregation and QoS enforcement. Applicable for FTTH and broadband service provisioning.
reference: IEEE 802.1Q — VLAN Tagging; ITU-T G.984 — GPON; TM Forum SID GB922 — Resource

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the service qualification record. Primary key for the service_qualification data product in the order domain Silver layer.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 — Order Management

description: Reference to the customer premises or installation address record against which the network eligibility check is performed. Resolves to the address master in Netcracker Resource Inventory or the geographic address repository.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 — Geographic Address; TMF673 Geographic Address Management API

description: Foreign key linking to enterprise.corporate_account. Business justification: Enterprise service qualification requests require corporate account linkage for credit validation, contract eligibility checks, account-level service portfolio management, and pre-sales feasibility an

Reference to the customer (prospect or existing subscriber) on whose behalf the service qualification is being performed. Links to the customer master in Salesforce CRM / Netcracker BSS. Serves as the PARTY_REFERENCE for this transaction.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 — Party Management; Salesforce CRM Customer 360

description: Foreign key linking to inventory.fiber_cable. Business justification: Fiber service qualification checks whether fiber cable infrastructure reaches the customer address. The qualification process references the specific cable segment for availability and capacity assess

Reference to the parent order or pre-order request that triggered this service qualification check in Oracle Communications Order and Service Management. Null if the qualification was initiated as a standalone eligibility check outside of a formal order.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 — Order Management; Oracle OSM

description: Foreign key linking to enterprise.managed_service. Business justification: Re-qualification or upgrade of an existing managed service requires linking the qualification record to the managed service being assessed. Enables managed service upgrade eligibility reporting and te

Foreign key linking to partner.mvno_profile. Business justification: MVNO service qualifications must check MVNO-specific constraints: subscriber capacity limits, IMSI/MSISDN range availability, spectrum access rights, and technical capabilities - required for MVNO sub

Foreign key linking to inventory.olt_asset. Business justification: Fiber service qualification checks OLT port capacity and operational status before committing to a fiber service offer. Direct reference to the serving OLT asset is required for capacity management, q

Reference to the specific product offering in the Netcracker Product Catalog for which eligibility is being assessed. Drives which product offers are surfaced to the sales agent or digital channel upon a positive qualification outcome.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 — Product Catalog; TMF620 Product Catalog Management API

description: Foreign key linking to network.ran_cell. Business justification: Mobile service qualification identifies serving cell, measures signal strength, and validates capacity availability. Critical pre-sale process for determining mobile service eligibility and setting cu

Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: service_qualification.broadband_subsidy_eligible and regulatory_zone_code indicate qualifications governed by regulatory obligations (universal service, broadband subsidy programs). Linking to the gov

Foreign key linking to enterprise.sdwan_topology. Business justification: Network infrastructure readiness qualification for SD-WAN topology deployment (bandwidth availability, latency assessment, underlay transport options) must reference the SD-WAN topology. Enables topol

Foreign key linking to enterprise.enterprise_site. Business justification: Service qualification for enterprise customers is performed against a specific physical site. Site-level qualification drives field survey scheduling, infrastructure readiness assessment, and installa

Foreign key linking to network.slice. Business justification: Service qualification for 5G slice services checks whether a specific slice type and configuration is available in the coverage area. Pre-sales qualification for enterprise slice offerings must refere

Foreign key linking to workforce.technician. Business justification: When field_survey_required=true on a service qualification, a technician is dispatched for a pre-sales site survey. Telecom pre-sales operations track which technician conducted the survey to link sur

Foreign key linking to network.transmission_link. Business justification: Fixed broadband and leased-line service qualification checks capacity and availability on a specific transmission link. Pre-sales and order entry teams query transmission link status and utilization t

Primary street address line of the customer premises being qualified for service. Captured at qualification time as a point-in-time snapshot to avoid dependency on address master changes post-qualification.
reference: GDPR Article 4; TM Forum TMF673 Geographic Address Management API

description: Secondary address line (apartment, suite, floor, unit number) of the customer premises being qualified. Nullable for single-dwelling units.
reference: GDPR Article 4; TM Forum TMF673 Geographic Address Management API

description: Pipe-delimited list of all access technologies technically available at the qualified address (e.g., 'FTTH|LTE|5G_NR'). Enables the sales system to present all eligible product offers, not just the primary requested technology. Sourced from Netcracker Resource Inventory network coverage data.
reference: TM Forum TMF645 Service Qualification API v4; Netcracker OSS/BSS Suite

description: Indicates whether the service address qualifies for government broadband subsidy programs (e.g., FCC Affordable Connectivity Program (ACP), NTIA BEAD, UK Gigabit Voucher Scheme). Determined based on regulatory_zone_code and address-level coverage data. Relevant for compliance reporting to FCC, NTIA, and Ofcom.
reference: FCC Affordable Connectivity Program (ACP); NTIA BEAD Program; Ofcom Gigabit Voucher Scheme; BEREC

description: City or municipality of the service installation address. Used for geographic coverage reporting and network rollout planning analytics.
reference: TM Forum TMF673 Geographic Address Management API; ITU-T E.164

description: Free-text description of any technical or operational constraints identified during the qualification check when the outcome is ELIGIBLE_WITH_CONSTRAINTS. Examples: 'Maximum speed limited to 100 Mbps due to copper loop length', 'CPE upgrade required', 'Shared GPON port — peak-hour congestion possible'. Null when outcome is ELIGIBLE or NOT_ELIGIBLE.
reference: TM Forum TMF645 Service Qualification API v4; TM Forum SID GB922

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code of the service installation address (e.g., USA, GBR, DEU). Required for multi-country operators and regulatory jurisdiction determination.

Indicates whether a Customer Premises Equipment (CPE) installation or upgrade is required to deliver the requested service at the qualified address. True: CPE installation/upgrade is mandatory; False: existing CPE is compatible or no CPE is needed.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 — Resource Inventory; Oracle OSM Service Fulfillment

description: Type of CPE required for service delivery at the qualified address. ONT (Optical Network Terminal) for FTTH/GPON; ROUTER for broadband; MODEM for DSL; GATEWAY for combined modem/router; OUTDOOR_UNIT/INDOOR_UNIT for fixed wireless. Null when cpe_required is False.
reference: ITU-T G.984.2 (ONT/GPON); TM Forum SID GB922 — Resource Inventory

description: Audit timestamp recording when this service qualification record was first persisted in the Silver layer data product. Populated by the ingestion pipeline from Oracle OSM source system.

Estimated number of calendar days from order confirmation to service activation at the qualified address. Derived from field workforce availability (Granite WFM), infrastructure readiness, and network node capacity. Displayed to customers during the ordering flow to set delivery expectations.
reference: TM Forum eTOM — Service Fulfillment; Oracle OSM Order Orchestration; Granite WFM

description: Estimated round-trip network latency in milliseconds at the qualified address for the requested service type. Relevant for QoS-sensitive product offers (VoLTE, gaming, enterprise SD-WAN) and SLA eligibility determination.
reference: ITU-T Y.1541 (QoS); 3GPP TS 22.261; TM Forum SLA Management

description: Indicates whether a physical field survey by a Telecommunication technician is required before service can be provisioned at this address. True when infrastructure readiness is PARTIAL or NOT_READY, or when the qualification engine cannot determine feasibility remotely. Triggers a field dispatch work order in Granite WFM.
reference: TM Forum eTOM — Service Fulfillment; Granite Telecommunications WFM

description: Standardized reason code explaining why the address is NOT_ELIGIBLE for the requested service. NO_COVERAGE: outside network footprint; CAPACITY_EXHAUSTED: node at full capacity; INFRASTRUCTURE_NOT_BUILT: planned but not yet deployed; ADDRESS_NOT_FOUND: address could not be geocoded or matched; TECHNICAL_BARRIER: physical impediment; REGULATORY_RESTRICTION: regulatory constraint. Null when outcome is ELIGIBLE or ELIGIBLE_WITH_CONSTRAINTS.
reference: TM Forum TMF645 Service Qualification API v4; FCC Broadband Data Collection

description: Indicates the physical network infrastructure readiness at or near the service address. READY: full infrastructure in place; PARTIAL: partial build-out, service possible with constraints; NOT_READY: no infrastructure present; UNDER_CONSTRUCTION: active network build in progress with a planned completion date.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 — Resource Inventory; Netcracker OSS/BSS Suite

description: Audit timestamp recording the most recent update to this service qualification record in the Silver layer. Updated on any status change, outcome determination, or data correction.

Geographic latitude coordinate (WGS84 decimal degrees) of the service installation point. Used for precise network coverage mapping, field dispatch routing via Granite WFM, and FCC broadband location fabric matching.
reference: FCC Broadband Data Collection — Location Fabric; WGS84; NTIA BroadbandUSA

description: Geographic longitude coordinate (WGS84 decimal degrees) of the service installation point. Used in conjunction with latitude for geospatial coverage analysis and network node proximity calculations.
reference: FCC Broadband Data Collection — Location Fabric; WGS84; NTIA BroadbandUSA

description: Maximum technically achievable downstream bandwidth in Megabits per second (Mbps) at the qualified address, as determined by the network qualification engine based on distance to node, line quality, and technology capability. Drives product tier eligibility in the sales flow.
reference: FCC Broadband Data Collection; ITU-T G.9700 (G.fast); 3GPP TS 38.306 (5G NR); ETSI TS 101 388 (ADSL)

description: Maximum technically achievable upstream bandwidth in Megabits per second (Mbps) at the qualified address. Critical for symmetric service offerings (FTTH/GPON) and business-grade SLA commitments.
reference: FCC Broadband Data Collection; ITU-T G.984 (GPON); 3GPP TS 38.306 (5G NR)

description: Indicates whether Mobile Number Portability (MNP) is applicable to this service qualification, relevant for mobile service types (LTE, 5G NR) where the customer intends to port an existing number. True triggers MNP eligibility checks in the downstream order flow.
reference: GSMA PRD IR.26 — Mobile Number Portability; FCC MNP Rules 47 CFR Part 52; Ofcom MNP Guidelines

description: Free-text notes or remarks added by the sales agent, network engineer, or automated qualification engine to provide additional context about the qualification result. May include special instructions for the field team, customer-specific considerations, or escalation notes.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922; Oracle OSM

description: Postal or ZIP code of the service installation address. Key dimension for network coverage gap analysis, FCC broadband availability reporting, and NTIA compliance.
reference: FCC Broadband Data Collection (BDC); NTIA BroadbandUSA; ISO 3166-1

description: The sales or service channel through which the qualification request was initiated. ONLINE: digital self-service portal; RETAIL_STORE: in-store agent; CALL_CENTER: inbound/outbound agent via Salesforce CRM; PARTNER_API: wholesale or MVNO partner API; FIELD_AGENT: mobile field sales; SELF_SERVICE: automated IVR or chatbot.
reference: TM Forum eTOM — Customer Relationship Management; Salesforce CRM

description: The date and time when the qualification check was fully resolved and an outcome was determined. Null if the qualification is still IN_PROGRESS or was CANCELLED before completion.
reference: TM Forum TMF645 Service Qualification API v4; ISO 8601

description: The date after which the qualification result is considered stale and must be re-evaluated before an order can be placed. Network conditions, capacity, and infrastructure status can change; this date enforces re-qualification for orders placed after a defined validity window (typically 30–90 days).
reference: TM Forum TMF645 Service Qualification API v4; Oracle OSM Order Orchestration

description: Business result of the pre-order service qualification check. ELIGIBLE: the address/location can receive the requested service without restriction; NOT_ELIGIBLE: service cannot be delivered to this location; ELIGIBLE_WITH_CONSTRAINTS: service is technically feasible but subject to limitations (e.g., reduced bandwidth, extended lead time, CPE upgrade required).
reference: TM Forum TMF645 Service Qualification API v4; ETSI TS 103 221

description: Externally visible, human-readable reference number assigned to the qualification request by Oracle Communications Order and Service Management (OSM). Used by sales agents and customers to track the qualification check.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 — TMF645 Service Qualification API

description: The exact date and time (ISO 8601 with timezone offset) when the qualification request was submitted by the sales or ordering system. This is the principal business event timestamp for this transaction.
reference: TM Forum TMF645 Service Qualification API v4; ISO 8601

description: The originating operational system that submitted the qualification request. OSM: Oracle Communications Order and Service Management; SALESFORCE: Salesforce CRM; NETCRACKER: Netcracker OSS/BSS Suite; PARTNER_PORTAL: wholesale/MVNO partner portal; SELF_SERVICE_PORTAL: customer-facing digital portal. Used for data lineage and system-of-record traceability.
reference: TM Forum SID GB922 — Data Lineage; Oracle OSM; Netcracker OSS/BSS Suite

description: Current lifecycle state of the qualification request workflow within Oracle OSM. IN_PROGRESS: check is being executed; COMPLETED: outcome determined; FAILED: system error prevented completion; CANCELLED: request withdrawn before completion.
reference: TM Forum TMF645 Service Qualification API v4; TM Forum SID GB922

description: Regulatory or geographic zone code assigned to the service address, used to determine applicable regulatory obligations (e.g., FCC Universal Service Fund (USF) eligible area, NTIA BEAD-eligible zone, Ofcom Universal Service Obligation (USO) area, BEREC regulated zone). Drives compliance reporting and subsidy eligibility.
reference: FCC Universal Service Fund (USF); NTIA BEAD Program; Ofcom USO; BEREC; ITU-T D.50

description: Indicates whether this qualification result has been flagged as requiring re-execution before an order can proceed. Set to True when the qualification_expiry_date has passed, when network topology changes have occurred in the serving area, or when the customer requests a different service type.
reference: TM Forum TMF645 Service Qualification API v4; Oracle OSM

description: The access technology or service type for which eligibility is being assessed. Values align with the network technologies offered by Telecommunication: FTTH (Fiber to the Home), GPON (Gigabit Passive Optical Network), LTE (Long-Term Evolution), 5G NR (5G New Radio), ADSL, VDSL, FTTB (Fiber to the Building), FTTC (Fiber to the Cabinet), FIXED_WIRELESS. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FTTH|GPON|LTE|5G_NR|ADSL|VDSL|FTTB|FTTC|FIXED_WIRELESS — promote to reference product]
reference: 3GPP TS 22.261; ITU-T G.984 (GPON); ETSI TS 136 300 (LTE); 3GPP TS 38.300 (5G NR); ITU-T G.992 (ADSL/VDSL)

description: Measured or predicted radio signal strength in dBm at the qualified address for wireless service types (LTE, 5G NR, Fixed Wireless). Null for wireline technologies. Sourced from Ericsson Network Manager RAN coverage data or Nokia NetAct RF planning tools.
reference: 3GPP TS 36.214 (LTE RSRP); 3GPP TS 38.215 (5G NR RSRP); ITU-R M.2135

description: Indicates whether this price plan can be included in product bundles or multi-service packages (true) or must be purchased standalone (false).

Business rules defining which customers or accounts are eligible for this price plan. May include customer segment, credit score requirements, geographic restrictions, existing service requirements, or promotional qualifications.
Geographic regions, markets, or service areas where this price plan is available. May be defined as country codes, state/province codes, zip/postal codes, or market identifiers.
The quantity of units included in the base price before overage charges apply. For example, 50GB of data included in a mobile plan. Null if plan does not include a bundled quantity.

description: Identifier of the user or system that last modified this price plan record. Supports change tracking and accountability.

description: The pricing structure model applied to this plan. Flat rate for fixed charges, tiered for step-based pricing, volume-based for quantity discounts, usage-based for consumption charges, event-based for transaction charges, or hybrid for combinations.

description: The date when promotional pricing or terms expire and standard pricing takes effect. Null for non-promotional plans or promotions without a defined end date.

Policy for prorating charges when service starts or ends mid-billing cycle. Full period charges the entire amount, daily proration calculates based on days used, no proration means no refund or partial charge, custom follows specific business rules.

[ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: mobile_voice|mobile_data|broadband|ftth|iptv|voip|sd_wan|mpls|5g_nr|lte|ott|device — promote to reference product]

description: Tax classification category for this price plan determining applicable tax rates and treatment. Telecom-specific category applies to services subject to special telecommunications taxes or regulatory fees.

JSON or structured text defining the tier breakpoints and rates for tiered pricing models. Specifies quantity ranges and corresponding unit prices for each tier. Null for non-tiered plans.

description: Version identifier for this price plan definition. Incremented when pricing terms, rates, or structure are modified. Supports price plan change tracking and historical analysis.

Flat amount per unit: fixed dollar per activation. Percentage of ARPU (Average Revenue Per User): commission based on customer lifetime value proxy.

Number of days after the end of the commission period before payment is issued. Allows time for transaction validation and clawback assessment.

Current lifecycle state of the commission plan. Draft: under design. Active: in effect and earning commissions. Suspended: temporarily paused. Expired: past effective end date. Terminated: permanently closed.

Classification of the commission structure. Flat rate: fixed amount per sale. Tiered: rate increases with volume. Residual: ongoing percentage of recurring revenue. SPIFF (Sales Performance Incentive Fund): short-term bonus for specific products. MDF (Market Development Fund): channel partner co-marketing incentive. Accelerator: multiplier for exceeding quota.

Frequency or duration type of the target measurement period. Monthly targets reset each month; quarterly targets align with fiscal quarters; annual targets span the fiscal year; custom periods have user-defined start and end dates.

The total cost paid by the operator to acquire this spectrum license, including auction bids, transfer fees, and transaction costs. Expressed in the currency specified by the acquisition cost currency code.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the acquisition cost.
The date on which the operator acquired ownership or rights to this spectrum license. May differ from issue date if acquired through secondary market.

The annual regulatory fee charged by the granting authority for holding this spectrum license. Expressed in the currency specified by the fee currency code.
The total bandwidth allocated to this license in megahertz. Calculated as the difference between upper and lower frequency bounds.

The date on which the license becomes operationally valid and the licensee may begin using the spectrum. May differ from issue date due to administrative or technical conditions.

Indicates whether any regulatory enforcement action (warning, fine, suspension) has been taken against this license. True if enforcement action exists, False otherwise.
